observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Fast-track the assessment, processing, review and filing of reimbursable claims to PhilHealth;



Identify lapses on procedures and processes, and set-up efficient and effective control mechanism to minimize, if not eliminate, unresolved issues on claims; and 



Ensure the timely submission and completeness of documents supporting the claims for reimbursement including the immediate refiling of RTH claims as indicated in the notification letters so as not to invalidate the same.
observations.managementAction:
As of 
December 31, 2024, out of 2,984, In Process (IP) claims, 97 were submitted within 
7 days while 2,887 claims were submitted within 60 days. Total paid claims increased to 2,856 or totaling P37.554 million compared to 1,816 claims paid totaling P24.372 million as reported on December 31, 2023.

As of December 31, 2024, there were zero Not Yet Transmitted (NYT) claims for 
CYs 2020-2023.

RTH claims for 
CY 2024 decreased to 377 compared to RTH claims of 459 in 
CY 2023.

As of December 31, 2024, 2,984 In Process (IP) claims were submitted within the reglementary  period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:

SJMC to submit regularly to CAD the schedule of reimbursable claims, on whatever status, for recording in the books of accounts and necessary adjustments if any;



CAD to record receivables and income under accrual basis of accounting; and



CAD and SJMC evaluate the collectability of the reimbursable claims from PhilHealth and set-up provisions for impairment
observations.managementAction:
Receivables totaling P6,219,338.29 and P20,090,024.41 were recorded under JEV Nos. 100-2024-12-0368 dated 
December 27, 2024 and 100-2024-12-0373 dated 
December 31, 2024, respectively.

Adjusted to correct the receivables and income account balances under JEV Nos. 
100-2024-04-0092 and 100-2024-04-0093 both dated April 30, 2024 amounting to P446,506.00 and P906,968.40, respectively.

Allowance of Impairment was set-up for the reimbursable claims from PhilHealth under JEV Nos. 
100-2024-05-00117 and 100-2024-05-00118 both dated 
May 13, 2024, amounting to P17,840,317.00 and P6,219,338.29, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a catch-up plan for the timely implementation of the 32 unimplemented LDRRM PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
The CDRRMO submitted a Catch-up Plan for the 32 unimplemented LDRRM PPAs last May 2024. Ten projects were reprogrammed, and 17 projects were revised. Four projects were implemented and paid while unspent fund for CY 2019  was reverted to the General Fund under JEV No. 300-2024-12-043 dated 
December 27, 2024 amounting to P1,570,397.86.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to improve significantly the planning, budgeting, execution/utilization of the LCCAP fund to ensure the achievement of the City’s mandate on climate change.
observations.managementAction:
Five unimplemented projects totaling P4.3M were reprogrammed and three projects totaling P1.9M were revised. Procurement of Truck Manlifter/ Boom for disaster preparedness procured in 
CY 2023 was delivered and paid in CY 2024 in the amount of 
P7.950 million. Further, the project for the installation and delivery of radio repeater set/ communication equipment totaling P2.888 million was already completed on 02/28/2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management closely monitor the efficient and timely implementation of the planned LCPC PPAs to ensure the full achievement of its mandate of promoting the best interest and rights of children of the City.
observations.managementAction:
The amount of unimplemented PPAs totaling P5.636 million was reverted to the GF. As of 
August 16, 2024, the amount of P5,147,975.80 was obligated for the Supplemental Feeding Program.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Management for being fully compliant with Item 3.2 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated 
November 4, 2020, in its identification, prioritization and implementation of the PPAs under the 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: PPAs under the 20% DF were implemented and completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the SJMC PhilHealth Division to conduct thorough review and evaluation on all reimbursement claims to ensure that the same are duly supported with complete and proper documentation,
observations.managementAction: All forms and supporting documentation pertaining to the patients' benefit claims have been carefully examined and validated before being transmitted to PHIC. | .

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters and ensure that the same is received by the concerned persons and seek legal remedies so that the past due receivables will be settled the soonest possible time. For the receivables from deceased individuals, we recommend that the LCE seek legal remedies for the institution of claim against their estates, hence, would have additional funds to finance projects for the benefit the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters and ensure that the same is received by the concerned persons and seek legal remedies so that the past due receivables will be settled the soonest possible time. For the receivables from deceased individuals, we recommend that the LCE seek legal remedies for the institution of claim against their estates, hence, would have additional funds to finance projects for the benefit the constituents.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the LDRRMO-designate, MAO, MSWDO, and MHO to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant the duly accomplished/certified distribution list of the issued/distributed welfare goods and food supplies amounting to P3,737,987.29, farm implements and equipment aggregating P227,179,958.00, construction materials amounting to P7,047,235.00, and drugs and medicines amounting to  P3,459,455.00  to the end-users respectively,  and as his basis in recording the items under the expense account.
Also, require the accountant to recognize the issuance of inventories aggregating P5,929,687.00, thus the affected accounts will be correctly stated in the FS.
Henceforth, the Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), supported  with a duly accomplished Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for all issued/distributed inventory including the issuance of office supplies, accountable forms, plates and stickers, food supplies and other supplies aggregating P4,384,414.22, and to regularly prepare and furnish these forms to the Municipal Accountant together with the duly accomplished and certified distribution list of all goods distributed to the end-users as his basis in the preparation of journal entry to record the expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts, hence to present fairly the affected accounts in the FS.
observations.managementAction:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the LDRRMO-designate, MAO, MSWDO, and MHO to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant the duly accomplished/certified distribution list of the issued/distributed welfare goods and food supplies amounting to P3,737,987.29, farm implements and equipment aggregating P227,179,958.00, construction materials amounting to P7,047,235.00, and drugs and medicines amounting to  P3,459,455.00  to the end-users respectively,  and as his basis in recording the items under the expense account.
Also, require the accountant to recognize the issuance of inventories aggregating P5,929,687.00, thus the affected accounts will be correctly stated in the FS.
Henceforth, the Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), supported  with a duly accomplished Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for all issued/distributed inventory including the issuance of office supplies, accountable forms, plates and stickers, food supplies and other supplies aggregating P4,384,414.22, and to regularly prepare and furnish these forms to the Municipal Accountant together with the duly accomplished and certified distribution list of all goods distributed to the end-users as his basis in the preparation of journal entry to record the expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts, hence to present fairly the affected accounts in the FS.

MCR 2025 Limitation Manpower Inventory Committee shall work with the accounting to carry out a thorough and complete physical inventory to reconcile with the reported PPE in the financial statement and maintain a complete and accurate record based on the result of the inventory in the FS to properly disposition the non-existing/missing properties and unserviceable PPEs, to derecognize small value properties below the capitalization threshold to semi-expendable properties.
observations.reason: Manpower limitation: small value properties which are below the capitalization threshold to be reclassified to semi-expendable properties.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate or trace the documents of recorded infrastructure assets and make the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct book value of the foregoing infrastructure assets, hence to present fairly and reliably the FS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and relevant offices to reconcile records, prepare adjusting journal entries and finalize the reconciliation of infrastructure asset balances and ensure proper recording of depreciation upon validation of supporting documents.
observations.reason: Discrepancies in the reconciliation (₱699,469.28 variance) with the Provincial Accountant. Initial historical records, documentation and coordination with the Provincial Accounting Office are ongoing; initial communications and delays in securing documents and reconciliation with the Provincial Accountant have caused continuous delay. The Municipal Accountant is conducting reconciliation to finalize the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a correcting/adjusting journal entry to restate the financial statements of the ensuing year, hence reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the FS, to wit:
GF Books:
(a) Debit Military, Police & Security Equipment 294,000.00; Debit Furniture and Fixtures 298,966.65; Credit Prior Period Adjustment 592,966.65
(b) Debit Prior Period Adjustment 374,740.00; Credit ICT Equipment 339,240.00; Credit Medical Equipment 35,500.00
(c) Debit Military, Police and Security Equipment 2,539,500.00; Debit Welfare Goods for Distribution 3,519,569.00; Credit ICT Equipment 2,539,500.00; Credit Food Supplies for Distribution 3,519,569.00
SEF Books:
Debit Prior Period Adjustment 422,240.00; Credit ICT Equipment 422,240.00
Henceforth, properly record the succeeding transactions in their correct account classification hence, to present fairly and accurately the financial position and performance of the LGU, thus could be relied upon in decision making.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made an adjusting journal entry to correct the errors pending the submission of the journal entry voucher (JEV).

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to make a compilation of any record/documentation and prepare the corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long outstanding liabilities to support the possible reversion in order not to overstate the liability accounts.  Likewise, attach Subsidiary Ledgers and all available records to support the amount of the JEV and attach a copy in case of reversion to the unappropriated surplus.  If there may be claims in the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in requesting for a supplemental budget for the settlement of the payable.
The Audit Team also recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to make a compilation of any record/documentation and prepare the corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long outstanding payable amounting to P104,579.43 to support the possible reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the GF in order not to overstate the liability accounts.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to compile records/documentation and prepare assessment/evaluation report, attach subsidiary ledgers and supporting records for possible reversion to unappropriated surplus and for future claims/supplemental budget basis.
observations.reason: Still verifying the existence of the accounts and compiling supporting documentation.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the variance of P699,469.28 with the records of the Provincial Accountant to ensure correct and accurate balance of the Due to LGUs account in the FS and to ensure the correctness of the remittances made, thus FS will be fairly presented.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile with the Provincial Accountant and verify remittances and records to ensure correct balances.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the supporting documents and explanations of the transactions recorded in the GJ for CY 2024 and henceforth, to continually support the GJ with the same documentation to ascertain the validity and propriety of the financial transactions recorded therein and the correctness of journal entries made.
Further, the Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to follow the prescribed procedures in the recording of financial transactions from the JEV to the corresponding journals and then to the GL as embodied in Annexes 1 to 5 of Volume II of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, ensuring that the necessary details, explanations, computations and supporting evidence/s are incorporated therein, hence to facilitate systematic verification of accounts presented in the FS.
Moreover, provide the necessary details in the Schedule of Receivables and Payables such as the names and addresses of the debtors and creditors, the specific description or nature of each account, and the date of accruals or incurrence of the receivables and obligations to facilitate verification of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant to submit supporting documents and explanations for transactions recorded in the GJ and to ensure future compliance with proper documentation and recording procedures per NGAS Annexes 1 to 5. Provide complete details in schedules of receivables and payables.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate submission of the DVs for audit and verification purposes, hence to ascertain the validity and propriety of the transactions and proper audit action, hence, any deficiencies will be communicated to management for the latter’s immediate appropriate action. Further, Management strictly observes the timely submission of transaction documents to facilitate the review thereon and avoid further audit action as provided in Section 39 (3) and 122 (2) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate submission of the DVs for audit and verification; reminded concerned offices of timely submission and instituted measures to consolidate and forward pending DVs to COA.
observations.reason: Some DVs have been submitted and processed for transmittal; others are still being consolidated and reviewed. High volume contributed to delays; the Accountant and Treasurer have reminded offices to comply.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that Management strictly observe the provisions of Section 44 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs on the correct procedures of check disbursement process, ensuring to secure first the certification on the availability of appropriation before processing the transaction for payment, hence, to determine that the disbursement has a valid appropriation and to avoid the incurrence of appropriations overdraft.  Likewise, ensure that the forms involved are completely dated to ascertain that all transactions went through the correct process of disbursement, thus, ascertaining the validity, and propriety of the financial transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer and concerned staff to ensure CAFOA/ALOBS certification is obtained prior to processing disbursements and to ensure forms are completely dated and processed in the correct sequence.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor to oversee the immediate transfer of the TDs of the 10 parcels of land aggregating P2,723,501.62 in the name of the Municipality of San Juan and to submit immediately the certified true copies of the TCT of the 44 parcels of land aggregating P113,622,471.06 and TDs of the 10 parcels of land aggregating P48,415,900.00, to ascertain absolute ownership over these parcels of land and to avoid possible ownership dispute which can result to wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Reissuance of lost titles; securing requirements for transfer and registration; payment of transfer taxes; processing of titles and submission of certified true copies to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some titles already titled pending submission of copies; processing of titles for some lands ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
Instruct the concerned officials to submit the lacking documents to the Municipal Accountant who will verify the completeness and accuracy thereof and subsequently submit the same to the Auditor in order for the latter to verify the validity, propriety and correctness of these documents and compliance of said transactions with the abovementioned provisions of law and regulations; and
Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant and concerned officials of strict compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 by supporting all disbursement vouchers with complete and accurate documents before effecting payment thereof to ascertain the validity, propriety and correctness of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials to submit lacking documents to Municipal Accountant for verification and subsequent submission to Auditor; Municipal Accountant and concerned officials to ensure strict compliance with documentary requirements before payment.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the submission of the liquidation reports and supporting documents of the aforementioned cash advances to ascertain the validity, propriety and correctness of these transactions.
Likewise, the Audit Team recommended that concerned officials and employees strictly comply with the provisions set forth in Items 4 and 5 of COA Circular 97-002 in the succeeding cash advances, and direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor its compliance, hence to facilitate efficient control over the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer committed to immediately submit required liquidation reports; Municipal Accountant to monitor compliance and ensure future adherence to COA Circular 97-002.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
Instruct the GSO to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and require the drivers to prepare Report of Official Travels per vehicle and properly accomplish the Driver's Trip Tickets pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 in order to provide adequate basis in determining the reasonableness of fuel consumption.
Also, require the supplier to immediately refund the excess payment of gasoline expenses amounting to P5,000.00, or instruct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the amount from payments of succeeding purchases due to the supplier, hence to restitute the overpaid amount.
observations.managementAction: Require the GSO to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and require drivers to prepare Reports of Official Travels per vehicle and properly accomplish Driver’s Trip Tickets; require supplier to refund excess payment of ₱5,000.00 or deduct from future payments; Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to verify overpayment and require refund if necessary.
observations.reason: Drivers and staff instructed and trained on proper trip ticket preparation; some drivers and staff changes and GSO staffing issues caused delays; refund processing with supplier is still under negotiation; preparation of monthly reports and full implementation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the GSO to assign property/inventory number of all properties and inventories to facilitate identification of properties which is useful in the conduct of annual physical inventory. Likewise, this will facilitate proper security and custodial of the properties of the Municipality.
Henceforth, the Audit Team recommended that the property/inventory numbers of motor vehicles shall be indicated in the driver’s trip ticket for easy verification of the validity and reasonableness of issued fuel. Likewise, the property/inventory numbers of properties for repair and maintenance shall be provided in the Repair History Report and Pre-Inspection Report to enable verification if properties under repair are duly owned by the Municipality. Also, this would facilitate proactive scheduling for maintenance of properties which will reduce downtime and costs, hence resulting in the efficient usage of government resources.
observations.managementAction: Assign or hire a General Services Officer (GSO) or assign staff to tag property/inventory numbers for all PPE and semi-expendable items; incorporate property numbers in trip tickets, repair history and inspection reports; train staff on tagging and monitoring.
observations.reason: GSO staffing constraints and heavy workload delayed full tagging; initial tagging and inclusion in trip tickets and reports started; plan to assign/hire a new GSO to fully implement tagging.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that henceforth, Management strictly comply with the provisions set forth in Section 68 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and Section 5 Volume 2 of the Government Procurement Manual in the assessment of the number of CD delays in the performance of the contract and the computation of liquidated damages which shall be at least equal to one-tenth of one percent (0.001) of the cost of the unperformed portion for every day of delay to ensure timely completion of the contract for the immediate usage and enjoyment by the intended beneficiaries/constituents of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management to strictly comply with RIRR of RA No. 9184 and Government Procurement Manual in assessing delays and computing liquidated damages; ensure procurement personnel enforce rules on extensions and liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare the disbursement vouchers for the remittance of the shares/dues to the barangays in the total amount of P2,380,547.73; and
henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to release the share of each barangay on RPT and CTCs on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter to enable the latter to utilize the funds for its operations.
observations.managementAction: Prepare DVs and remit the shares/dues to barangays; adopt quarterly remittance practice and ensure Municipal Accountant and Treasurer release shares within five days after quarter end.
observations.reason: Remittances were typically done annually by the Municipal Treasurer; 2024 balances were remitted in 2025; practice being adjusted to quarterly remittance as recommended.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to abide strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 by:
properly accomplishing and updating the signboards/billboards by providing all the relevant information pertaining to the project at hand to enable the public to see and read relevant information for them to assess if PPAs are fairly implemented and justifiably completed which in turn promotes good governance through transparency; and
informing the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted for verification.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Engineer to properly accomplish and update signboards/billboards by providing all relevant project information and to inform the Audit Team within ten days after award or before start of project that signboards/public notices are posted for verification.
observations.reason: Implementation in progress; municipality to implement the Audit Team’s recommendations in succeeding projects; monitoring and posting being scheduled.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
ensure that identified programs/projects under the 20% DF are implemented as planned to fully attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the municipality;
require the concerned officials to review the feasibility of the unimplemented programs/projects and plan for the necessary realignment of these funds to a more viable priority development project; and
encourage the LGU to formulate plans that are attainable within a year to maximize the benefits that could be derived from the full utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: MPDC and concerned offices to review unimplemented projects and reprogram funds where necessary; ensure future projects are feasible and implementable within the year; ongoing reprogramming of continuing appropriations.
observations.reason: Some projects under continuing appropriations are due for reprogramming; MPDC is reviewing unimplemented projects for reprogramming.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to ensure the optimal utilization of the RA 7171 and 8240 Municipal Share through proper and timely implementation of the identified PPAs so that the benefits expected from its timely completion will be delivered to the tobacco farmers.
require the concerned officials to review the feasibility of the unimplemented projects and plan for the necessary realignment of these funds to a more viable development projects that help in the attainment of the purpose and objective of the fund which is to advance the self-reliance of the farmer beneficiaries.
propose to the Sanggunian the re-appropriation of the prior year’s savings from implemented projects thus, maximize implementation and utilization of the funds for the benefit of the tobacco farmers.
observations.managementAction: MPDC to review unimplemented RA 7171/8240 projects for reprogramming; Municipal Engineer to assist in implementation; propose re-appropriation of prior year savings to Sanggunian.
observations.reason: MPDC is reviewing the unimplemented projects for reprogramming; volume of projects caused implementation delays.

observations.managementAction: Strengthen procurement planning and preparation of consolidated APP and PPMPs; centralize procurement for common-use items through GSO; ensure PRs/POs/AIRs are properly completed; record purchases as inventory under perpetual inventory method.
observations.reason: Procurement planning and consolidation initiated; GSO staffing and technical expertise constraints; steps taken but full centralization and compliance ongoing.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert to the unappropriated surplus of the GF the unspent balance of LDRRMF for the year 2018 amlounting to P537,605.64 and every year thereafter to have additional funds for other social services of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already drawn a JEV for the reversion of the TL_DRRMF to the unappropriated surplus of the GF; submission of the JEV to the Audit Team is pending; revert the unspent balance to unappropriated surplus.
observations.reason: Reversion JEV already prepared and submitted; funds reverted.

observations.managementAction:
The BAC now conducts Pre-procurement for all types of PPAs that fall within the specified ABC threshold that requires such activity.



The BAC ensures that winning bidder submits the required documents before contract signing.


Invitation letters are now being sent to observers within the prescribed period.


Notifications of Bid Submission are being prepared and sent to all bidders.



The BAC ensures that each stage of procurement process through competitive bidding and all its corresponding documents are strictly followed and prepared, respectively, in congruence with the Procurement Law.

The BAC is required to ensure that all procurement reports and documentation are meticulously prepared, accurately capturing the actual dates and times of each activity.

The Municipal Accountant made a checklist as a tool to ensure that all required documents are submitted by the BAC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to enforce strict compliance of the contractors by ensuring that all posted signboards for each infrastructure project contain all the relevant information and details, and that the project status is maintained as current and updated, thereby achieving full disclosure in the implementation of government projects and the right of the people to access up-to-date information on matters of public concern.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer started to strictly enforce the audit recommendation by visiting the sites and ensuring that the billboards are updated regularly.
observations.reason: Pictures were attached to the contract-related documents as proof of Management’s compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor to re-evaluate the actual use of the portions of municipality-owned properties leased to private entities and update the tax declarations, as applicable. 

Thereafter, the Municipal Treasurer to enforce the collection of real property taxes against these private entities leasing a portion of the properties of the municipality.
observations.managementAction:
On September 24, 2024, the Municipal Assessor conducted an ocular inspection on the properties mentioned in the audit observation. 

Tax Declaration, Appraisal and Assessment Sheets of each beneficial user are already prepared to be forwarded to the Provincial Assessor for approval.
observations.reason:
Tax Declaration, Appraisal and Assessment were already prepared.


Not Implemented. RPTs were not yet collected from the private entities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Department Heads to fast-track the completion of the required supporting documents by communicating to the Job Order Contracts, taking actions to address the cause of the delay in the processing of payments to ensure payments in a timely manner in accordance with the standard operating procedures provided in the system procedures.
observations.managementAction:
Following the receipt of the audit recommendation, the processing and release of JO wages has been streamlined, such that wages are released within three working days from receipt of complete supporting documents.  

Further, the Accounting Office has enrolled them in the online payment of payroll under the LBP We Access.
observations.reason: Verification during the post-audit of transactions revealed that Management already complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant finalized the reconciliation of said accounts.
observations.reason: Reclassification of affected liability accounts already effected in the CY 2024 financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned department/ office heads to:

Properly plan the PPAs most importantly their procurement schedule taking into consideration the time of implementation based on necessity and some other factors;

Judiciously prepare and accomplish the PPMPs for all of their PPAs; and 


Furnish the BAC and TWG Office the required pertinent technical specifications so that the latter offices will be able to prepare the Bidding Documents for the initiation of the procurement activities of the PPAs on or before the proposed procurement schedule.
observations.managementAction: The Department Heads assured compliance with the audit recommendations provided.
observations.reason:
PPAs included in the APP were started and implemented within the time schedule presented.


Not Implemented.

The Audit Team was not furnished with copies of PPMP, hence, Management’s compliance cannot be verified.


Not Implemented.

Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Human Resource Management Officer – Designate to submit to COA the complete leave cards of the retired/separated employees on CY 2021 for further review and verification of their leave balances as of date of retirement.

We also recommended that the LCE consider filling-up any vacant HR plantilla positions in order to provide the HR Division adequate manpower in the performance of their functions.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO II was filled up on May 16, 2024 by Mr. Lawrence Ople.
observations.reason:
Such records are now being submitted to the Audit Team.


Implemented.

Review of the manpower complement of the Municipality revealed that Mr. Lawrence Ople was the newly appointed HRMO II.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Council in coordination with the concerned department/office heads to:

Prioritize the implementation of the unimplemented development projects at year-end and strictly observe the guideline under DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020; and

Include in the AIP and annual budget only those projects that are procurement-and-implementation ready in order to fully optimize the utilization of the 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC assured compliance thereof.
observations.reason:
Review of the Quarterly Report of GPPA of the Municipal Engineer disclosed that all unimplemented projects at the end of the prior year are the ones being prioritized in the current year. 


Not Implemented. Certain PPAs identified in the budget were found to have no corresponding POWs.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the LCE prioritize the full implementation of the Municipal SWM projects especially the long-awaited construction of Engineered Sanitary Landfill and ensure effective and efficient enforcement of environmental ordinances towards the attainment of the objectives of the LGU’s Ten-Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction:
The Engineered Sanitary Landfill is already operational. It was provided by the PGLU and completed on May 2, 2023. 

As to the enforcement of existing environmental ordinances, the Office of the MENRO is releasing notice letters to business establishments with noted violations.
observations.reason:
Based on the documentary and physical verification conducted, it is hereby validated that the Engineered Sanitary Landfill has been completed and is already operational. 
Furthermore, it is confirmed that the Office of the MENRO is actively implementing the issuance of notice letters to business establishments found to have environmental violations, as now part of their regular monitoring and enforcement activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE through the BAC and TWG to conduct strict, extensive and efficient bid evaluation and post-qualification by scrupulously scrutinizing the documents submitted by the bidder particularly the Statement of All On-going Government and Private Contracts, List of Contractor’s Personnel of contractors and List of Contractor’s Equipment.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and TWG have assured to comply.
observations.reason: The contractors now disclose other projects contracted with other government agencies in the Statement of All On-going Government and Private Contracts as per Post-qualification Report of the TWG.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by speeding up the implementation of the prioritized programs, projects and activities as per Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and that closer coordination among officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to achieve the desired level of outcomes.


Instruct the Local Development Council to immediately look into the unimplemented developmental projects as embodied in the APP and develop feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement those projects.

Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very prudent, meticulous, conscientious and precise in evaluating the timely and perfect implementation of each and every project.  Likewise, conduct regular inspection of projects together with the Project Monitoring Committee so that accomplishment is monitored and evaluated based on the project implementation schedule as stipulated in the APP.

Ensure that priority socio-economic and development programs and projects are executed within the fiscal year to facilitate delivery of public services at the soonest possible time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality assured to optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund for social, economic and environmental services.  Unimplemented PPAs in the previous years would be thoroughly evaluated as to their viability for implementation within the year.
observations.reason:
Review of the Quarterly Report of GPPA of the Municipal Engineer disclosed that all unimplemented projects at year-end of prior year are the ones being prioritized in the current year.


Implemented.

Same reason as above.


Implemented.

PPAs included in the APP were started and implemented within the time schedule presented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Limit the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA and ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay which is ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 52 of Republic Act No. 11620.

Ensure that the required minimum take home pay is attained after all the mandatory and other authorized deductions from the salary of each employee are deducted from the gross salary to guarantee that there is enough amount to sustain the needs of the employee for a month.

See to it that an employee applying for a loan will only avail of the amount that has a monthly amortization of not more than the amount in excess of ₱5,000.00 after deducting all the authorized deductions.
observations.managementAction: The Management no longer approve loan application of employees with net take home pay below ₱5,000.00 for the CY 2024.
observations.reason:
Post-audit of payrolls during the year showed Management’s compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer, Municipal Accountant and the members of the Inventory Committee to follow the procedural guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE account balances and as (1) Preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking; (2) Inventory taking; (3) Reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records; (4) Disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs; (5) Derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability; and (6) Accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had conducted physical count of its PPEs as of December 31, 2024. The General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office is still in the process of updating their respective records which includes the property cards, property ledger cards. Likewise, they also prioritized the disposal of all unserviceable PPEs which is currently ongoing, before conducting the one-time cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold those taxes arising from Accounts Payable of CY 2023 still outstanding as of date. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Administrator to coordinate with the department heads for the delays in submission of documents pertaining to the obligations incurred at year-end. Likewise, ensure that the corresponding supporting documents of obligations that will form part of Accounts Payable for CY 2024 will be submitted on time to the Accounting Office so as to properly record and withheld taxes and be remitted on the subsequent month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office had already withheld taxes for those payables of CY 2023 still outstanding as of date. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Administrator to coordinate with the department heads for the set-up of Payables for CY 2024. The Municipal Accounting Office had submitted copies of Journal Entries for the set-up of Payables for CY 2024 and the withholding Taxes to Due to BIR account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to the following: Cause the immediate implementation of the unimplemented projects and the completion of the on-going projects identified in the audit finding. Likewise, provide an update as to the status of those projects. If the projects will no longer be implemented and the funds are reprogrammed to another projects, include those along with the reasons in the status for submission to the audit team; and b. Consider the unexpended balances of the completed projects as of December 31, 2024 of P809,186.36 for reversion to unappropriated surplus for possible allocation to other developmental projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had provided an explanation for the non-implementation of projects noted in the CY 2023 AAR.
observations.reason: The Municipality partially implemented several PPAs charged under the Gender and Development Fund for the Municipality. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 3, Page Nos. 71 to 75, Part II of this Report For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to implement the programs, projects and activities under Current and Continuing Appropriation and under the Trust Liability  DRRMF account, which were noted in this audit findings. Conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those identified PPAs to be included in the LDRRM Plan are those that can be implemented and completed within the target period. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to update the Audit Team with the status of those projects or if they will no longer be implemented, cite the reasons for non-implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had implemented several projects funded from CY 2023 and previous years continuing appropriation. While some of the projects were reverted and replanned for other development projects.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 5, Page Nos. 67 to 68, Part II of this Report For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS Focal Point Person and GAD TWG to conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those identified PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget are those that are considered by the Municipality as gender activities that can be implemented and completed within the target period.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. OIC  Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the Trust Liabilities account particularly the noted fund transfers, prepare adjusting Journal Entries, if necessary, to adjust the correct balance of the affected accounts, and provide copies to the audit team. b. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to cause the immediate implementation of the project concreting of road at Barangay Quipot; and c. Municipal Local Finance Committee to strictly comply with the posting and reporting requirements in the utilization of LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC, pursuant to Section 5 of DBM LBC Nos. 144 and 151.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had instructed the OIC - Municipal Accountant to review the composition of Trust Fund and prepare Journal Entries to reclassify the fund transfer from Trust Liabilities to Due to NGAs account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to verify those stale checks as of December 31, 2023 with its payee to determine the actual existence of those obligations. Likewise, ensure that checks issued in CY 2024 will be monitored and that payees are duly notified of their outstanding checks at least one month before they become stale. We also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office to furnish the Municipal Treasurer of the bank reconciliation statement showing in complete detail the list of outstanding checks for the succeeding months of CY 2024 to serve as guide in preparing the notification letters.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had already started sending notification letters to those payees whose checks had become stale or will become stale. The Municipal Accounting Office had already furnished the Municipal Treasurer with the Bank Reconciliation Statement to identify the checks that are nearing the six month period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that cash advances will only be granted to accountable officers with no previous unliquidated cash advances. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that cash advances granted will not exceed the maximum cash accountabilities and the Municipal Treasurer to verify the bond coverage.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had provided the reasons for granting cash advances with previous unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Due to limited number of Disbursing Officers and voluminous transactions of the office cash advances for the months of January 2024 to April 2024 were still granted to Disbursing Officers despite existence of unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. GSO-OIC to prepare and submit the consolidated Property Inventory Form (PIF) listing of all the insurable properties and other assets to GSIS for their evaluation and approval, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor, in compliance to Section 5 of RA No. 656 and COA Circular 2018-002; and b. Municipal Budget Officer to include in the annual budget adequate appropriations to fund the application of insurance of all the insurable properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already started coordination with GSIS for the determination/identification of insurable properties and preparation of the Consolidated Property Inventory Form.
observations.reason: Not Adequate appropriation for the insurance of its properties were not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality always adhere to the provisions of Sections 4(3) and 4(8) of P.D. No. 1445 that trust fund be utilized for the specific purpose it was intended and exercise sound fiscal administration in the local government operations provided not contravening to existing laws and regulations. We also recommended that the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Accountant reconcile employees' records with the GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality conducted reconciliation and corrections; Minor deficiencies noted were corrected on the succeeding months.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality conduct the periodic physical count of its properties and reconcile the results with the property and accounting records. We also recommended that the General Services Officer maintain the subsidiary records for each class of property, plant and equipment or the property cards prescribed in the accounting manual and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and reconcile the property cards with the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN).
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had conducted the periodic physical count and reconciled the records. The General Services Office maintained subsidiary records for each class of property which were updated.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by Regional Director on July 3, 2024, to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the approved Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan covering the period CY 2015 to 2025 in compliance with declared policy of the State to adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program and knowing that the LGUs are primarily responsible for the implementation and enforcement of the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality fully implemented the approved Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan and the plan was revised and submitted.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by Regional Director on July 3, 2024, to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Public Employment Service Officer- Designate to hasten the implementation of the Diversified Livelihood Formation due to Typhoon Odette Program funded by the DOLE RO VIII for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Trace the variance noted in the Due to NGAs year-end balance in the Notes to FS and SLs/GL and make correcting journal entries, if necessary; and
 draw journal entries to transfer the Municipality’s 20% share from Fire Code Fees to the Trust Liabilities account.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer expedite the preparation of the final fund utilization reports of all implemented and completed PPAs funded by National Government Agencies. It is also recommended to promptly return the unexpended balances to extinguish the Municipality’s liability in the books of accounts, and put the funds to use for other equally important projects of the Source Agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and the claimants concerned secure Certification from the Secretary of the DOH that the place of assignment/travel is a strife-torn/embattled/disease-infested/distress or isolated areas/stations, or areas declared under state of calamity or emergency, or with volcano activity or eruption.
Also, we recommended that the Public Health Workers refund the overpayment of health benefits they received.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the Certification by the Secretary of the DOH as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2023 and strictly observe the guidelines on the grant of hazard pay, subsistence and laundry allowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
require the deputy collectors to remit all collections daily and intact to the Liquidating Officer or at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00, and
issue a written authority deputizing the Barangay Treasurers to collect Community Tax on her behalf.  In the absence of said authority, require the collectors to immediately turnover collections and accountable forms to the Municipal Treasurer.  Henceforth, no accountable forms shall be issued to Barangay Treasurers without a written authority as deputy collectors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:

Stop the payment of confidential expenses thru reimbursement. The release and utilization of the funds shall be thru a cash advance by a Special Disbursing Officer or the Head of the Agency, and

Comply with the aforementioned reporting requirements, upon request and grant of cash advance and its liquidation.
observations.reason:
Cash advance vouchers made in CY 2024 were not submitted by the Municipal Accountant or her
authorized representative in a sealed envelope, to the Audit Team for
post- audit within seven (7) days after release of check as prescribed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: 

Initiate the commencement of the PPAs funded by NGAs that are due for implementation to attain the purpose of the fund; 
 
Require the Municipal Accountant to effectively enforce the liquidation of fund transfers once PPAs are completed including the remittance of the unspent balance to the National Treasury; and 

The Municipal Treasurer to remit the outstanding national collections to Bureau of Treasury. 

The Municipal Accountant initiate the request for write-off of the unsubstantiated balances of the Due to NGAs account pursuant to COA Circular 2016-006 dated December 19, 2016.
We recommended to keep up the implementation of DRRM PPAs in the subsequent years to ensure full utilization of funds and implementation of approved PPAs as envisioned in the LDRRM and AIP.

observations.recommendation: In the light of the foregoing observations, we recommended that Management utilize the HGDG in the selection of PPAs and budget determination to arrive at a more gender-responsive PPAs and ensure the full and proper implementation thereof.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: As advised by DILG, HGDG will be used only for attributed programs. In our case, HGDG will not be used for programs under GAD mandates for they are already direct GAD programs as specified in the PPA’s that GAD related in the DILG-PCW-NEDA-DBM JMC 2013-01. In 2019 training attended by the Budget Officer and former MSWDO with the DILG-NEDA-CAR and PCW as facilitators, the DILG Resource Person also mentioned that HGDG will not be used in the programs under GAD Mandates using MOOE, PS and CO funds. Further, if fund used in the programs is outside MOOE, PS and CO funds and the LGU would want to attribute as part of GAD fund, then HGDG will be utilized. The undersigned (current MSWDO) was also informed that this matter was also settled between DILG, PCW and COA-CAR during GAD Forum in 2018 at Baguio City where representatives of COA-CAR made sure to relay the information to the entire COA-CAR to avoid issuance of AOM to this matter. In this matter, HGDG was not administered because there was no attributed program implemented for the year 2023 in the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Determine the unclaimed retention money for its possible payment and coordinate with the Acting Municipal Engineer for the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance for these projects which will serve as their basis in the preparation of the Disbursement Voucher even without request for payment from the contractors/supplier; and

Make a compilation of any record/documentation and prepare the corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long outstanding liabilities to support the possible reversion in order not to overstate the liability accounts.  Likewise, Subsidiary ledgers and all available records to support the amount should be attached to the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) that has to be prepared in case of reversion to the unappropriated surplus. If there may be claims in the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in requesting for a supplemental budget for the settlement of the payable.
observations.managementAction: Partial payment of the outstanding liabilities was made.
observations.reason: Management was able to pay the total amount of P35,977,923.67 retention money, however the total amount of P9,310,555.60 is still outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor and officials concerned to prioritize the transfer of ownership over the nine parcels of land acquired to avoid exposing the same to possible third-party claims, land disputes and interests/penalties/surcharges for late registration.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing transfer of title
observations.reason:
According to the Municipal Assessor, the process in the transfer of ownership of the nine (9) parcels of land is ongoing.

However, title or any document of ownership of the other 37 lands with a total market value of P93,351,476.20 (based on the submitted inventory report) were not furnished to the Auditor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Head of Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies held for distribution or consumption and ensure prompt submission of duly and completely accomplished Report on Physical Count of Inventories not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively as provided in Section 124, Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs in order to ascertain the condition and completeness of the inventory accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE instruct the BAC to carefully examine the procurements of goods or services of the Municipality in order to consolidate those which are similar in nature to avoid splitting of contracts which is in consonance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and to ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government is obtained.
observations.managementAction: Management tried their best to abide with the recommendation, however there were still some occurrences.
observations.reason: Management procured supplies, materials and equipment per office and as the need arises

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat, as a support staff of the BAC, see to it that the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed are posted in the PhilGEPS website within the prescribed period of 3 and 15 calendar days in order to ensure procurement transparency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct Supply Officer or the official responsible in accepting deliveries to furnish a notice to the Office of the Auditor on the deliveries of procured items within 24 hours from the acceptance to comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 96-010. If personal submission of said notice is not possible, the duly signed scanned copy of the notice could be submitted thru the official email address of the Audit Team (r108agb@yahoo.com) to enable the latter to conduct inspection of procured items to determine whether or not such deliveries conformed with the quantity, quality and specifications in the Purchase Order.  Thus, in case of any discrepancies, Management be immediately informed for its appropriate and immediate action. Likewise, it is encouraged that Management attach pictures of the items delivered in the Disbursement Voucher.

No. 7160, thus, maximize implementation and utilization of the funds for the PPAs to uplift the economic and social well-being of the constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of recorded parcels of land in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 39 of PD No. 1445 to establish ownership thereof and to fully protect the interest of the municipality.  



We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to exercise added endeavor to locate documents and determine the details of unidentified land accounts for proper recognition and establishment of ownership thereon.

29 parcels of land identified by the Municipal Assessor as property of the LGU were not recorded in the books and eight parcels of land recorded at market value.

We recommended that the Management:

Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the General Service Officer and Municipal Assessor to exert further efforts in retrieval of documents required to establish ownership and cost valuation of the unrecorded land to facilitate its recording in the books, at acquisition cost, or fair value, if acquired through non-exchange transaction;

Make the necessary adjustment by recording the land account at acquisition cost, or fair value, if acquired through non-exchange transaction, based on available supporting documents; and

Initiate the transfer of ownership of road lots to various barangays which are not held for use by the Municipal Government.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Limit the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA and ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay which is ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 52 of Republic Act No. 11620.



Ensure that the required minimum take home pay is attained after all the mandatory and other authorized deductions from the salary of each employee are deducted from the gross salary to guarantee that there is enough amount to sustain the needs of the employee for a month.

See to it that an employee applying for a loan will only avail of the amount that has a monthly amortization of not more than the amount in excess of ₱5,000.00 after deducting all the authorized deductions.

of
