observations.recommendation: 46. The Provincial DRRM Officer agreed to strive for the timely and full implementation of the LDRRM-related PPAs and observe the provisions of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 in allocating LDRRM Fund solely for DRRM-related projects.
observations.managementAction: Management continued efforts but utilization remained low. The Provincial DRRM Officer agreed to implement improvements.

observations.recommendation:
48. Recommend to the Local Chief Executive, through the Local Finance Committee, the appropriation of the amount of ₱585,969.43 in the General Fund to reimburse the 20 per cent DF for the costs of the equipment charged to the 20 per cent DF which were not directly attributable to local development projects.
49. Review and analyze the recorded CIP, including those reverted to PPE accounts, and correct erroneous charges thereto in order to fairly present the accounts in accordance with the applicable provisions of IPSAS 17 on the recognition of PPE.
observations.managementAction: Provincial Budget Office and PACCO to create adjusting entries to rectify the deficiency. The reimbursement AIP deemed inoperative by DBM in the past; PACCO and PBO to process adjustment entries; no final action yet.

observations.recommendation:
50. Require the Committee to conduct an actual count of inventories and prepare the RPCI; and the GSO and the Provincial Accountant to maintain property records and reconcile the same.
51. Consolidate and submit the SSMI and update records for proper accounting.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee had been recommended and reconstituted by Executive Order and had commenced work on inventory count. However, the Committee did not submit a copy of the inventory count to the Auditor. GSO and PAccO to update records; reconciliation resumed in ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
52. Enjoin the concerned department heads to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2015-008 and perform respective duties.
53. Require the Provincial Engineer to implement required reconciliations and asset management procedures.
observations.managementAction: Management had not taken action.

observations.recommendation:
54. Require the concerned Heads of Offices submit supporting documents to substantiate the payment of AHDP such as authority from the Agency Head, report on the number of COVID-19 patients attended/monitored, daily status/monitoring report, and actual work done specifying number of hours rendered.
55. Provide written document from the Office of the MAIP Program Chief for Region 7 authorizing the Chief of Hospital to approve MAIP grants above ₱50,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Management required the concerned offices to submit the necessary supporting documents; however, submissions were incomplete. Some documentation remains outstanding and unprovided to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
56. Require the responsible office to submit copies of distribution lists duly acknowledged by recipients indicating name, signature, date of receipt, kind and quantity of items received.
57. For documentation and accountability, ensure that POs contain specifications and brand names to determine price reasonableness.
observations.managementAction: Distribution lists were requested and some submitted; some distribution lists were incomplete. The PDRRMO and other concerned personnel were consolidating recipients. Utilization of rapid tests supported only with names of recipients; signatures were missing in some instances.

observations.recommendation:
58. Management instruct the PEMO to monitor the submission of 10-year SWMPs of the Municipalities and thereafter enjoin the PSWMB to fast track the formulation of the Provincial 10-year SWMP of the Province.
59. PEMO to coordinate with LGUs for submission and consolidate the data for finalization of the Provincial SWMP.
observations.managementAction: PEMO and PAccO to follow up on missing LGU SWMPs; LGU submissions ongoing. As of date, some LGUs have approved 10-year SWMPs; others pending.

observations.recommendation:
60. Stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials directly to expense and record purchases and utilization of these items in accordance with Sections 114 and 121 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
61. Require the requisitioning office to submit the RIS to the GSO on a weekly basis.
62. Require the GSO to consolidate all the RIS on the SSMI and submit the same to the Accounting Office; and
63. Require the Provincial Accountant to record to the related expense accounts the supplies consumed based on the SSMI and to maintain perpetual inventory records to be reconciled periodically with the records of the GSO.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and Accounting Office to stop practice and to record transactions properly. Implementation hampered by lack of personnel and difficulties in manual recording; partial measures being taken.

observations.recommendation:
70. Coordinate with other government agencies for the possible collection, transport and disposal of accumulated pathological and non-pathological hospital wastes in the Province.
71. Regularly inspect and maintain the cleanliness of the storage area as well as the area surroundings of the non-pathological hospital in compliance with the provisions of the Health Care Waste Management Manual.
observations.managementAction: No accredited forwarder available; PDRRMO attempted to fast-track liquidations by consolidating recipients. Regular inspection and maintenance hampered by lack of manpower and storage facilities. Some actions initiated.

observations.recommendation: 73. Exert and intensify the collection of outstanding loans by sending final demand letter. Take legal action for coop borrowers who are operational and have capacity to pay but refuse to make repayment of their outstanding loans.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters sent; some payments received; some borrowers retired or separated making trace difficult. Collection efforts ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the:
Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Supply Officer, to annually conduct the complete the physical count of all properties, the preparation of the RPCPPE, and reconcile the result of the inventory with the balances recorded by the Municipal Accountant to ensure the existence, completeness and accuracy recorded in the PPE accounts and reported in the financial statement; and 

Supply Officer to maintain the PC which should be reconciled with the PPELC maintained by the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: The same audit observation on the PPE account is discussed in Part II Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMO to fast track the implementation of the approved DRRM PPAs to ensure full utilization of funds and implementation of PPAs as envisioned in the LDRRM Plan and AIP. 

Also, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to stop processing and charging expenditures that are not allowed under prescribed guidelines in the utilization of the disaster fund.
observations.reason: An audit observation on inappropriate charging of expenses to the LDRRM Fund is discussed in Part II Finding No. 3 of this report.
An audit observation on the non-optimal utilization of the 20% DF current and prior year appopriations is discussed in Part II Finding No. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: 

Initiate the commencement of the PPAs funded by NGAs that are due for implementation to attain the purpose of the fund; 
 
Require the Municipal Accountant to effectively enforce the liquidation of fund transfers once PPAs are completed including the remittance of the unspent balance to the National Treasury; and 

The Municipal Treasurer to remit the outstanding national collections to Bureau of Treasury. 

The Municipal Accountant initiate the request for write-off of the unsubstantiated balances of the Due to NGAs account pursuant to COA Circular 2016-006 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: An audit observation on the implementation of all PPAs funded by funds received from National Government Agencies is discussed in Part II Finding No.4 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the efforts exerted by the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the LDRMMO and other stakeholders in the DRR Management activities of the Municipality during the year.  

We recommended to keep up the implementation of DRRM PPAs in the subsequent years to ensure full utilization of funds and implementation of approved PPAs as envisioned in the LDRRM and AIP.
observations.reason:
Implementation rate for the programmed LDRRM PPAs increased to 95% during the year.


Full utilization of the fund was not obtained.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Head, Engineering Office to:

Ensure that all conditions preliminary to the commencement of construction have been addressed; and

Review thoroughly the request for work suspensions by the contractors based on complete and relevant documents.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Engineer did not ensure that all conditions preliminary to the commencement of construction were addressed.

Delay in the implementation of PPAs was observed during the audit year. This is discussed in Part II Finding No. 2 in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 
Require the Supply Officer to prepare a separate Inventory Report on obsolete, unserviceable/damaged and beyond repair PPEs using the Waste Material Report (WMR-Appendix 68) and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IRRUP-Appendix 67) of the GAM for LGUs. 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to drop from the books disposed items using the original copy of the WMR and IRRUP when properly authorized as basis for adjusting the accounts affected by means of a journal entry voucher; Implemented but without authorization by the COA

Require the Supply Officer to update property cards at the time of delivery and reconcile  with the ledgers cards of the Municipal Accounting Office.
observations.reason:
Dropping of unserviceable properties cannot be undertaken since the submitted IIRUP is still subject for perusal by the Audit Team and endorsement to  the Technical Audi Group for inspection.
 
Property cards are not updated as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
1.19 We recommended that Management:
a. Require the CAD to prepare adjustments to:
• Record the CIP-Buildings and Other Structures pertaining to the SJMC
project of P332,173,902.73 to its proper asset account; and
• Record the cost of the demolished buildings/structures in the amount
of P151,947,400.99 and the cost of the new buildings/structures
constructed thereat once appraised;
b. Cause the appraisal of the new buildings/structures constructed in the
location of previously reported demolished buildings/structures as
reflected in the RPCPPE; and
c. Direct the GSD to immediately prepare the required documents for the
disposal of unserviceable PPE and submit the request to CAO for
evaluation and technical inspection.

2.13 We recommended that Management direct the CAD to:
a. Recognize AP for the completed infrastructure projects in the amount of
P93,292,701.54 and record these payables of the City in the prescribed
General Journal; and
b. Require the CAD and GSD to submit to CAO the lacking documents to
support the payables of P62,046,856.35.

3.15 We recommended that Management:
a. Fast-track the assessment, processing, review and filing of reimbursable
claims to PhilHealth;
b. Identify lapses on procedures and processes, and set-up efficient and
effective control mechanism to minimize, if not eliminate, unresolved
issues on claims; and
c. Ensure the timely submission and completeness of documents
supporting the claims for reimbursement including the immediate refiling
of RTH claims as indicated in the notification letters so as not to invalidate
the same.

4.10 We recommended that Management require:
a. SJMC to submit regularly to CAD the schedule of reimbursable claims, on
whatever status, for recording in the books of accounts and necessary
adjustments if any;
b. CAD to record receivables and income under accrual basis of accounting;
and
c. CAD and SJMC evaluate the collectability of the reimbursable claims from
PhilHealth and set-up provisions for impairment.

5.17 We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the CAD and City Treasury Department to remit in full the amount of
P10,840,504.22 to GSIS and PhilHealth in compliance with laws, rules and
regulations; and
b. Direct the CAD to make an in-depth analysis and reconciliation of the Inter-
Agency Payable accounts with negative balances and discrepancies of
P14,105,034.71 and prepare adjustments to reflect the correct balances of
the accounts at certain period.

6.14 We recommended that Management prepare a catch-up plan for the timely
implementation of the 32 unimplemented LDRRM PPAs.

7.13 We recommended and Management agreed to improve significantly the
planning, budgeting, execution/utilization of the LCCAP fund to ensure the
achievement of the City’s mandate on climate change.

8.12 We recommended that Management closely monitor the efficient and timely
implementation of the planned LCPC PPAs to ensure the full achievement of
its mandate of promoting the best interest and rights of children of the City.

1.10. In view of the noted lapse, we recommended and Management agreed to
instruct the-
• Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical inventory of all
properties and equipment of the LGU and accomplish the
corresponding RPCPPE to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor
within the prescribed period;
• Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain PPE
Ledger Cards and Property Cards for easy reconciliation of the
account;
• General Services Officer to maintain ARE/PAR and ensure its renewal
every three years; and
• Municipal Accountant to reconcile PPE records with the General
Services Officer and to effect necessary adjusting entries for
variances noted, if any, to establish the accuracy of the reported
balances of the PPE.

2.4. We recommended and Management agreed to require the established
Inventory Committee to conduct comprehensive annual physical inspection
and count of local road network projects in the Municipality, and the General
Services Officer to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road
Network, maintain and update Local Road Inventory and Road Map annually
and submit such reports to this Office for proper evaluation.

3.5. We recommended and Management agreed to adhere with the provisions of
Sections 233, 235 and 248 of RA 7160 and Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS
for LGUs, and furnish a copy to COA.

4.8. We recommended and Management agreed to review the documentary
requirements in COA Circular No. 2012-001, and other relevant rules and
regulations.

5.5. Considering the foregoing observations, we recommended and
Management agreed to comply with the mandates of Item 5.10.1 of COA
Circular No. 97-002 and Item 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

6.6. Considering the foregoing observation, we recommended that
Management:
• Revisit MOAs for the unimplemented PPAs and properly assess the
implementation of the desired projects and/or activities, if practicable;
• Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the Bureau of Treasury
accounts of the source agencies the unutilized portion of funds for the
completed projects, as well as the balance of the funds for projects
which are already impracticable; and
• Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the
account and prepare adjusting entries for the unreconciled accounts
once reconciled.

7.11. In view of the noted lapses, we recommended and Management agreed to
effect payments only on vouchers that are properly documented and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and attach checklist of
documentary requirements for every transaction/DV and ensure that all
required documents were submitted.

8.6. In view of the noted lapses, we recommended and Management agreed to
ensure the submission of accounts within the prescribed periods
pursuant to Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Paragraph 7 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006.

9.4. We recommended and Management agreed to initiate the refund of the
personal share to the dismissed LGU official since the withheld amounts
were not anymore remitted to the insurance agency, and the Municipal
Accountant adjust accordingly, the refund and the reversal of the
government share in the books of the LGU to arrive at a more accurate
and reliable balances of the affected accounts.

10.5. We recommended and Management agreed to revisit the payroll
deductions and strictly comply with the minimum net take-home pay of
₱5,000.00 provided under Section 56 of RA 11936. Any deficiency in loan
payments and other obligations because of the mandatory minimum
requirement may be addressed through direct payment arrangements to
be voluntarily made by the employees to the concerned institutions.

11.7. Considering the foregoing observation, we recommended that Municipal
Engineer submit justification as to the deficiencies noted and to require
the contractor to undertake the remedial measures cited by the Technical
Services of COA-CAR. In case the contractor fails to institute the
necessary corrective actions, we recommend that management
implement the remedies provided for in Section 62 of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184.

12.9. In the light of the foregoing observations, we recommended that
Management, henceforth, utilize the HGDG in the selection of PPAs and
budget determination to arrive at a more gender-responsive PPAs and
ensure the full and proper implementation thereof.

13.6. In view of the noted deficiency, we recommended and Management
agreed to abide with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2014-59 and ensure the proper utilization of funds for the Senior Citizens
and Persons with Disabilities to improve the well-being of the elderly and
the persons with disabilities.

14.6. We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the proper and full
implementation of projects, programs, and activities under the fund for
the support and protection of children within the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the SB to pass a resolution authorizing the transfer of multi-purpose buildings, and other facilities to the various recipient barangays and schools, and then prepare Deeds of Donation for the actual transfer and acceptance by the latter to substantiate the transfer of ownership of the said properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee who shall prepare PIP containing the specific assignments/duties of the committee members, the cut-off date and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start to finish.
Thereafter, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Inventory Committee, GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to undertake the procedural guidelines for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to come up with more reliable account balances to be presented in the financial statements.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to prepare Property Cards and PPELC, respectively, for each class of PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to enhance their preparation of supporting documents taking into consideration the clarity and consistency of the terms and conditions integrated therein, thereby reducing the possibility of misinterpretation and avoiding disputes during contract execution.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor to grant only delivery extensions based on grounds that were cautiously investigated to be meritorious and supporting documents justifying the request for extension should be attached to the request letter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, direct the Project Engineers to:
strictly require all contractors to ensure their entire workforce are wearing the required safety gear or personal protective equipment at all times within the construction project site for the protection and welfare of both the construction workers and the general public;
closely monitor the proper implementation of the CSHP and standards by the contractors at the project sites by maintaining detailed records of contractor’s non-compliance per site visitation, which can be referenced during future payment processes; and
formulate an internal policy that clearly defines the appropriate sanctions or penalties to be applied to contractors found in violation of the CSHP to ensure accountability and adherence to safety protocols.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to enforce strict compliance of the contractors by ensuring that all posted signboards for each infrastructure project contain all the relevant information and details, and that the project status is maintained as current and updated, thereby achieving full disclosure in the implementation of government projects and the right of the people to access up-to-date information on matters of public concern.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO to install a sound internal control, such as specific guidelines on the grant of AICS and other financial assistance to its constituents to avoid granting of assistance in varying amounts to ensure equitable distribution of financial assistance.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the legislative department to stop identification and implementation of PPAs that are not aligned with its mandate, powers, duties and functions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor to re-evaluate the actual use of the portions of municipality-owned properties leased to private entities and update the tax declarations, as applicable.
Thereafter, the Municipal Treasurer to enforce the collection of real property taxes against these private entities leasing a portion of the properties of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Department Heads to fast-track the completion of the required supporting documents by communicating to the Job Order Contracts, taking actions to address the cause of the delay in the processing of payments to ensure payments in a timely manner in accordance with the standard operating procedures provided in the system procedures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The BAC to ensure that all PPAs including those funded from Continuing Appropriations are included in the APP; and
The Department Heads of implementing Office such as Municipal Engineer, to see to it that PPAs are specifically identified in the plan and budget fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services
Officer, Municipal Accountant and the members of the Inventory Committee to follow
the procedural guidelines on the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances
prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 such as (1) Preliminary activities prior to
the conduct of inventory taking; (2) Inventory taking; (3) Reconciliation of inventory
count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records; (4) Disposition procedures
for non-existing/missing PPEs; (5) Procedures in the derecognition of non-
existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability; and (6) Accounting
entries and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
review the noted observations above and effect the necessary adjustments after
relevant documents are secured. Submit copies of the adjusting journal entries made
to the audit team. If adjustments will not be made, submit an explanation letter
supported with necessary schedules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
withhold those taxes arising from Accounts Payable of CY 2023 still outstanding as of date.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Administrator
to coordinate with the department heads for the delays in submission of documents
pertaining to obligations incurred at year-end. Likewise, ensure that the
corresponding supporting documents of obligations that will form part of Accounts
Payable for CY 2024 will be submitted on time to the Accounting Office so as to
properly record and withheld taxes and be remitted on the subsequent month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and
Municipal Planning and Development Officer to the following:

a. Provide an explanation for the non-implementation and completion of the
identified 29 projects with an appropriation of P33,117,987.35, non-completion
within the year of the on-going projects of P4,100,000.00 and non-utilization of the
remaining balance of P2,970,330.54 for the three projects.

b. Cause the immediate implementation of the unimplemented projects and the
completion of the on-going projects identified in the audit finding. Likewise,
provide an update to the audit team as to the status of those projects. If the projects
will no longer be implemented and the funds reprogrammed to another projects,
include those along with the reasons in the status for submission to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to implement the programs, projects
and activities under Current and Continuing Appropriation and under the Trust
Liability – DRRM account, which were noted in this audit findings. Conduct
thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those identified PPAs to be included in
the LDRRM Plan are those that can be implemented and completed within the target
period.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to update the
audit team with the status of those projects or if they will no longer be implemented,
cite the reasons for non-implementation.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
require the GFPS Focal Point Person and GAD TWG to conduct thorough planning
and analysis to ensure that those identified PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and
Budget are those that can be implemented and completed within the target period.
Take into consideration the feasibility and propriety of the proposed PPA before
including it in the plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:

a. OIC – Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the Trust Liabilities
account particularly the noted fund transfers, prepare adjusting Journal Entries, if
necessary, to adjust the correct balance of the affected accounts, and provide
copies to the audit team.

b. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to cause the immediate
implementation of the project and the utilization of the fund transfer intended for the
concreting of road at Barangay Quipot; and

c. Municipal Local Finance Committee to strictly comply with the posting and
reporting requirements in the utilization of LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC,
pursuant to Section 5 of DBM LBC Nos. 144 and 151.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to verify
those stale checks as of December 31, 2023 with its payee to determine the actual
existence of those obligations recorded in the Accounts Payable. Likewise, ensure
that checks issued in CY 2024 will be monitored and that payees are duly notified of
their outstanding checks at least one month before they become stale.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting
Office to furnish the Municipal Treasurer of the bank reconciliation statement showing
in complete detail the list of outstanding checks for the succeeding months of CY 2024
to serve as guide in preparing the notification letters.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
provide explanation letter for the occurrence of granting cash advances to each
disbursing officers with previous unliquidated cash advances and disbursement
vouchers which lacked the required certification from their office.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that cash advances will only be granted to accountable officers with no
previous unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. GSO-OIC to prepare and submit the consolidated Property Inventory Form (PIF)
listing of all the insurable properties and other assets to GSIS for their evaluation and
approval, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor, in compliance to Section 5 of RA No.
656 and COA Circular 2018-002; and

b. Municipal Budget Officer to include in the annual budget adequate appropriations
to fund the application of insurance of all the insurable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Human Resource
Management Officer and Municipal Accountant reconcile employees’ records with the
GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the unremitted
balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the
cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper adjustments
to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed
penalties.

1.14 We reiterated our recommendation from the prior year and Management
agreed to observe the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No.
2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances to have reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence,
condition, and accountability as follows:

a. Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical
count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at the station, and disposition
for non-existing/missing PPE items and require the Accountant and
Property Officer to reconcile their records based on the results of the
actual physical inventory and effect necessary adjustments;
b. Prepare the required PIP to be reviewed and approved by the Head of
Agency and submitted to the Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before
the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and
c. Adopt the uniform numbering system for property numbers of PPE and
require the Property Unit to update the property stickers based on the
prescribed format and to include the required information under Section
5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

1.15 We further reiterated the prior year’s recommendation and the Municipal
Mayor agreed to relieve the designated members of the Inventory Committee
from their regular duties during the conduct of the inventory.

2.11 We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to:

a. Prepare and submit the BRS within 10 days from receipt of the bank
statements as required under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996, so that the validity and existence of the Cash in
Bank account could be ascertained; and
b. Ensure that the balances of Cash in Bank in the BRS reconcile with that in
the financial statements.

2.12 We further reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to
assign additional personnel to the MAO to reconcile the bank records with the
books of accounts to facilitate the preparation of the monthly BRS.

3.12 We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Ensure that obligations incurred are properly recorded in the books of the
Municipality as soon as the goods are accepted or services rendered and
the corresponding bills are received from the contractor or supplier;
b. Require that obligations shall be covered with duly approved DVs
supported with the required documents such as the Inspection and
Acceptance Report and bills as received as the basis for recording of the
payables in the books;
c. Establish a cut-off period for the submission of DVs and require the
different department heads to properly comply; and
d. Ensure that appropriate taxes are withheld upon recognition of obligations
to suppliers and contractors so that year-end obligations recorded to BIR
are fairly presented in the financial statements.

4.12 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that:

a) The Municipal Assessor furnish the MTO with a copy of the Assessment Roll
as the basis for the collection of real property taxes and for updating the
RPTAR/ Index Card;
b) The Municipal Treasurer’s Office assign a personnel in charge of the regular
updating of the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card and maintain a list showing
the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to
be furnished to the MAO;
c) The Municipal Accountant set up tax receivables and deferred tax income
based on the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible in
the current year submitted by the Municipal Treasurer;
d) The Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for accounts RPT
Receivable and SET Receivable in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume II; and
e) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference
between the RPT and SET Receivables account balances totaling
₱1,281,110.52 and henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation, together with
the Municipal Assessor, of the total collectibles from the Basic and Special
Education Tax with the balances of the RPT Receivables and SET Receivables
accounts to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences.

5.9 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to analyze and conduct
a thorough review of the dormant accounts payable, retrieve pertinent supporting
documents, and check/validate the existence of actual claimants by sending
confirmations prior to the reversion of these outstanding payables; if the transactions
cannot be substantiated with necessary documents and the payable accounts are
found to be non-existing due to the absence of identified valid claimants, recommend
for reversion to Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund all those which
remained outstanding for two years or more and for which no actual claims,
administrative or judicial, have been filed or which are not covered by perfected
contracts on record in accordance with Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445 and DBM-COA
Joint Circular No. 99-06 dated November 13, 1999, so that these may be made
available to finance other developmental projects and activities.

6.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting journal entries to rectify the misstatements in the affected
accounts by:

a) derecognizing the amount of those parts which were replaced;
b) and recognizing the cost of the repairs and maintenance through the
proposed entry below in compliance with IPSAS 17:
Dr. Other Structures ------------------------------------------₱497,646.12
Cr. Accumulated Surplus ------------------------------------₱497,646.12

6.8 We further recommended and Management agreed that when preparing the
Annual Budget, the Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Engineer
carefully determine whether the identified project, program, or activity falls
under repairs and maintenance as part of maintenance and other operating
expenses or if they should be considered as capital expenditures.

7.26 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

a. Record advance payments for 15 per cent mobilization fee under the account
Advances to Contractors and ensure that withholding taxes are in accordance
with the provisions Section 4 of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 12-2001 dated
September 7, 2001;
b. Initially record the costs incurred for ongoing construction or development
in the CIP account and reclassify the same in the appropriate PPE account
upon its completion;
c. Deduct the 10 per cent retention fee from each progress payment for
infrastructure projects up to 50 per cent of work completion, if the project is
within schedule, otherwise, should be continuously imposed; and
d. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the previously recorded
accounts payable related to liquidated damages and credit the same
amounts to its related PPE account.

8.14 We recommended and Management agreed to evaluate and strengthen/formulate
internal control procedures, specifically, those related to the receipt, recording, and
issuance of accountable forms guided by the handbook on ICSPPS, to minimize the
risk of unauthorized use of funds or loss to the government.

8.15 We further recommended and Management agreed to strictly prohibit
designating collecting officers with job order status to collect and require that
personnel handling funds and assets be sufficiently bonded.

9.7 We recommended and the Municipal DRRM Officer agreed to immediately
cease utilizing LDRRMF for purposes that are not eligible under the fund's
guidelines.

9.8 We further recommended and Management agreed to take the necessary steps
to refund the amount of ₱44,990.00, which represents a portion of the LDRRMF
appropriations that was spent on an expense not eligible for charging to the
fund. The refund should be made from the appropriations of the GF Proper to
rectify the improper utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to implement and follow the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute and recognize the depreciation expenses for the infrastructure assets presented in Table 2.

Also, we recommended the Supply Officer to:
prepare the RCPPE with complete details such as the number of units actually on hand, condition whether serviceable or not and whereabouts of the property; and

 maintain and update the PCs on a regular basis.

Lastly, we recommended that the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant:

 Undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the PCs maintained by the Property Custodian and the PPELCs maintained by the Accounting Office are reconciled, and

Prepare the necessary report on the results of the reconciliation to the Municipal Mayor for his appropriate action on non- existing/ missing properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Engineer, MPDC and other concerned personnel to ensure that the PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned, as well as procurement-and-implementation-ready such that factors that may impede implementation shall be considered.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, MPDC and Municipal Engineer fast-track and monitor the implementation of the PPAs funded out of the 20% Development Fund both for current and prior years’ balances to achieve the purpose of the fund.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor through the LDRRM Council and Municipal Budget Officer, to stop charging non disaster related expenses to the LDRRM Funds.

We also recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit a distribution list, including quantification and identification of the items distributed to and received by the identified beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Public Employment Service Officer- Designate to hasten the implementation of the Diversified Livelihood Formation due to Typhoon Odette Program funded by the DOLE RO VIII for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
trace the variance noted in the Due to NGAs year-end balance in the Notes to FS and SLs/GL and make correcting journal entries, if necessary; and
draw journal entries to transfer the Municipality’s 20% share from Fire Code Fees to the Trust Liabilities account.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer expedite the preparation of the final fund utilization reports of all implemented and completed PPAs funded by National Government Agencies. It is also recommended to promptly return the unexpended balances to extinguish the Municipality’s liability in the books of accounts, and put the funds to use for other equally important projects of the Source Agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and the claimants concerned secure Certification from the Secretary of the DOH that the place of assignment/travel is a strife-torn/embattled/disease-infested/distress or isolated areas/stations, or areas declared under state of calamity or emergency, or with volcano activity or eruption.

Also, we recommended that the Public Health Workers refund the overpayment of health benefits they received.

We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the Certification by the Secretary of the DOH as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2023 and strictly observe the guidelines on the grant of hazard pay, subsistence and laundry allowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
require the deputy collectors to remit all collections daily and intact to the Liquidating Officer or at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00, and
issue a written authority deputizing the Barangay Treasurers to collect Community Tax on her behalf.  In the absence of said authority, require the collectors to immediately turnover collections and accountable forms to the Municipal Treasurer.  Henceforth, no accountable forms shall be issued to Barangay Treasurers without a written authority as deputy collectors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:

 Stop the payment of confidential expenses thru reimbursement. The release and utilization of the funds shall be thru a cash advance by a Special Disbursing Officer or the Head of the Agency, and

Comply with the aforementioned reporting requirements, upon request and grant of cash advance and its liquidation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by speeding up the implementation of the prioritized programs, projects and activities as per Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and that closer coordination among officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to achieve the desired level of outcomes.

Instruct the Local Development Council to immediately look into the unimplemented developmental projects as embodied in the APP and develop feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement those projects.



Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very prudent, meticulous, conscientious and precise in evaluating the timely and perfect implementation of each and every project.  Likewise, conduct regular inspection of projects together with the Project Monitoring Committee so that accomplishment is monitored and evaluated based on the project implementation schedule as stipulated in the APP.

Ensure that priority socio-economic and development programs and projects are executed within the fiscal year to facilitate delivery of public services at the soonest possible time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize Accounts Payable that are consummated transactions with complete documentation to prove that these are valid liabilities.  

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the stale check and replacement check may be issued upon submission of the stale check; 

practice the sending of written notice to the payee at least one month before the check becomes stale; and 

coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan revisit the Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance.  Consider to increase the monthly rental rates that is reasonable and equitable at the present time for both the Municipality and the Lessees. 

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

diligently collect the rentals and the surcharges from the Lessees; 

send demand letters to Lessees with delinquent accounts; and 

furnish the Municipal Accountant the Report of Delinquency of Tenants. 


We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of accounts the receivables on the rentals from the Market Stall and Government Lots, based on the report forwarded by the Municipal Treasurer and the corresponding income earned therefrom.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

 To increase the amount of fidelity bond of the Cashier sufficient to cover her accountability. 

Stop the practice of the grant of additional cash advance to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.

We commended the officials of the Municipality of San Juan for ensuring that the expenditures charged under the 20% Development Fund were in compliance with the provision of Item No. 3.2.5 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, dated November 4, 2020. 

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials to maintain and sustain the said positive practice in complying with prescribed rules and regulations.

observations.reason: The number of JO personnel working in the Municipality as of December 31, 2023 decreased by 74%, from 140 in CY 2022 to 37 in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC to submit written justification why award of contract was made to Contractor A and Contractor B despite of delays in its on-going contracts as disclosed in the Statement of All On-going Government and Private Contracts Including Contracts Awarded but Not Yet Started. 

We also recommend to the BAC to submit written justification why a recommendation for an award of contract was made despite the misrepresentation of data in bidding documents and the omission of data in the Statement of On-going Contracts Including Contracts Awarded but Not yet Started. 

Lastly, in future procurements of infrastructure projects, we recommend to the BAC to thoroughly scrutinize the post-qualification of the bidding documents and to require, where necessary, documents to comprehensively undertake the post-qualification of bidders.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Nurse/designated personnel by the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to:

prepare and maintain an accurate report on the Summary/List of vaccines received and distributed; 

ensure proper monitoring on the movement of COVID-19 vaccines thru reconciliation with Provincial Health Services Office (PHSO) as to the the correct number of vaccines received by the RHU based on complete supporting documents; and 
coordinate with the PHSO as to the scheduling of the retrieval of expired vaccines from the RHU. 

MHO to properly plan the utilization and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines, including nearly expiring vaccines, to minimize or prevent wastages thereof.

In order to present reliable, accurate balance for the inventory accounts in the Financial Statements, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor calls the attention of Municipal Accountant to maintain the complete Supplies Ledger CARD (SLC) for each inventory item to monitor the perpetual acquisition and utilization thereof.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert effort to enforce immediate settlement of the audit disallowances and charges.  Likewise, ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular 2009-006 that the settlement of disallowances and charges is made within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Audit disallowances were already settled. The SASDC reflects a zero balance for disallowances as of December 31, 2023.

observations.reason: Audit disallowances were already settled. The SASDC reflects a zero balance for disallowances as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the unutilized balances to the Bureau of Treasury or return the unutilized funds to the source agencies to finally end any obligation created by these transfers and to fully liquidate these accounts.  

In addition, we recommended the Management consider Section 2.9 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2 dated December 4, 2020 on the utilization of unexpended cash balances of public funds held in trust by LGUs for purposes that have been completed or abandoned may be transferred by the LGUs concerned to the respective general funds which could be made available for appropriation to support local government programs and projects in response to the Covid-19 pandemic.
observations.managementAction: Management remitted unutilized funds received from NGAs.
observations.reason: Unutilized fund which remained unremitted amounted to ₱2,479,148.59.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the LDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the unexpended balance of the previous LDRRMF, both the continuing appropriation and special trust fund, to ensure the most possible available funds for disaster risk reduction. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to revert back to the General Fund the LDRRMF which remained unutilized for five years which shall be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: Management incorporated the current appropriations in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.reason: Balances of the previous LDRRMF, both continuing appropriation were not included in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Local Disaster Risk Management Fund Management Officer to fully implement the planned programs, activities and projects for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery so that the MDRRM Fund would be utilized and the intended benefits would be fully achieved.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
Efforts exerted by the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the LDRRMO and other stakeholders in the DRR Management activities of the Municipality during the year.
observations.reason: With remaining balance unsettled as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1. The Inventory Team to finish/complete the conduct of the annual physical inventory of PPE by type and submit a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in the prescribed format not later than January 31 of each year. Reconcile the RPCPPE with the ledger balances. 2. Require the Supply Officer to prepare a separate Inventory Report on obsolete, unserviceable/damaged Report (WMR-Appendix 68) and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IRRUP-Appendix 67) of the GAM for LGUs. 3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to drop from the books of disposed items using the original copy of the WMR and IRRUP when properly authorized as basis for adjusting the books. 4. Require the Inventory Team to assign property number to each PPE Inventory to facilitate validation and ascertain existence; 5. Require the Supply Officer to update property cards at the time of delivery and reconcile with the ledgers cards of the Municipal Accounting Office. 6. Require the Supply Officer to determine to whom the missing properties were issued. He shall then issue a demand for the production, otherwise the accountable officer should be asked to apply for relief of accountability following prescribed procedures/requirements. 7. Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify PPE items amounting to P574,783.56 in accordance with the PPSAS Chart of Accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipal Accountant Implemented not submitted the notice of loss, reconciliation, RPCPPE.
observations.reason: The Supply Officer did not submit the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the conduct of a thorough and complete physical inventory and invite a representative from the Audit Team to witness the activity and reconcile the results of the count with the property and accounting records to ascertain the accuracy, completeness, and existence of the recorded PPE accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the understatement of the appropriate/ related payable and PPE accounts in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements:
Infrastructure assets/Other PPE accounts             15,683,290.35
CIP-infrastructure assets/Other CIP accounts      15,587,373.58
Accounts Payable			       31,270,663.93
Likewise, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the Statement of Work Accomplished of unbilled on-going and completed projects at financial statement preparation as basis for its recording in the book of accounts to ensure fair presentation.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify and request first from the Municipal Agriculturist the list of recipients to confirm actual distribution of procured goods that are intended for distribution prior to its recognition as expense. Also, we recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to restate the affected 2022 accounts by recording the following adjusting journal entry to recognize the undistributed inventories as at December 31, 2022:
Agricultural and Marine Supplies for Distribution      27,469,900.00
Prior Period Adjustment				        27,469,900.00

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Inventory and Appraisal Committee to conduct inventory of all unserviceable properties, after which, prepare the IIRUP and submit a copy to COA for its inspection and for the immediate disposition of the unserviceable properties, thus provide the Management additional income from the proceeds of the saleable unserviceable properties and from the use of the space where these were stored, also, the Supply Officer/Property Custodian will be relieved from unnecessary accountability.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to respectively implement the one-time cleansing of accounts prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and derecognize small value properties which are below the capitalization threshold in order not to overstate the PPE and equity accounts and to ascertain the fair presentation of the same in the FS.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts and prepare the necessary adjustments so that the RPT Receivable and the SET Receivable are of the same amount which is also equal to the respective corollary accounts Deferred RPT Income and Deferred SET Income.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Determine the unclaimed retention money for its possible payment and coordinate with the Acting Municipal Engineer for the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance for these projects which will serve as their basis in the preparation of the Disbursement Voucher even without request for payment from the contractors/supplier; and
Make a compilation of any record/documentation and prepare the corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long outstanding liabilities to support the possible reversion in order not to overstate the liability accounts.  Likewise, Subsidiary ledgers and all available records to support the amount should be attached to the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) that has to be prepared in case of reversion to the unappropriated surplus. If there may be claims in the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in requesting for a supplemental budget for the settlement of the payable.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor and officials concerned to prioritize the transfer of ownership over the nine parcels of land acquired to avoid exposing the same to possible third party claims, land disputes and interests/penalties/surcharges for late registration.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Head of Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies held for distribution or consumption and ensure prompt submission of duly and completely accomplished Report on Physical Count of Inventories not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively as provided in Section 124, Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs in order to ascertain the condition and completeness of the inventory accounts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert to General Fund the unspent balance of the collections from bidding documents.  Also, we recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC to consider the correct allowable honoraria for awarded projects to avoid over/under payments of their honoraria.

We recommended the LCE instruct the BAC to carefully examine the procurements of goods or services of the Municipality in order to consolidate those which are similar in nature to avoid splitting of contracts which is in consonance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and to ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government is obtained.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat, as a support staff of the BAC, see to it that the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed are posted in the PhilGEPS website within the prescribed period of 3 and 15 calendar days in order to ensure procurement transparency.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the unutilized balances to the Bureau of Treasury or return the unutilized funds to the source agencies to finally end any obligation created by these transfers and to fully liquidate these accounts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct Supply Officer or the official responsible in accepting deliveries to furnish a notice to the Office of the Auditor on the deliveries of procured items within 24 hours from the acceptance to comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 96-010. If personal submission of said notice is not possible, the duly signed scanned copy of the notice could be submitted thru the official email address of the Audit Team (r108agb@yahoo.com) to enable the latter to conduct inspection of procured items to determine whether or not such deliveries conformed with the quantity, quality and specifications in the Purchase Order.  Thus, in case of any discrepancies, Management be immediately informed for its appropriate and immediate action. Likewise, it is encouraged that Management attach pictures of the items delivered in the Disbursement Voucher.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Auditor list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year. Also, provide a notice to the Office of the Auditor within ten days, after the award of infrastructure projects or before its start that the project signboard was already posted. If personal submission of said list or notice is not possible, the duly signed scanned copy of the list or notice could be submitted to the official email address of the Audit Team (r108agb@yahoo.com). Thus, enable the Auditor to conduct timely verification and monitoring of the implemented infrastructure projects and deficiencies, if any, would be communicated to Management for its immediate and appropriate action.  More so, the constituents are also informed of such public concern, hence transparency for good governance is ensured.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of P.D. No. 1445 in order to substantiate the legality, validity and propriety of transactions and in order to avoid the issuance of NS. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission of the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers and lacking supporting documents for audit purposes, thus legality, validity and propriety of the transactions would be ascertained.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to prioritize the implementation of the projects funded out of the share of the Municipality from the tobacco excise taxes through maximum use of all available resources so that benefits will be timely delivered to the tobacco farmers/constituents. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE propose to the Sanggunian the re-appropriation of the savings from implemented/ completed projects from previous years thus, maximize implementation and utilization of the funds for the benefit of the tobacco farmers.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to implement the PPAs which remained unutilized from the continuing appropriations. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE, propose to the Sanggunian for the reversion of PPAs that are no longer among the priority programs of the Municipality or no obligations arising therefrom exist and/ or savings from completed projects to the surplus of the general fund at the end of the fiscal year as provided in Section 322 of R.A. No. 7160, thus, maximize implementation and utilization of the funds for the PPAs to uplift the economic and social well-being of the constituents.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of real property taxes and issue notice of delinquency as required in R.A. No. 7160 to all delinquent taxpayers for them to settle all their taxes due to augment income of the Municipality that could be used to finance priority programs and projects.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to distribute the inventories on hand in order for the intended beneficiaries/recipients to enjoy the purpose for which these goods/items were purchased and to avoid its possible expiration, unserviceability and/or deterioration.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the LDRRMO to incorporate in the 2023 LDRRMFIP the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for CYs 2021 to 2017 amounting to ₱5,458,969.28 to ensure the most possible available funds for disaster risk reduction and to return to General Fund the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for CY 2016 amounting to ₱1,553,746.85 which could be appropriated for other social services.

1. Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Supply Officer, to
annually conduct the complete the physical count of all properties, the
preparation of the RPCPPE, and reconcile the result of the inventory with the
balances recorded by the Municipal Accountant to ensure the existence, completeness and accuracy recorded in the PPE accounts and
reported in the financial statement; and
2. Supply Officer to maintain the PC which should be reconciled with the
PPELC maintained by the Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to recognize Accounts Payable that are consummated transactions with
complete documentation to prove that these are valid liabilities.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to:
a. send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the stale check and
replacement check may be issued upon submission of the stale check;
b. practice the sending of written notice to the payee at least one month
before the check becomes stale; and
c. coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the necessary adjustment/s in
the books of accounts, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan revisit
the Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance. Consider to increase the monthly rental
rates that is reasonable and equitable at the present time for both the
Municipality and the Lessees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to:
a. diligently collect the rentals and the surcharges from the Lessees;
b send demand letters to Lessees with delinquent accounts; and
c. furnish the Municipal Accountant the Report of Delinquency of Tenants.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of
accounts the receivables on the rentals from the Market Stall and Government
Lots, based on the report forwarded by the Municipal Treasurer and the
corresponding income earned therefrom.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. To increase the amount of fidelity bond of the Cashier sufficient to cover her
accountability.
b. Stop the practice of the grant of additional cash advance to any official or
employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or
a proper accounting thereof is made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMO to fast track
the implementation of the approved DRRM PPAs to ensure full utilization of
funds and implementation of PPAs as envisioned in the LDRRM Plan and AIP.

Also, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to
stop processing and charging expenditures that are not allowed under
prescribed guidelines in the utilization of the disaster fund.

To ensure that the 20% DF is optimally utilized and monitored, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal
Development Council, require the:
1. The Municipal Budget Officer to render a periodic update to the Municipal Mayor
on the SAAOB of the 20% DF;
2. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure the
implementation of PPAs during the year and recommend to the Municipal
Development Council the re-alignment of appropriations of PPAs Continuing
Appropriation no longer identified as priority development PPAs;
3. The Municipal Engineer and other implementing offices concerned, to monitor the
implementation of PPAs of the 20% DF in order to achieve the objectives of the
fund; and
4. Municipal Development Council, to ensure that the development projects to be
funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned, as well as procurement-and-
implementation-ready in order to achieve the objectives of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials to
maintain and sustain the said positive practice in complying with prescribed rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Create clear-cut policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and
propriety of hiring Casual/JOs taking into consideration the existing regular
employees;
b) Limit the hiring of personnel under Casual/Job Order contracts to those extremely
necessary and deploy only to offices that actually are in need of their services;
c) Require Job Order employees to submit primary personal identification, undergo
interview and the HRMO to evaluate the performance of each JO to mitigate the
risk of hiring unqualified personnel; and
d) Incorporate in the contract the number of man hours, quantity, and duration of the
work to be performed by the JO personnel and require them to submit quantified
accomplishments reports to support payments of wages.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
1. Initiate the commencement of the PPAs funded by NGAs that are due for
implementation to attain the purpose of the fund;
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to effectively enforce the liquidation of
fund transfers once PPAs are completed including the remittance of the
unspent balance to the National Treasury; and
3. The Municipal Treasurer to remit the outstanding national collections to
Bureau of Treasury.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC to submit written
justification why award of contract was made to Contractor A and Contractor B despite
of delays in its on-going contracts as disclosed in the Statement of All On-going
Government and Private Contracts Including Contracts Awarded but Not Yet Started.

We also recommend to the BAC to submit written justification why a
recommendation for an award of contract was made despite the
misrepresentation of data in bidding documents and the omission of data in the
Statement of On-going Contracts Including Contracts Awarded but not yet
Started.

Lastly, in future procurements of infrastructure projects, we recommend to the
BAC to thoroughly scrutinize the post-qualification of the bidding documents and to
require, where necessary, documents to comprehensively undertake the post-
qualification of bidders.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Nurse/designated personnel by the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to:
1. prepare and maintain an accurate report on the Summary/List of vaccines
received and distributed;
2. ensure proper monitoring on the movement of COVID-19 vaccines thru
reconciliation with Provincial Health Services Office (PHSO) as to the the
correct number of vaccines received by the RHU based on complete
supporting documents; and
3. coordinate with the PHSO as to the scheduling of the retrieval of expired
vaccines from the RHU.
b. MHO to properly plan the utilization and distribution of COVID-19
vaccines, including nearly expiring vaccines, to minimize or prevent
wastages thereof.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor and officials concerned to prioritize the transfer of ownership over these parcels of land acquired by the Municipality to avoid exposing the same to possible third party claims and land disputes.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The ownership of the 15 parcels of land was already transferred in the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjusting/correcting entries to bring the correct balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconciled the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD Funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to fully attain the objective for which the funds were provided.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented all the programs and projects under the GAD funds.
