observations.recommendation:
In order to present the correct balance of Cash in Bank account as of December 31,2021, the Municipal Mayor prompted the:
The Municipal Treasurer: 1. to prepare a List of Unreleased Checks to be attached to the Report of Checks Issued and submit to the Municipal Accountant and to COA Auditor; 2. should restore unreleased checks by preparing a journal entry voucher at the end of the year based on the list submitted by the MT; 3. draw another entry at the start of the ensuing year to reverse the previous entry made and recognize the availability of the checks for release.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
The Municipal Treasurer prepared the List of Unreleased Checks and submitted the list to the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Accountant prepared Journal Entry Voucher to restore unreleased checks and reverse the entries at the start of the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
In order to present reliable, accurate balance for the inventory accounts in the Financial Statements, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor calls the attention of:
1. Supply Officer to: a) submit the SSMI together with the corresponding RIS to the Municipal Accountant to be used as basis in preparing JEV to the record in the books of accounts the issuance of inventory items; b) trace all the pertinent documents pertaining to prior year’s transactions and make necessary report in order for the Accountant adjust and present the accurate balances of the affected Inventory accounts in the financial statements.
2. Municipal Accountant to: a) coordinate with the Supply Officer in facilitating the necessary retrieval and/or reconstruction of records and reports pertinent to unrecorded issuances of inventory items; b) effect the recording of adjustment in the books by preparing JEV based on the SSMI’s prepared/submitted by the Supply Officer; and c) maintain the complete Supplies Ledger CARD (SLC) for each inventory item to monitor the perpetual acquisition and utilization thereof.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Supply Officer did not maintain the complete SLC for each inventory item.
The Supply Office did not maintain the required reports and the Municipal Accountant is to adjust inventory balances upon receipt.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to correctly record in the due NGAS account under Trust Fund, the funds received from the Bureau of Treasury. Another JEV shall be prepared to transfer the cash donations from General Fund to Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert effort to enforce immediate settlement of the audit disallowances. Likewise, ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular 2009-006 that the settlement of disallowances and charges is made within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to coordinate with the SAs to provide MOA or its equivalent in order to provide the pertinent information as to the utilization of the fund, and to require the Municipal Accountant to initiate preparation of final Fund Utilization Reports and refund unutilized balances to the SAs.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: In order to established the regularity of the grant of the AHDP and SRA, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents for the proper disposition in audit of the disbursements. Henceforth, only those disbursement vouchers and payrolls with complete supporting documents should be processed for payment.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert effort to enforce immediate settlement of the audit disallowances and charges. Likewise, ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular 2009-006 that the settlement of disallowances and charges is made within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the LDRRM Office to keep up the implementation of DRRM PPAs in the subsequent years to ensure full utilization of funds and implementation of PPAs as envisioned in the LDRRM and AIP.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Per validation of implementation of PPAs, only 58% were implemented.
observations.reason: Not Per validation of CY 2022 PPAs, only 16 out of 29 were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly abide to the rules and regulations as required by the 2019 Revised IRR of RA 9184. We recommended that the members of the BAC ensure that winning bidders submit complete documentary requirements prior to entering into contract. We recommended that the BAC justify the noted lapses. Henceforth comply with the rules and regulations as required by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management noted and we will comply with the rules and regulations as required. The Management has exerted its efforts to look for a qualified observers in order to comply on the rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government, through the BAC Secretariat comply with the posting requirements on all its procurement.
observations.managementAction: BAC admits there is lapse in our judgement when we comply with the posting requirements on all its procurement. Regarding the issue of expiration of the Philgeps, we admit part of this as the BACs fault, but it is not entirely. At that time, we cannot access the Philgeps account. We still have to go to at their office in manila to solve the issue.

observations.recommendation:
The Management to:

Undertake assessment and evaluation of the viability of the operation of these economic enterprises and decide whether to establish separate books of accounts for each LEE, or maintain special accounts only for every LEE in the GF proper; and

Cause the in-house development of the existing accounting system to incorporate the revision for the accounts pertaining to the respective LEEs.
observations.managementAction:
The City opted to just continue maintaining a special accounts in the GF proper for the City’s LEEs financial transactions due to issue in the sustainability in the operation of the City’s economic enterprises.

Considering that the City defer the establishment of separate books of accounts for the LEEs, the in-house development of the accounting system for the subject economic enterprise is not feasible at this period.
observations.reason:
Considerable delay in the conduct of assessment and evaluation of the viability of the City’s economic enterprises.




Assessment and evaluation as to the viability of the LEEs still needs to be undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
The City Accountant to:

Cause the necessary correction for the abovementioned error in the GAAMS as well as the discrepancy amounting to P13,591 on the beginning balance of the SL under the SEF.
observations.managementAction: The beginning balance of P13,591 in the SEF was found to be a system error. Hence, the CAD had requested the Information and Communication Technology Office to rectify the same.
observations.reason: The CAD is not authorized and not in a position to rectify the noted error.

observations.recommendation: The Management to instruct the Sangguniang Panlungsod to facilitate amendment of the Revenue Code to include the commencement and termination of the implementation and imposition of the SHT.
observations.managementAction: The City Legal Officer explained that the amendment of the CSJRC to include the termination of the imposition of the SHT was no longer coordinated to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for amendment since the City Government has an on-going socialized housing projects with the NHA. Further averred that once the program was fully accomplished, the collection of the SHT by the City will automatically ceased.
observations.reason: Lack of appropriate actions by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Property Custodian and Municipal Accountant to prioritize the reconciliation of the results of the physical count of the property and the accounting records. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE, after which prepare a duly and completely accomplished RPCPPE using the prescribed format to facilitate reconciliation of the same with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The records of the Inventory Committee and the municipal accounts were being reconciled. The Inventory Committee conducted the physical inventory of PPE and submitted the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: The submitted RPCPPE does not reconcile with the balance of PPE as appearing in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate all records that could be retrieved and prepare the corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long outstanding liabilities to support the possible reversion of the accounts payable to unappropriated surplus in order not to overstate the liability accounts. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to attach the compiled documents including a photocopy of the subsidiary ledgers to the JEV representing the reverted accounts payable so that in the event that there may be claims in the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in requesting for a supplemental budget for the settlement of the payable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and verified the nature of these long outstanding payable accounts for segregation into valid claims from those without documentation and no claimants.
observations.reason: Long outstanding liabilities had increased to ₱9,490,680.90.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor and officials concerned to prioritize the transfer of ownership over these parcels of land acquired by the Municipality to avoid exposing the same to possible third-party claims, land disputes and potential loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The reissuance of the title of acquired lots is on process.
observations.reason: The ownership over the 15 parcels of land was still not in the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Head of the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies held for distribution or consumption and ensure prompt submission of duly and completely accomplished RPCI not later than January 31 of each year as provided in Sections 194, Volume I of GAM in order to ascertain the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the management. No completely accomplished RPCI was submitted on or before January 31 of CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct Supply Officer or the official responsible in accepting deliveries to furnish a notice to the Office of the Auditor on the deliveries of procured items within 24 hours from the acceptance to comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 96-010. If personal submission of said notice is not possible, the duly signed scanned copy of the notice could be submitted thru the official email address of the Audit Team to enable the latter to conduct inspection of procured items to determine whether or not such deliveries conformed with the quantity, quality and specifications in the Purchase Order. Thus, in case of any discrepancies, Management be immediately informed for its appropriate and immediate action. Likewise, it is encouraged that Management attach pictures of the items delivered in the Disbursement Voucher.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Supply Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Various programs/projects under RA 7171 the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to prioritize the implementation of the funded projects out of the share of the Municipality from the tobacco excise taxes through maximum use of all available resources so that benefits will be timely delivered to the tobacco farmers/constituents.
observations.managementAction: programs/projects were implemented during the year.
observations.reason: One hundred projects with a total appropriation of ₱308,642,198.00 were not implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the MPDC to ensure the full implementation of the priority projects which remained unimplemented in 2020 and ensure that the 20% Development Funds be optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic developmental and environmental outcomes of the LGU in accordance with the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: Various programs/projects under the 20% Development Fund were implemented during the year.
observations.reason: Priority programs/projects implemented during the year. ₱5,989,680.93 or 28.16% remained unutilized at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD Funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to fully attain the objective for which the funds were provided.
observations.managementAction: GAD programs were implemented during the year.
observations.reason: GAD programs amounting to ₱1,474,941.00 or 26.87% remained unutilized at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the current LDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the unexpended balance of the previous LDRRMF, both the continuing appropriation and special trust fund, to ensure the most possible available funds for disaster risk reduction. Likewise, it is recommended that unutilized LDRRMF after five years be reverted back to the General Fund to be used for other social services.
observations.managementAction: Funds from the current appropriation were incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.reason: Examination of the LDRRMFIP revealed that unutilized funds from previous years were not included in the submitted plan and unutilized LDRRMF beyond five years were not reverted back to the General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanism of the Municipality’s CBMS and GAD Databases through preparation of master plan indicating the timelines, process, budget and other necessary actions. Thereafter, start the implementation in CY 2021 in order to ensure updated, operational and reliable information provided by said databases which will serve the Municipal Government in attaining an evidence-based planning, policy-making, and relief distribution in the future.

Additionally, we recommended that Management augment the number of employees responsible for the maintenance and development of the databases, more specifically to update the data contained therein as data gathering and collating mechanism of the GAD and CBMS Databases is certainly a tedious work.
observations.managementAction:
Page 44 of AAR 2020 | The CBMS and GAD Focal Person are still in the process of updating the databases.








Further, there is no request for additional manpower from the responsible department. Hence, there is still multi-tasking among the personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management: 

Limit the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA and ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay which is ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 52 of Republic Act 11620.




Ensure that the required minimum take home pay is attained after all the mandatory and other authorized deductions from the salary of each employee are deducted from the gross salary to guarantee that there is enough amount to sustain the needs of the employee for a month.

See to it that an employee applying for a loan will only avail of the amount that has a monthly amortization of not more than the amount in excess of ₱5,000.00 after deducting all the authorized deductions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality conduct the complete periodic physical count
of its properties and reconcile the results with the property and accounting records.
Also, adhere to the prescribed format or Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated
November 23, 2015 in reporting the results of the physical count of Property, Plant
and Equipment – Local Road Networks.
We further recommended that the General Services Officer maintain the subsidiary
records for each class of property, plant and equipment or the property cards
prescribed in the accounting manual and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November
23, 2015 and reconcile the property cards with RPCPPE and RPCLRN.

We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the guidelines set forth in
Paragraph 29 of IPSAS 1 - Presentation of Financial Statements, of the Handbook of
International Public Sector Accounting Pronouncements, 2020 Edition, Volume I for
the fair presentation of its financial statements and for providing useful information to
the users for accountability purposes and decision making purposes.

We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the provisions of the
Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184; Section 3.15 of Local Budget
Circular No. 122 dated January 31, 2020 of the Department of Budget and
Management (DBM); Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular No. 134, dated January 20,
2021 of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM); Section 6.4.2 of
Memorandum Circular No. 2020-088 dated May 21, 2020 of the Department of the
Interior and Local Government; Special Provisions No. 1 (A) (4) (e) (i) and (ii) of
Republic Act No. 11465 or General Appropriations Act Fiscal Year 2020 – Assistance
to Municipalities Volume I – B and Section 28, Chapter 4, Book VI of E.O. No. 292, s.
1987. Likewise, we recommend that the municipal mayor instruct:
a. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with the posting
requirement in the procurement of goods and infrastructures of the
Municipality under the competitive bidding;
b. The Municipal Accountant account funds transfers in accordance with the
guidelines provided by the granting agency particularly Local Budget
Circulars issued by the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) as
well as Memorandum Circular issued by the Department of the Interior and
Local Government; and
c. The Municipal Treasurer return to the Bureau of the Treasury the
undisbursed/unexpended fund transfer pursuant to Section 28, Chapter 4,
Book VI of E.O. No. 292, s. 1987.

We recommended that the Municipality always adhere to the provisions of Sections
4(3) and 4(8) of P.D. No. 1445 that trust fund be utilized for the specific purpose it was
intended and exercise sound fiscal administration in the local government operations
provided not contravening to existing laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials
ensure the effective utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund which is primarily
intended to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes. Delays in the
completion of development projects definitely had an adverse impact to the
community development or realization of development objectives.
We also recommended that the development programs/projects/activities contained
in the Municipality’s Local Development Investment Program (LDIP) be also those
programs/projects/activities in its Annual Investment Program that shall be the ones
to be budgeted and implemented as defined in Section 3.2 of Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 1 Series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007 of Department of the Interior and
Local Government, National Economic and Development Authority, Department of
Budget and Management and Department of Finance. And the development
projects/activities shall be shown in itemized appropriations in the municipal Annual
Investment Program and municipal Annual Budget and that Annual Investment
Program be filled out according to the existing guidelines to provide an accurate and
useful data.
We further recommended that the Municipality implement changes in its Annual
Budget pursuant to Article 418 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No.
7160.

We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the provisions of Republic
Act No. 10121 and rules and regulations of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012; to wit:
a. Prioritize disaster response and early rehabilitation and recovery when the area
is declared under the state of calamity;
b. Prepare the annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund
Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) according to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Plan (LDRRMP);
c. Prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as prescribed in the
COA Circular No. 2012-002 disclosing all the sources of the fund and their
utilization;
d. Transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of Quick Response Fund and
Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) – Maintenance and
Other Operating Expenses for CY 2021 totaling P4,190,178.94 to the Special
Trust Fund in the Trust Fund Books.

We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the approved Municipal Solid
Waste Management Plan covering the period CY 2015 to 2025 in compliance with
declared policy of the State to adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological
solid waste management program and knowing that the LGUs are primarily
responsible for the implementation and enforcement of the provisions of the
Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.

We recommended that the Local School Board, adhere strictly to the provisions of
Section 272 of RA No. 7160; Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG
and the two addenda of the Joint Circular in the appropriation of SEF that the
beneficiaries of the special purpose fund are the elementary and secondary schools
of the District of the Municipality of San Juan.
We further recommended that the copy of School Improvement Plan (SIP) submitted for
audit include the certification of acceptance and approval issued by the Schools
Division Superintendent to show that the document does contain strategic
prioritization policies in the allocation of SEF to the schools.

We recommended that the Municipality stringently observe guidelines provided in
Sections C.1 (5) and C.3 (1) of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, the DBM, and the
NEDA, to effectively plan and implement GAD PPAs of the LGU and that five percent
GAD budget shall endeavor to influence the remaining 95 percent of the LGU budget
toward gender-responsiveness.

1.15 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require:
1. The reconciliation of the balances of the Municipal Accountant’s books
and the RPCPPE;
2. The Municipal Accountant to adjust the recognized PPE not reaching the
P15,000 threshold as inventory upon acquisition and expense upon
issuance;
3. The Inventory Team to follow and fill-in the appropriate data on the
RPCPPE as described in COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
4. The Supply Officer-Designate, Inventory Team and Municipal Accountant
to perform the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts through the
procedures prescribed in the COA Circular 2020-006.

2.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to remit the outstanding national collections to
Bureau of Treasury;
b. The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries
for the retention fees recognized in Due to NGAs as follows:
Due to NGAs xx
Guaranty Deposit Payable xx
c. The Municipal Accountant initiate the request for write-off of the
unsubstantiated balances of the Due to NGAs account pursuant to COA
Circular 2016-006 dated December 19, 2016.

3.5 In order to present the correct balance of Cash in Bank account as of
December 31, 2021, the Municipal Mayor prompted the:
The Municipal Treasurer:
1. to prepare a List of Unreleased Checks to be attached to the Report of
Checks Issued and submit to the Municipal Accountant and to COA
Auditor;
The Municipal Accountant:
1. should restore unreleased checks by preparing a journal entry voucher
at the end of the year based on the list submitted by the MT;
2. draw another entry at the start of the ensuing year to reverse the
previous entry made and recognize the availability of the checks for
release.

4.9 In order to present reliable, accurate balance for the inventory accounts in the
Financial Statements, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor calls the
attention of:
1. Supply Officer to:
a) submit the SSMI together with the corresponding RIS to the
Municipal Accountant to be used as basis in preparing JEV to
record in the books of accounts the issuance of inventory items;
b) trace all the pertinent documents pertaining to prior year’s
transactions and make necessary report in order for the Accountant
to adjust and present the accurate balances of the affected Inventory
accounts in the financial statements.
2. Municipal Accountant to:
a) coordinate with the Supply Officer in facilitating the necessary
retrieval and/or reconstruction of records and reports pertinent to
unrecorded issuance’s of inventory items;
b) effect the recording of adjustment in the books by preparing JEV
based on the SSMI’s prepared/submitted by the Supply Officer; and
c) maintain the complete Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each
inventory item to monitor the perpetual acquisition and utilization
thereof.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare Journal entry Vouchers (JEV) to correctly record in the
due to NGAs account under Trust Fund, the funds received from the Bureau
of Treasury. Another JEV shall be prepared to transfer the cash donations from
General Fund to Trust Fund.

6.14 To ensure that the 20% DF is optimally utilized and monitored, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal
Development Council to require the:
1. Office of the Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate books of
accounts for the 20% DF in accordance with regulations;
2. The Municipal Budget Officer to render a periodic update to the
Municipal Mayor on the SAAOB of the 20% DF and identify excess funds
of completed projects and recommend for its reversion and made
available for re-appropriation;
3. The Municipal Planning and Development Office to ensure the
implementation of PPAs during the year and recommend to the
Municipal Development Council the realignment of appropriations of
PPAs Continuing Appropriation no longer identified as priority
development PPAs;
4. The Municipal Engineer’s Office, and other implementing offices
concerned, to monitor the implementation of PPAs of the 20% DF, in
order to achieve the objectives of the fund;
5. Municipal Development Council, to revisit PPAs charged to Continuing
Appropriations, identify balances to be re-channeled to more priority and
relevant PPAs of the Municipality; and ensure that the development
projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned, as well as
procurement-and implementation-ready.

7.10 We commended the efforts exerted by the Municipal Mayor in coordination
with the LDRRMO and other stakeholders in the DRR Management activities
of the Municipality during the year. We recommend to keep up the
implementation of DRRM PPAs in the subsequent years to ensure full
utilization of funds and implementation of approved PPAs as envisioned in the
LDRRM Plan and AIP.

7.11 Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.

8.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Head, Engineering
Office to:
a. Ensure that all conditions preliminary to the commencement of
construction have been addressed; and
b. Review thoroughly the request for work suspensions by the contractors
based on complete and relevant documents.

9.11 In view of these noted deficiencies, we recommended for the Municipal Mayor
to coordinate with the SAs to provide MOA or its equivalent in order to provide
the pertinent information as to the utilization of the fund, and to require the
Municipal Accountant to initiate preparation of final Fund Utilization Reports
and refund unutilized balances to the SAs.

10.11 In order to establish the regularity of the grant of the AHDP and SRA, we
recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
submit the lacking documents for the proper disposition in audit of the
disbursements. Henceforth, only those disbursement vouchers and payrolls
with complete supporting documents should be processed for payment.

1.8 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
1.8.1 Management create an Inventory Committee (IC) that will perform
the conduct of comprehensive annual physical count of PPE in
accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume
1. Also, a representative of the Audit Team should be invited to
witness the inventory taking for this purpose;
1.8.2 Management ensure that the IC prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) which shall be
reconciled with the PPE records kept by the General Services Office
(GSO) and Accounting Office; and that a copy of the RPCPPE be
submitted to the COA for verification not later than January 31 of
each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS;
1.8.3 The GSO maintain Property Cards for PPE and ARE/PAR and
ensure their renewal every after three years pursuant to Section 492
of GAAM, Volume 1, to establish effective management and control
over PPE;
1.8.4 The Municipal Accountant maintain complete and updated PPELC
for each category of PPE pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual
for LGUs to provide ready information on the accounts and to support
the balances in the financial statements;
1.8.5 The GSO and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their property
records with the results of the physical count to ensure that the PPE
recorded in the property records actually exist and all existing PPEs
are recorded in the books, in order to establish correctness and
completeness of the balances of PPE accounts. Any variance should be
handled accordingly and subsequently adjusted if the need arises;
1.8.6 The Property and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed
procedures in COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of
unserviceable PPEs, if any.

2.8 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
2.8.1 That the Municipal Engineer, General Services Officer, and
Municipal Accountant conduct a yearly physical count of local road
network projects and submit to this office the necessary reports and
consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
2.8.2 That the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and
the General Services Officer the description and the cost segregation
of the road components of various road infrastructure projects;
2.8.3 That the General Services Officer maintain a Local Road Inventory
and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property
Card for all roads and its components;
2.8.4 That the Municipal Accountant keep and maintain a subsidiary ledger
for roads and its components and reconcile the same with the result of
the physical inventory-taking at the end of the year.

3.6 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
3.6.1 The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer, and the Municipal
Treasurer (in her capacity as General Service Officer) determine the
breakdown of the Road Network to establish and compute for the
depreciation;
3.6.2 Determine the road lot component of the road network and exclude it
from the computation of the depreciation, in accordance with Item
IV.4 of COA Circular No. 2015-08;
3.6.3 Trace and determine the date of acquisition of the various assets
under the Road Network in order to compute for the accumulated
depreciation; and
3.6.4 Further, the computed prior years’ depreciation be taken up as Prior
Year Adjustments to be closed to the Government Equity.

4.6 In view of the noted lapses, we recommend that Management -
4.6.1 Submit the Certified List of All Real Property Tax Delinquencies and
the List of Tax Delinquent for more than five years, if any, and
reconcile the same with the accounting records; and
4.6.2 Formulate additional strategies to intensify and facilitate tax
collections. If need be, avail of remedies embodied in Section 174 of
R.A. No. 7160 to ensure collection of taxes due the municipal
government in order to increase the collection efficiency.

5.9 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended that Management:
5.9.1 Strictly comply with the guidelines set by the CSC in accomplishing
the DTRs of the Municipality employees;
5.9.2 Require the Human Resource Management Office to update and
reconcile the individual personnel Leave Cards and deduct from the
leave credit balances the corresponding tardiness and undertime
incurred; otherwise, the overpayment caused by the non-deduction
will be disallowed in audit;
5.9.3 Submit the updated personnel Leave Cards for audit, and henceforth
observe its proper submission; and
5.9.4 Monitor the tardiness and undertime of the employees and consider
the provisions of the Revised Rules on Administrative Cases in the
Civil Service in the imposition of sanctions, if warranted, to protect
the interest of the government.

7.19 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that:
7.19.1 Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to submit the above stated lacking documents for the
audit team to evaluate and establish the validity of the
disbursements; and
7.19.2 Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer effect payments only on
vouchers that are properly documented and instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and attach checklist of documentary
requirements for every transaction/DV and ensure that all
required documents were submitted.

8.7 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
8.7.1 Discontinue the procurement of gasoline, oil and lubricants through
reimbursement basis;
8.7.2 Obtain quotations from at least three bon fide suppliers. The
Municipality may opt to issue Purchase Order for fuel consumptions
good for one (1) month but payment should be only for actual
withdrawals or pay only what is consumed within a given period;
8.7.3 Include the procurement of gasoline, oil and lubricants in the
Municipality’s Annual Procurement Plan to ensure economy and
efficiency in its procurement process;
8.7.4 Prepare Monthly Fuel Consumption Report and Monthly Report on
Official Travel, to be supported with a duly accomplished Driver’s
Trip Tickets; and
8.7.5 Henceforth follow strictly the provisions and existing rules and
regulations of the Revised IRR of the R.A. No. 9184.

9.9 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer, henceforth, ensure the submission of accounts and other
financial and mandatory reports within the prescribed periods pursuant to
Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160, Section 70 of NGAS for LGUs, Item
4 of COA Circular No. 2010-01 and Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-
006 to avoid audit suspensions.

10.4 We have recommended that all perfected contracts and purchase orders be
submitted to COA within five days after perfection thereof for the
immediate review of the reasonableness of the cost, conduct of inspection
and other necessary audit procedures.

11.8 We have recommended that Management submit and/or attach copies of
the approved APP and its supporting PPMPs to every transaction involving
procurement for audit purposes. Henceforth, prepare APP and PPMPs to
serve as guide in the procurement activities of the local government unit.

11.13 We have recommended that Management submit copies of ABC, if any, for
contracts involving the procurement of goods, and henceforth, prepare and
compute the ABC for every procurement intended to be bid.

11.17 We have recommended that BAC attach all bids received, other than the
winning bid, to its corresponding contract documents to ensure
transparency in the bidding process.

11.21 We have recommended that BAC submit the lacking statement for
contracts involving the procurement of goods, and, henceforth, ensure that
all bidders submit their statement of conformity to technical specifications
as assurance that the quality and specifications specified in the contract will
be followed.

12.5 We have recommended that BAC thoroughly review and evaluate the
submitted technical and eligibility documents of contractors to ensure that
contracts are awarded to the most qualified bidder.

13.5 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that Management abide
with the provision of Section 322 of the Local Government Code for the
unexpended balance of previous years appropriations.

17.5 In view of the noted deficiency, we have recommended that Management
abide with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-59 and
DBM-DSWD Circular No. 2003-01, and ensure the proper allocation of the
required funds for the Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities in order
to improve the well-being of the elderly and the persons with disabilities.

19.5 We have recommended that Management identify GAD PPAs through the
use of HGDG which would be the basis in the budget determination
depending on its gender responsiveness or attribute to GAD interventions
and follow the format prescribed by the PWC-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No.
2013-001 in the preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget.

21.5 We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer to prepare separate AIPs for the LCPC and SC funds. Henceforth,
require the GAD Focal Point System Chairperson to utilize the HGDG in
the selection of PPAs to be included in the GPB in order to properly address
the gender issues and concerns of the municipality.

We recommended that Management direct the CGSD to immediately undertake the necessary procedures in the disposal of the subject unserviceable properties amounting to P55,712,390.60 pursuant to Section 167 of COA Circular No. 92-386, to avoid further deterioration of the same.

We recommended that Management require the CAD to record only as payables those procured goods and services which were actually delivered and accepted or services rendered and supplier/creditor bills were received as of the cut-off period pursuant to Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require the CHD to immediately implement the project as regards the fund received from the DOH in accordance with the terms and conditions in the MOA, for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries;
Instruct the CDRRMO to immediately coordinate with the PCSO to determine the appropriate action to be undertaken as regards to the unutilized calamity fund; and
Require the CAD to conduct follow-up on the requested copy of MOAs for the proper disposition on the dormant unexpended balances of fund transfers, and to facilitate the return of the 
CYs 2020 and 2021 unutilized balances to the concerned SAs pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that Management require the CENRO to:
Adopt a calibrated catch-up plan to fast-track the implementation and completion of the SSJKP to help and contribute in the formulation of an effective programs towards improving the City’s waste diversion and waterways rehabilitation efforts; and
Ensure strict compliance with the submission of periodic reports on the status of the project as well as with the other terms and conditions of the MOA.

We recommended that Management require the SJMC Medical Director to:
Refrain from approving the grant of medical assistance to eligible beneficiaries beyond the signing authority as prescribed under Item 6, Section VI of DOH AO No. 2020-0060;
Ensure that all claims for medical assistance beyond the P50,000.00 limit of authority by the Medical Director be forwarded to the proper authorities, for approval; and
Henceforth, compliance with the pertinent provisions of existing guidelines in the implementation of MAIP program be strictly observed.

We recommended that Management enjoin the City Accountant and the City Human Resource Development Officer to immediately enforce the collection of these receivables from the money due the concerned officials and employees.  Further, see to it that the appropriate amount of taxes is withheld from the taxable income of all City employees to avoid the occurrence of the same issue in the future.

We recommended that Management require the CAD to:
Submit the lacking documents as well as the original and/or authenticated copies of the supporting documents of the 25 DVs amounting to P141,435,481.34 for audit; and
Ensure that all disbursement transactions are supported with complete, original and authenticated documentations, as applicable, pursuant to P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the LSB of San Juan City to ensure that the SEF is allocated and utilized only for eligible programs/projects as provided under Item 3.0 of DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 1. s. 2017; and
Henceforth, ensure that SEF be allocated and used only to allowable PPAs as mandated under relevant laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:
Submit immediately the lacking documents and the original and/or authenticated copies of the supporting documents for the subject disbursement transactions totaling P37,717,518.29, for audit; and
Henceforth, ensure that all claims against the funds of the City be supported with complete, original and authenticated documentations, as applicable, pursuant to PD No. 1445 and COA Circular 
No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management require the SJCDC:
To ensure the efficient implementation of all PPAs programmed during the year in accordance with the scheduled period of its execution; and
Make representation with the SP to facilitate the approval of the Resolution granting authority to the City Mayor to enter into a Deed of Absolute Sale with the heirs of the registered owner of the land to attain maximum utilization of the 20% DF.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the CDRRMO to timely implement all planned Disaster Risk Reduction Management PPAs to achieve the City’s visions of a safer, adaptive and disaster-resilient community.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to ensure that only gender responsive PPAs that will address the identified emerging and/or continuing gender issues and concerns be included in the GPB pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 
No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Management require the GFPS-TWG to:
Submit the GPB and GAD AR within the required period prescribed in the aforementioned rules and regulations; and
Ensure that the submitted GAD AR was supported with pertinent documents/reports/information as mentioned above.

We recommended that Management appropriate/allocate in the City’s annual budget the 1% of its IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the PPAs of the City’s LCPC pursuant to Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG MC No. 2012-120.

We recommended that Management require the LCPC to ensure that only PPAs allowed to be funded by the 1% IRA for the strengthening of the LCPC be programmed and included in the AIP and in the budget of the City as required under Item II of DILG MC No. 2012-120.

We recommended that Management:
Assign properly trained and dedicated additional personnel to the SJMC PhilHealth Division to help and assist in the processing and encoding of reimbursement claims to ensure the timely submission/filing of the claims to PhilHealth.
The SJMC PhilHealth Division to –
Strictly file the PHIC benefit claims within the 60-day reglementary period and ensure that the same are duly supported with complete and proper documentation;
Immediately act and comply with the requirements of PhilHealth as regards the RTH claims;
Ensure that the services rendered by the SJMC and included in the claims were from accredited doctors and within the benefits allowed to be claimed against PhilHealth; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of the RIRR of NHIA, as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606 on the reimbursement of claims to PhilHealth.

We recommended that Management instruct:
The SJMC to prepare and submit a Schedule of PhilHealth reimbursement claims to the CAD pertaining to all services rendered by the SJMC to member patients covering CYs 2020 and 2021. Henceforth, regularly provide the CAD of copies of all transmitted claims to serve as basis in the recording of receivable and income in the books of accounts; and
The CAD to record in the books the receivable and income from PhilHealth based on the Schedule of PhilHealth reimbursement claims prepared and submitted by the SJMC.

We recommended that the Management –
Direct the Head of the CSWDO to submit the required photos of the 664 beneficiaries during the payout to prove that they actually received the financial assistance; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Sec. 4(6) of 
PD No. 1445 and DSWD MC No. 09 on the documentation needed to support the claims against government funds.

We recommended that Management –
Instruct the City Accountant to promptly remit in full all the withheld taxes to the BIR within 10 days following the close of the calendar month during which the withholding was made as provided in Section 5 of RR No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018, to avoid incurring penalties and surcharges; and
Henceforth, ensure that the taxes withheld are fully remitted within the prescribed period as mandated under pertinent BIR Revenue Regulation.

We recommended that Management:
Require the CAD to:
Immediately remit the amount of P4,395,635.50 representing the premium contributions and loan amortizations due the GSIS; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 8291 in the remittance of premium contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS.
Instruct the CHRDO to coordinate with the GSIS to establish the actual amount of loan amortizations that should be deducted and withheld from the compensations of the debtor-employees.

We recommended that Management require the CAD to:
Facilitate the reconciliation of the observed variance in the balances appearing in the SL, the Sub-accounts and the Breakdown of the Due to GSIS-GFP and Due to GSIS-Hospital Loans; and
Henceforth, ensure that the balances appearing in the SL should always tally with the balances appearing in the Sub-accounts and Breakdown, at any given time.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant exert
effort in preparing and submitting the BRS within the prescribed period to establish
the correctness of the Cash in Bank account.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor assign additional
personnel in the Accounting Office to reconcile the bank records with the books of
accounts to facilitate the preparation of the monthly BRS.

We recommended that the concerned department heads strictly comply with the
accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system
and earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under
COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.

We also recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct an annual
physical count of all its Local Road Networks and the results thereof reported in the
RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting Unit not later than
January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting
records and the necessary adjusting entries be prepared before the statement date.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Inventory Committee conduct a
physical count of movable properties annually, prepare and submit the RPCPPE to
the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year.

We further recommended and Municipal Mayor agreed to relieve the
Property Custodian and the designated members of the Inventory Committee from
their regular duties during the conduct of the physical inventory.

We furthermore recommended that the Property Division and Accounting
Office maintain property cards and ledger cards, respectively, in compliance with
Section 114 of the Manual in NGAS, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Agricultural Officer to conduct an inventory of
breeding stocks and render a report thereon, including the fair value of its
stock, to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books of
accounts and to keep a systematic record of births, deaths and disposal of
breeding stocks. In case of death or disposal, he shall facilitate the request for
relief from accountability, for dropping the cost of the asset from the books; and
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain livestock ledger cards and to
update these monthly using the reports submitted by the Municipal
Agricultural Officer and require, as well, the Property Custodian to maintain
property cards as reference for identifying each animal in the conduct of
physical count as well as for reconciliation with accounting records.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning
Development Officer to adequately and thoroughly plan all projects before their
inclusion in the AIP to ensure that only those that can be implemented immediately
are included.

We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer improve their
monitoring of project implementation by maintaining an updated list of projects
and taking corrective measures for projects lagging far behind schedule.

We furthermore recommended that Management assign additional
personnel in the MEO to hasten the implementation of projects funded out of the
20% DF.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure compliance with
Section 3.5 of GPPB Circular 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020, otherwise, they will be
held answerable for negligence in case of uncorrected defects on the delivered goods.

We recommended and the OIC-LDRRMO agreed to coordinate with the
OCD on the preparation and submission of the Municipality’s PSCP pursuant to
NDRRMC Memorandum No 33, s. 2018.

We further recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the
following activities:
a. To conduct risk assessment;
b. To identify critical processes and functions;
c. To determine scenarios that made disrupt normal operation;
d. To conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;
e. To formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical
processes and functions and its related steps to be followed to
eliminate, if not mitigate, the impact of the determined
disruptions following the prescribed templates and
requirements in the PSCP guidebook; and
f. To submit plan to the MDRRMO for review, to the Local Chief
Executive for approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the
passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the contractors to post
warranty security in the amount provided under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR
of RA 9184 in procuring infrastructure projects. Otherwise, structural defects
and/or failure within the warranty period not covered by the surety bond and not
repaired by the contractor shall be borne by person liable.

We recommended that Management:
a. Conduct a bidding for medicines based on the estimated quantities
and kind of drugs anticipated to be prescribed for a period of three
months or less at the option of management, which shall serve as basis
for the choice of pharmacy with whom the Municipality shall enter
into an agreement for the purchase of medicines under the program;
b. Include in the memorandum of Agreement with the chosen pharmacy
the provision that only claims supported with complete documents are
honored by the pharmacy and to see to it that the recipient signs the
claim stub provided by the MSWD; and
c. Require the Municipal Social Welfare Office to prepare individual
checklists for each type of assistance, to attach a checklist to the
Certificate of Eligibility, and to see to it that the documents required
under DSWD AO No. 1, s. of 2011 are available and attached thereto
before releasing the Certificate of Eligibility to the client.

1.14 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the conduct of a thorough and complete physical inventory and invite the Audit Team or his representative to witness the activity, and reconcile the results of the count with the property and accounting records and consider the one-time cleansing of accounts prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to ascertain the accuracy and fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

2.5 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to make a compilation of any record/documentation retrieved and prepare the corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long outstanding liabilities to support the possible reversion in order not to overstate the liability accounts. Likewise, subsidiary ledgers and all available records to support the amount should be attached to the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) that has to be prepared in case of reversion to the unappropriated surplus. In the event that there may be claims in the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in requesting for a supplemental budget for the settlement of the payable.

3.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor and officials concerned to prioritize the transfer of ownership over these parcels of land acquired by the Municipality to avoid exposing the same to possible third party claims and land disputes.

4.4 We recommended the LCE instruct the Head of Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies held for distribution or consumption and ensure prompt submission of duly and completely accomplished RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively as provided in Section 124, Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs in order to ascertain the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

5.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjusting/correcting entries to bring the correct balances of the accounts.

6.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts and prepare the necessary adjustments so that the RPT Receivable and the SET Receivable are of the same amount which are also equal to the corollary accounts Deferred RPT Income and Deferred SET Income.

7.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 in order to substantiate the legality, validity and propriety of transactions and in order to avoid the issuance of NS. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission of the lacking supporting documents for audit purposes, thus legality, validity and propriety of the transactions would be ascertained.

8.6 We recommended that the LCE instruct the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to prioritize the implementation of all projects funded out of the share of the Municipality from the tobacco excise taxes through maximum use of all available resources so that benefits will be timely delivered to the tobacco farmers/constituents.

9.6 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to ensure the full implementation of the priority projects which remained unimplemented in 2021 and ensure full utilization of the 20% Development Funds in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic developmental and environmental outcomes of the LGU in accordance with the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

10.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD Funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to fully attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

11.4 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the unutilized balances to the Bureau of Treasury or return the unutilized funds to the source agencies to finally end any obligation created by these transfers and to fully liquidate these accounts.

11.5 In addition, we recommended the Management consider Section 2.9 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2 dated December 4, 2020 on the utilization of unexpended cash balances of public funds held in trust by LGUs for purposes that have been completed or abandoned may be transferred by the LGUs concerned to the respective general funds which could be made available for appropriation to support local government programs and projects in response to the Covid-19 pandemic.

12.9 We recommended that the LCE instruct the LDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the unexpended balance of the previous LDRRMF, both the continuing appropriation and special trust fund, to ensure the most possible available funds for disaster risk reduction. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to revert back to the General Fund the LDRRMF which remained unutilized for five years which shall be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Adjust the affected accounts in accordance with applicable provisions of the IPSAS:
Overstatement of the “Road Networks” accounts and corresponding Accumulated Depreciation account under subtopic a:
Dr			         Cr
Prior Period Adjustment		₱106,956,033.38
Road Networks					₱106,956,033.38
Accumulated Dep’n		₱10,160,824.09
Prior Period Adjustment					  ₱10,160,824.09
Overstatement of the Accumulated Depreciation account under subtopic b.2:
Dr			         Cr
Prior Period Adjustment            ₱ 3,985,220.77
Accumulated Dep’n				  ₱ 3,985,220.77
Discontinue the policy of assigning residual value to the LRNs; modify the computation of the monthly depreciation expense by disregarding the residual value in the formula, and by taking into consideration the second paragraph of PAG for IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to:
provide the Municipal Accountant with the List of Delinquent Stallholders with amount due to facilitate timely recording of rental income;
exert effort to collect the unpaid rentals by exhausting available administrative and legal remedies;
Municipal Accountant to:
recognize the total unpaid rentals and its surcharges in the books of the Municipality to the appropriate receivable and income account, in compliance with the provisions of IPSASs 13 for the fair presentation of the financial statements; and
maintain subsidiary ledgers for each lessee who rented the establishments owned by the Municipality for proper monitoring and recording of rental income.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant with coordination/help of Municipal Treasurer to:
exert effort to collect these long outstanding receivables particularly the accounts with available reference documents by sending demand letters. Exhaust all remedies to collect and properly document efforts made; and
exert more effort to locate all related documents pertaining to the long outstanding receivables in order to establish their existence and validity.
For the receivables that are proven to be uncollectible but duly supported with required documents, we recommended that the LCE file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
We further recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize allowance for impairment of receivables in accordance with IPSAS to present fairly the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
review, confirm and verify all information available to establish the validity and existence of these dormant payables;
propose remittance or refund to the concern agency if balances were confirmed or adjust the accounts accordingly if the concern agency has no recognized receivable from the municipality in line with the objective of fair presentation of liabilities in the financial statements; and
maintain complete and reliable subsidiary ledgers and conduct regular monitoring of outstanding liabilities.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Department/ Office Heads to:
Properly plan their PPAs taking into consideration possible contingencies in the future to avoid insertions that were not considered in the AIP; and
For insertions, notify the appropriate offices/ committees for their inclusion/ incorporation in the AIP by crafting a supplemental AIP, or any other means prescribed by the BOM and the Code.
Appropriate offices/ committees, and reviewing bodies to:
Conduct strict and rigorous review of the local budget preparation forms, as to the contents and substance; and
Ensure the consistency of the budgets (annual and supplemental) with the AIPs (annual and supplemental) pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the BOM and the Code, as discussed.
We further recommended that the LCE ensure that utmost diligence and competence is exercised in their dealings and transactions, most importantly in the preparation of documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Various department heads/ requesting office to:
Discontinue the practice of appropriating funds in particular budget years without the intention of implementing the related PPA of the said funds, for the reason that said funds are being accumulated and reserved to fund a particular PPA; and
Evaluate existing continuing appropriations whether these are already qualified to be declared as “Savings”.; and
Municipal Budget Officer to recommend the passing of an appropriation ordinance enacting a supplemental budget and a supplemental AIP, if so warranted, before utilizing the “Savings”.

We recommended that the LCE direct:
All Municipal officials and employees to:
properly utilize Civil Service Form No. 6 (Revised 2020) or the Application for leave form upon availing of leaves and/or monetization of leave duly approved by the department heads. Duplicate copy will be kept in the HRMO file while the triplicate copy will be attached to the DV; and
submit the required documentary requirements if one avails of the monetization of 50% or more of the leave credits pursuant to Section 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave, as amended.
Various department heads to:
screen and evaluate the purpose of every official/employee applying for monetization of earned leave credits, especially in availing monetization of 50% or more pursuant to Section 23 of the above rules before approving such leave; and
limit regular monetization of vacation leaves to ten days or up to 30 days pursuant to the above rule. Application of which can only be deducted from the vacation leave pursuant to Section 22 above;
Human Resource Management Officer – Designate to:
ensure that at least five days is retained after monetization of vacation leave credits in accordance to Section 22 of the Rules; and
strictly observe Section 23 of the Rules and CSC Resolution No. 000034 which provides that sick leave credits shall be monetized only when vacation leave are exhausted.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure that all online disbursements are duly supported with a disbursement voucher which would invariably bear the approval of proper officials and with complete documentation;
Immediately submit to COA the disbursement vouchers, approved ADA, approved Request Letter for Fund Transfers and all other supporting documents for online disbursements made for CY 2021;
Municipal Treasurer to:
Ensure that all fund transfer and online payments are supported by an Authority to Debit Account (ADA) or its equivalent; and
Prepare a Report of ADA Issued to record all the ADA issued daily.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Instruct the designated market stallholder collector to intensify his collecting efficacy;
Periodically provide the designated collector of the list of delinquent stallholders and amount still due and demandable for monitoring and collecting purposes; and
Strictly enforce the terms and conditions stipulated in the Municipal Revenue Code of 2021 and the Contract of Lease agreement relative to the termination of contract, ejectment, revocation of business permit and the garnishment of improvements, equipment and merchandise contained in the stalls rented.
We further recommended that the LCE incorporate in the Contract of Leases with stallholders the surcharge provision of 25% for late payments/non-payments of rentals in accordance with Section 5 of Article S of the Municipal Revenue Code of 2021 so that stallholders will be compelled to pay their obligation promptly.

We recommended that the LCE direct Municipal Planning and Development Council in coordination with the concerned department/office heads to:
Prioritize the implementation of the unimplemented development projects at year-end and strictly observe the guidelines under DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020; and
Include in the AIP and Annual Budget only those projects that are procurement-and-implementation-ready in order to fully optimize the utilization of the 20% DF.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the LCE:
Prioritize the full implementation of the Municipal SWM projects especially the long-awaited construction of Engineered Sanitary Landfill and ensure effective and efficient enforcement of environmental ordinances towards the attainment of the objectives of the LGU’s Ten-Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan;
If gratuitous permit be availed of for special use of forest lands for government agencies, the LGU shall accomplish and complete all the required documents and consequently fast track application with the DENR thereafter, coordinate with the DENR to monitor the issuance of ECC; and
Follow up with the PGLU the implementation of the Engineered Sanitary Landfill to address the environmental concerns on solid waste management and to continue its efforts towards a more effective and efficient arm of the government in conserving natural resources.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:
• The General Services Officer conduct annually the physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment and reconcile on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the accounting records to fully identify the discrepancies noted. Complete all the fields required in the RPCPPE form and the necessary remarks.
observations.managementAction:
Presented below are the proposed steps and actions that the management would like to act upon and to recalibrate the system pertinent to their roles and responsibilities:
 Facilitate and act on the complete physical count of its properties and reconcile the results with the property and accounting records;
 Follow the prescribed format needed in the conduct of physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment – Local Road Networks; and
 Maintain the subsidiary records for each class of Property, Plant and Equipment or the property cards prescribed in the accounting manual and COA Circular No, 2015-008 and reconcile the property cards with the Report on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN
observations.reason: The conduct of physical count is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:
• The Inventory Committee conduct the physical count of its Local Road Network and prepare the RPCLRN. Make use of the template provided in the Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 or Appendix 63 of the Local Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and complete the necessary information in every field. Submit the accomplished RPCLRN to the Accounting Office for reconciliation in the books;
• The Municipal Engineer provide cost segregation of every road networks owned by the Municipality per road component for the prior years. Submit the road networks duly segregated per road component to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO; and
• The Municipal Accountant provide strategies to identify the breakdown of beginning balances of Road networks.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive assisted the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office in the conduct of the physical count and the reconciliation of PPEs particularly to their Infrastructure Assets and Buildings and Other Structures accounts. Ensure that all PPEs in those groups of accounts are accounted and verified.
observations.reason: The physical count of the Local Road Network has been ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office reconcile their records to fairly present the inventory accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements as of December 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and Accounting Office conducted the reconciliation of their records on inventories.
observations.reason: The reconciliation of the records of both offices has been ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality consider the some of the most important qualitative characteristics of the financial reports of the public sector that make them useful to the users namely: relevance, faithful representation, understandability, timeliness, comparability and verifiability.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant present in the financial statements of special accounts public market and slaughterhouse the depreciation of these facilities and the total book value of these structures to provide adequate information as to assets, liabilities and equity and be able to determine whether the economic enterprises have become financially self-sustaining establishments.
observations.managementAction: The PPE and its depreciation presented in the financial statements were taken up in the Special Accounts – Public Market and Slaughterhouse.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality implement changes in its Annual budget pursuant to Articles 417 and 418 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 7160. Also ensure that the practice of social welfare department that kind welfare assistance be implemented considering the practices of social welfare and development department that target beneficiaries are explicitly identified and sex-disaggregated data generated to effectively address its welfare objectives. Likewise, procurement guidelines in the emergency or negotiated procurement be adhered to particularly GPPB Circular 01-2020.
observations.managementAction: The LGU believed that the 20% development fund utilization was in accordance with JMC N. 01 dated March 27, 2020. The LGU noted the recommendation and implemented the practices of social welfare department.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman, Vice-Chairman and its members fully justify the rice awarded procurements to Primavera Ricemill Corporation that showed price variance of ₱40.60 per kilogram of rice or a total sum of ₱6,140,750.00 and that the total sum has not to be the subject of audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) fully justify the awarded procurement. The goods procured from Primavera Ricemill Corporation were packed in 25 kilograms per sack while the rice procured from Dionisio Rice Grain Trading was packed in 50 kilograms per sack.
observations.reason: The rice procured from Primavera Ricemill Corporation was packed in 25 kilograms per sack.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality submit the necessary supporting documents to the audit team. Likewise, ensure that the terms, rules and regulations on the succeeding social amelioration programs are strictly followed and implemented by the Municipality to ensure timely delivery of relief to the constituents. Also ensure that succeeding disbursements are completely documented specially to those pertaining to PPAs which post-monitoring is essential to determine if the goals are attained by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will secure and compile supporting documents and explanations for the observations noted and submit the same to the COA auditors. The Municipality will also ensure succeeding disbursements are documented and post-monitoring be conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipality provide justification to the observations noted on the implementation of SAP – ESP to the audit team. Likewise, ensure that the terms, rules and regulations on the succeeding social amelioration programs are strictly followed and implemented by the Municipality to ensure timely delivery of relief to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Prepared a justification letter and submitted to concerned agencies. The Municipality assured compliance with terms and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman, Vice-Chairman and its members fully justify the awarded procurements to Sinotruck Howo A7 dump truck and HT 135W Hunter Wheel Excavator and Maravilla Trading for the delivery of one (1) unit Dump Truck – Model H3 and adhere to Section 12 of the IRR RA 9184 that BAC shall only recommend award and not award it.
observations.managementAction: The BAC strictly followed the rules and regulations of RA 9184 and its IRR on the procurement of said equipment. The awarding of contract for LGU projects was a task assigned to the Local Chief Executive.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the School Board strictly comply with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 and prepare the Annual and Supplemental Budget as prescribed for a more transparent and accountable reporting. We further recommended the Board effectively utilize the Fund and abide with procurement provisions for infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: The LSB complied with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG, prepared the annual and supplemental budget using the prescribed form. The LSB and the BAC will adhere to procurement requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the efficient and effective utilization of the 20% DF which is primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute to attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. The development programs/projects shall be planned in itemized budget and in the municipal AIP and LDIP. Any changes/reversions/realignments shall be pursuant to Articles 417 and 418 of the IRR of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The CDP and LDIP have been updated and realigned. The plans were reviewed by Provincial Review Committee. The plans are being finalized and review suggestions are being incorporated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to implement the programs and projects of the Mitigation Fund within the intended time and ensure that in-kind donations are accounted and reported consistently and follow prescribed templates.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO will implement the programs and projects under the Mitigation Fund within the intended time. The municipality prepared the CY 2021 Monthly and Annual Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as prescribed except for one assistance which was erroneously recorded and returned to the Bureau of the Treasury on May 19, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly follow the guidelines provided in JMC No. 2019-01 of DILG and DOF, and ensure that amended rates of fees and charges are reasonable and just sufficient to recover fixed and variable costs and create a business friendly environment.
observations.managementAction: A memorandum No. 25 series of 2021, dated May 03, 2021 was issued directing the Office of the Municipal Civil Registrar, Municipal Assessor and Municipal Agriculture to revert back to the fees and charges previously imposed by the LGU prior to enactment of Mun. Ordinance No. 08, 10 & 11 respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality immediately address the remaining obstructing issues to the final completion of the housing project. The lots and housing units must now be sold or awarded to the intended beneficiaries to prevent further losses.
observations.managementAction: Requested the Sangguniang to approve/pass a resolution allowing the use of the two-unit (2-storey) apartment type building located at Brgy Lipahan as isolation/quarantine facility instead of its original purpose as Housing Units to be sold to low-income earners Municipal Employees (LHB Resolution No. 01-2021).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Require the reconciliation, Inventory Team to finish/complete the conduct of the annual physical inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) by type and submit a Report on the Physicial Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in the prescribed format not later than January 31 of each year. Reconcile the RPCPPE with the ledger balances.
2. Require the Supply Officer to prepare a separate Inventory Report on obsolete, unserviceable/damaged and beyond repair PPEs using the Waste Material Report (WMR-Appendix 68) and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IRRUP-Appendix 67)
3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to drop from the books disposed items using the original copy of the WMR and IRRUP when properly authorized as basis for adjusting the accounts affected by means of a journal entry voucher;
4. Require the Inventory Team to assign property number to each PPE Inventory to facilitate validation and ascertain existence;
