observations.recommendation:
Management ensure compliance by Homegrown of the following:

Provisions of the contract, especially on the timely payment of rental fees to avoid the incurrence of penalty;
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented | Rental payments were monitored and currently updated.

observations.recommendation: Issuance of postdated checks for the succeeding years including the computed increase in rentals as stated in the contract;
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented | Management received post-dated checks as payment for rental fees.

observations.recommendation: Submission of Warranty Security in the form of cash or letter of credit issued by a universal or commercial bank in the amount of P60,000,000 to answer for the rental arrearages and damage to property by reason of Operator’s negligence; and
observations.reason: A Sangguniang Panlungsod committee hearing was held regarding this matter and a letter of demand was already issued.

observations.recommendation: Secure regularly property insurance for the Arena facility from the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented | The GSD has already secured building insurance from the GSIS for the Arena.

observations.recommendation:
Government Equity

The writing-off of the City’s dormant receivable accounts and fund transfers in the total amount of P5.470 million was made without seeking approval from the Commission on Audit contrary to COA Circular No. 2016-005, and resulted in the understatement of the Government Equity and other related accounts by the same amount. Moreover, the accounts payable reverted during the year amounting to P684,869 included the Guaranty and Security Deposits Payable and Trust Liability-DRRM balance, thus, understated the said accounts by the same amount. |  |  | 

We recommended that the CAD:

Restore the dormant receivable and fund transfer accounts that were written off without proper approval from the COA for fair presentation of the Government Equity (GE) account in the financial statements;
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented | Adjustments had been made in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 relative to the procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable and fund transfer accounts; and
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented | Management adhered to the regulations.

observations.recommendation: Restore the Guaranty and Security Deposits (G/SD) Payables and Trust Liability-DRRM accounts which were reverted.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented | Adjustments had been made in the books.

Trust Liability

The CY 2017 payrolls for the payment of honoraria to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) members totaling P1.450 million were not supported with the documents required under Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445 and Item 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, hence, considered as irregular expenditures. |  |  | 

We recommended that:

The Bids and Awards Committee, through the Chairperson, submit the lacking documents for the payment of honoraria to establish the validity and regularity of the claims and the payment thereof; and to facilitate audit of the said transactions. Non-submission of the said documents is a proper cause for the suspension of the said claims in audit;

Special Education Fund

Expenses charged against the Special Education Fund in the total amount of P10.460 million were not among those explicitly authorized under Section 272 of RA 7160 otherwise known as the Local Government Code (LGC) of 1991 and Joint Circular (JC) No. 1 series of 2017 of the Department of Education (DepEd)-Department of Budget and Management (DBM)- Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG). | Reiterated in Finding No. 13 |  | 

We recommended that:

The City Accountant:

Cause the refund from GF to SEF, the amount of P8,573,061, representing total disbursements for expenses that should not be paid out of the SEF but from the City’s general fund;

observations.recommendation:
Program or Performance Evaluation

Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management  Fund (LDRRMF)


The City’s LDRRMF was not properly utilized for the purpose for which the fund was created because the programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) charged to this fund amounting to P35.181 million were not linked to the four thematic areas covered by DRRM as enumerated under Item 5.0 of JMC No. 2013-1 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Moreover, the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) was not prepared, hence, utilization was not supported with the details of the PPAs to be funded by the allocated 30 percent Quick Response Fund (QRF) and 70 percent disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness, as well as those charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years. | Reiterated in Finding No. 14 |  | 

We recommended that Management:

Refrain from charging against the LDRRMF projects and activities that are not included or related to the DRRM activities as declared under Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 4.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Likewise, be guided with the provisions of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 as to the appropriate utilization of the LDRRMF, specifically with regard to the projects and activities for each thematic areas of disaster management enumerated thereto;

Refund from the General Fund to the Trust Liability-DRRM account the amount
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: of P35,180,800 representing the utilized amount of the LDRRMF for PPAs that do not fall within the disaster related activities specified under the law and statutes; and
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented | During CY 2018, Management adhered to the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Other Programs/Projects/
Activities (PPA)

The City Engineering Department (CED) failed to notify the public of their PPAs by non-posting of signboards within the venue where the PPA is located and non-completion of the required information for projects with signboards contrary to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer trace back the source of the discrepancies of the Cash in Bank account balances starting from 2010 records and prior and record all book reconciling items to bring the balances into agreement so that the correct balance of cash is presented in the financial statements;
The Municipal Accountant prepare monthly reconciliation statements between the bank, accounting records and cashbooks on all bank accounts of the LGU to avoid occurrence of additional discrepancy.  Verified reconciling items should be taken up immediately; and
The proposed adjustments be recorded to correct the cash book balance of cash in bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant are directed to trace back the source of the discrepancies of the Cash in Bank account balances starting from 2010 records and prior.                                                               All entries were adjusted in the Accounting and Cashbooks as proposed.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above observations, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer analyze the transactions in 2010 to determine the cause/s of the difference and make the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant is reminded that reciprocal accounts should always have corresponding entries and equal amounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal  Accountant were directed to undertake reconciliation of records as regards to fund transfer and journal entries. All Entries were adjusted in the accounting books as proposed.
Recommendation were noted and for compliance prospectively. The revision of the Inventory Report will follow.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct:

the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
the Municipal Accountant to trace the recorded road, highways and bridges which were closed to government equity prior to 2016 and subsequently record in its books of accounts the amount of road works equivalent to the required transition provision, keep and maintain Registry of Public Infrastructures for Road and subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation were noted and prospectively complied with.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to formulate strategies to intensify and facilitate tax collections.  If need be, avail of remedies embodied in Section 174 of RA 7160 to ensure collection of taxes due the municipal government in order to increase the delivery of basic services. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Certified List of All Property Tax Delinquencies to include all tax delinquents and reconcile the same with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Due to the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) set up of RPT Receivables amounting to ₱2.8M to ensure collections, Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer is directed to maintain Taxpayers' Index Card to keep track of the yearly collection of real property tax and support the balances of the account in the financial statements. The action plans are to issue demand letter and Tax campaign.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the following observations, we have recommended the following:

That the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to see to it that Disbursement Vouchers for government contracts are duly and properly supported before payments are made;
Guidelines to process financial assistance should be set and observed to regulate the program. Absence of required documentations should be an automatic basis for disqualification.  For this purpose, the following minimum requirements should be institutionalized, viz;
Medical Certificate (for medical assistance)
Death Certificate (for burial assistance)
School ID, enrollment form, certificate of registration (for educational assistance)
MSWDO case study with complete details.
Receipt of financial assistance duly acknowledged and dated.
The amount set for each type of financial assistance should also be complied with to ensure a non-discretionary determination of amounts given to each client; 

Payments for financial assistance should be made by check in the names of the recipients; and
The Municipal Accountant should ensure that all disbursements are with complete supporting documents and submitted within the reglementary period.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation were noted and prospectively complied with.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that all perfected contracts and purchase orders be submitted to COA within five days after perfection thereof for the immediate review of the reasonableness of the cost, conduct of inspection and other necessary audit procedures.
observations.managementAction: The BAC was instructed and directed to comply with the implementation of RA 9184. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant was directed that all perfected contracts and purchase orders be prepared and submittted to COA on time.

The Municipal Treasurer was directed to collect fees from the interested bidders for the cost of preparation and development of the bidding documents.

The BAC was instructed and directed to comply with the implementation of RA 9184 and attend trainings/updates on the Government Procurement Law.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above observations, we have recommended that the members of the BAC submit justification/explanation on the noted violations.  Henceforth, ensure that prospective bidders submit complete eligibility requirements before they are marked as “passed” and declared as LCB/HRB.
observations.managementAction: The BAC was instructed and directed to comply with the implementation of RA 9184. Due to inadvertence, Contract for the Procurement of Drug Testing Kit was erroneously entered into prior to bidding process date.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality maintain a special account and complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the 20% development fund in compliance with the aforementioned law to facilitate monitoring of development projects. Operations of the fund should be accounted for through the maintenance of books of accounts/complete subsidiary ledgers to enable the generation of financial statements at any given time. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer should regularly transfer the 20% development fund to facilitate monitoring of its utilization and ensure that the fund is not used for unauthorized programs, projects, and activities.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation were noted and prospectively complied with.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

the MDRRMO to prepare an investment plan for the LDRRMF of the Municipality for the current year and the unutilized balance from the previous years, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 (Annex C), to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the accomplishments and disbursement of the fund and guide in the accounting procedures to be taken up;
the Municipal Treasurer to transfer only the unutilized portion of QRF and DRRM-MOOE to the separate depository account for LDRRMF and the unutilized CO to remain in the General Fund and effect payments only on vouchers that are properly documented; and
the Municipal Accountant to:
reclassify the funds received for financial assistance to typhoon victims to Trust Liabilities –DRRMF; and
account the LDRRMF under the trust fund books.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer monitors the implementation of projects. Liquidated damages were imposed on unjustified delay of completion.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following:

the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer trace back the source of the discrepancies of the Cash in Bank account balances starting from 2010 records and prior and record all book reconciling items to bring the balances into agreement so that the correct balance of cash is presented in the financial statements;

the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer verify from the bank the details of all debit and credit memos reflected in the bank statements to record correctly these transactions; and

verified reconciling items should be taken up immediately after the preparation of the monthly reconciliation statements.

We have recommended that management formulate additional strategies to intensify and facilitate tax collections.  If need be, avail of remedies embodied in Section 174 of RA 7160 to ensure collection of taxes due the municipal government in order to increase the collection efficiency. Further, we have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Certified List of All Property Tax Delinquencies to include all tax delinquents and reconcile the same with the accounting records.

We also have recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the balance of the SET Receivable through entry to set up the receivable for the year. Henceforth, at the beginning of the year, set up receivables both for RPT and SET.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:

the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;

the Municipal Accountant to trace the recorded road, highways and bridges which were closed to government equity prior to 2016 and subsequently record in its books of accounts the amount of road works equivalent to the required transition provision, keep and maintain Registry of Public Infrastructures for Road and subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network and provide for the depreciation for the local road network; and

the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts and present fairly the financial statements. Henceforth, in determining whether to recognize the disbursements as expenses or property, plant and equipment, the program of work and work items be carefully considered and inquiry from the Municipal Engineer if warranted.

In view of the above observation, we reiterated that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer analyze the transactions in 2010 to determine the cause/s of the difference and make the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant is reminded that reciprocal accounts should always have corresponding entries and equal amounts. Also, the Municipal Accountant is reminded to review the entries of the receipt of the fund transfers to the concerned barangays.
We have recommended that management submit the Municipal School Board Budget to verify the legality of the disbursements.

We have recommended that management prepare its APP and supporting PPMPs to serve as guide in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects and furnish COA of the same for audit/evaluation purposes.  No procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP of the procuring entity.
We have recommended that the members of the BAC, TWG and BAC secretariat ensure the use of the standard forms of the PBDs.
We have recommended that the BAC invite observers in writing in all the stages of the procurement process in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
We have recommended that the Municipality adhere to the governing rules on the sale of bidding documents to all interested bidders as provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We have recommended that the members of the BAC submit justification/explanation on the noted violations and ensure that prospective bidders submit complete eligibility requirements and post the necessary bid security before they are marked as “passed” and “post-audited”.

We have recommended that the members of the BAC submit justification/explanation on the noted violations and ensure that winning bidders submit complete documentary requirements prior to entering into contract.

We have recommended that the Municipal Government, through the BAC Secretariat comply with the posting requirements on all its procurement.  Notices of Award and Proceed, and the approved contract be posted in the PhilGEPS website and website of the municipality, if any, within the reglementary period as required by the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

In view of the above observations, we have recommended that management refrain from indicating brand names in the description of goods requested and include in the ABC of goods the total approved budget regardless of source.
Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor consider sending the BAC members, TWG and BAC secretariat to attend trainings on the latest updates on the different provisions of the Government Procurement Law or R.A. 9184.

We have recommended that management furnish the audit team with copies of the MOA or trust agreements on all fund transferred by National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided in Item 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We have recommended that management determine the cause of delay and address the same in order to comply with the required submission dates of accounts and reports. We also have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursements.

We have recommended that all perfected contracts and purchase orders be submitted to COA within five days after perfection thereof for the immediate review of the reasonableness of the cost, conduct of inspection and other necessary audit procedures.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the municipal officials concerned to submit list of all on-going municipal PPAs at the beginning of the year and those that are to be implemented during the year, with complete information, regardless of source.

In view of the noted observations, we have recommended that:

the municipal accountant maintain a special account and complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the 20% development fund to facilitate monitoring of development projects. Operations of the fund should be accounted for through the maintenance of books of accounts/complete subsidiary ledgers to enable the generation of financial statements at any given time;

the municipal treasurer transfer the entire 20% development fund to facilitate monitoring of its utilization and ensure that the fund is not used for unauthorized programs, projects and activities; and

implement immediately the programs pertinent to the unutilized balance embodied in the AIP.

We recommended that the City Accounting Department coordinate with the City Treasury Department to look into the  variances noted and/or account for the discrepancies noted in the RPT and SET Receivables,  and make the necessary journal entry to correct the RPT/SET Receivables and the affected account balances in the books of accounts.

We recommended that:
The City Assessor:
Retain the records of the retired or cancelled PINs in the system to facilitate validation whether the RPUs are newly created/discovered/ consolidated;
Refrain from removing existing permanent PINs/RPUs with outstanding collectibles in the records;
See to it that every correction effected in the system is supported with approval from higher authority; and
Immediately notify the CTD of any change/update of information regarding the RPUs to facilitate updating of its records and reports.
The Information Technology Officer:
Install additional system controls that would check duplicates to avoid occurrence of duplicate PINs/transactions being posted in the system;
Disable or remove the edit function in the Billing Module of the RPTAS; and
Make sure that appropriate controls in the RPTAS are consistently in place and effective to ensure that system generated reports are accurate and complete; and
The CAD maintain SL for the RPT accounts in accordance with Section 111 of PD 1445, Section 10, Volume II of the NGAS Manual and COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording and reporting the financial transactions.

We recommended that:
The CAD:
Reconcile the balances of the accounts Office Supplies Inventory, Other Supplies and Materials Inventory and their corresponding Expense accounts;
Strictly apply the Perpetual Method in recording inventory purchases and outflow of the same; and
Properly classify expenses according to nature and use to comply with the requirements of  PPSAS 1.
The CGSD:
Regularly submit the SSMI to the CAD to enable the latter to record the cost of the supplies issued for a particular period; and
Include only in the SSMI items which are considered as Inventories particularly, office supplies inventories and other supplies and materials inventories.

We recommended that the CAD:
Restore the dormant receivable and fund transfer accounts that were written off without proper approval from the COA for fair presentation of the Government Equity (GE) account in the financial statements;
Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 relative to the procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable and fund transfer accounts; and
Restore the Guaranty and Security Deposits (G/SD) Payables and Trust Liability-DRRM accounts which were reverted.

We recommended that:
The Bids and Awards Committee, through the Chairperson, submit the lacking documents for the payment of honoraria to establish the validity and regularity of the claims and the payment thereof; and to facilitate audit of the said transactions. Non-submission of the said documents is a proper cause for the suspension of the said claims in audit; and
The CAD check the completeness of the supporting documents and refrain from processing claims of BAC honoraria if it is not supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that:
The City Accountant:
Cause the refund from GF to SEF, the amount of P8,573,061,  representing total disbursements for expenses that should not be paid out of the SEF but from the City’s general fund;
Demand from the LSB the refund of the amounts pertaining to expenses charged to SEF, otherwise, the transactions will be  suspended/ disallowed in audit with the City officials as the persons responsible/liable for approving the said transactions; and
The City Budget Officer to restrict the allocation and prioritization of the SEF to the programs and projects defined under the law, rules and regulations in order to maximize the benefits intended for the public elementary and secondary schools which could be derived from this fund.

For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended that the Management:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current  year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.
Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all the necessary documents for the purchase of the land to avoid doubts on the validity and propriety of the transaction.

We recommended that the LGU adhere to the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations regarding the payment of RATA of members of the local sanggunian and other municipal officials and employees. We further recommended that the RATA received during the absences of the concerned Sangguniang Bayan be refunded.

We recommended that the BAC require the submission from the winning bidders/contractors the duly approved Construction Safety and Health Program as forming part of the perfected contracts upon its submission for the contract review. Pending the approval by the DOLE of the program on the date of submission of perfected contracts for review, a certification that the program has been submitted for approval by the DOLE should be attached. Upon the final payment of these contracts, the Municipal Accountant should require said duly approved program. This is to ensure compliance with R.A. 9184 and issuance of the DOLE.
We recommended that management require the contractor to submit the materials test results to ensure the quality of the materials to be used for infrastructure projects.
We have recommended that management promulgate and implement programs, projects and activities that address gender issues and women’s concerns.

We recommended that the Municipality through its Local Social Welfare and Development Officer continuously monitor the implementation of the programs/projects under the LCPC to make sure that all plans will be implemented so that the benefits will be properly delivered to its constituents especially to the children.

We recommended that the Municipality execute a written Contract of Lease of Market Stall with the lessees of the stall in the public market so that the latter will be aware of their obligations. The Lease Contract also serves as a basis for revocation of the rights of the lessees in case of breach.

We recommended that the municipality see to it that the programs/projects under 20% Development Fund should be implemented for the benefit of the constituents. The municipal officials should review their plan to determine if these programs and projects are no longer relevant for the attainment of development in the municipality so that the funds budgeted to these programs may be re-aligned to a more useful and viable projects. Further, savings from implemented projects should be re-appropriated as part of current year’s development fund.

We recommended that:
a. Management revisit the programs funded by National Agencies and to properly implement them so that the desired purpose will be achieved.
b. Management ensure that funds from National Agencies be liquidated and unexpended balances should be returned to source agencies to relieve them from any accountability.

We recommended that the management settle/appeal disallowance within six (6) months from receipt of the notice. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/disallowed in post-audit.

For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we reiterated our
recommendation that management conduct the required physical count of the
PPE as of December 31 of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any
month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This
procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed
assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable
assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to
be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pinpoint
responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs. Moreover, to
maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of
property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and
monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them. For control and
monitoring, make use of ICS for semi-expendable items to avoid loss, as these are
usually small items.

We recommended that management enjoin the Inventory Committee to
include in the conduct of inventory of the properties of the municipality the
infrastructures and other community facilities and for the Inventory Committee
to prepare and submit the required Report to the DILG. We recommended
further that the Municipal Accountant complete the Registry of Public
Infrastructure and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant the
following:
1. strictly comply with the provisions of PPSAS Nos. 1 and 17 and DILG-
DBM JMC No. 21-3 dated May 23, 2017 for the proper recording and
monitoring of funds and recognition of completed projects for fair
presentation of the financial statements,
2. prepare the following proposed adjusting journal entry to recognize
completed projects under BUB and ADM as assets in the books of the
agency:
Water System 5,459,582.69
Motor Vehicles 3,409,800.00
Road Networks 3,855,918.46
Books 1,005,200.00
Government Equity 13,730,501.15

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following;
1. Notify the contractors about the defects and require them to perform
corrective actions to prolong the life of LGU’s infrastructure projects.
2. Require the Project Monitoring Committee to perform regular and thorough
monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that only those satisfactorily
completed and in accordance with the specifications are paid.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board comply to limit the
utilization of the SEF to the priority programs and projects defined under R.A. No.
7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 in order to
maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.
We further recommended that the amount of P229,350.00 be charged against the
General Fund.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the pertinent
provision of R.A. No. 7160 for proper monitoring and accounting of the transfers
and expenditures of the 20% Development Fund account. We further recommend the
transfer in the depository account of the said fund all the amounts due to it.

We recommended that Management, thru its MDRRMC, study and evaluate
its Annual MDRRM Plan and determine constraints/reasons for the minimal
implementation of its plans and programs, especially the Mitigation Fund, resulting
in the failure to attain its intended objectives and non-compliance to disaster
preparedness minimum standards for DRRM.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management strictly comply with
the provisions of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, PCW-DILG JMC No.
2014-01 and COA Circular No. 2004-00 and that concerned municipal officials and
employees fully implement programs/projects/activities funded under the GAD Plan
and Budget to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents. Further, for
the GFPS to ensure and monitor the effective implementation of the annual GAD
PPAs.

We recommended to Management to appropriate in the Annual Budget at
least one percent (1%) of their IRA for LCPC programs, projects and activities as
required under Section 15 of RA 9344. We recommend further that the design of
PPAs should be in accordance with the enumeration under Section II of DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive the following:
1. Revisit the organization structure of the municipality so as to create
positions which are essential in the delivery of public service to the
constituents and to lessen the number of JOCs,
2. Submit/attach Accomplishment Reports and certification that the
activities/services cannot be provided by regular and permanent
personnel to prove the validity and legality of the disbursements
pertaining to their salaries.
3. Strictly comply with the abovementioned provisions on the hiring of
JOCs.

We recommended that management adopt measures that would facilitate the
submission of their accounts on time as prescribed in the foregoing Sections of COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and P.D. 1445. We further
recommend that additional staff with accounting background be assigned at the
Accounting Office in order to address the delay on the submission of accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management cause strict
compliance to COA Circular 2009-1 on the timely submission of contract with all its
supporting documents by the BAC Chairperson/Secretariat to the Auditor within
five (5) days upon execution/issuance of the contracts.

We reiterated our recommendation, and Management agreed to comply,
that the Treasurer attach copies of the ORs to the RCD to enable the Municipal
Accountant to check the accuracy of the collections and deposits.

We recommended that Management immediately settle the aforesaid tax
liabilities to avoid further distrain or levy upon the personal and real properties of the
Municipality and accumulation of interests and surcharges to the detriment of the
funds of the LGU.

We recommended Management to enforce the immediate settlement of the
balance of the unsettled audit disallowances in order to protect the interest of the
government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to make appropriate adjustments in their respective records of the reconciling items noted in the course of reconciliation of records made by the said offices in order to arrive at an accurate and reliable balances of PPE accounts, including the proper accounting entries for construction of projects still in progress for a fair presentation of accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.

Management’s Comments:
The concerned Municipal officers claimed that they still in the process of
reconciling the noted differences in the PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:

c) Provide reconciliation of the P15,913,040.48 difference between the
accounting records and the certified list of delinquent taxpayers from the
Treasury Office so that a reconcile balanced would be presented in the
financial statements for a fair presentation of said accounts; and
d) Establish with certainty on whether supporting documents of the outstanding
receivable accounts are still available. Otherwise, document the process by
which such undertaking was taken so that appropriate measure for write-off
of affected accounts may be considered so as to rid the books of unreliable
accounts balances for accurate presentation in the financial statements of the
Municipality at year-end.

Management’s Comments:
The Accounting Office had coordinated with the Treasury Office to
establish the exact valuation of RPT Receivables and effect adjustments on
differences. As of date, exact valuation is still on-going.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the members of the
Local Development Council to submit satisfactory explanations on the
improprieties noted and to:

 Ensure that the proposed projects under the 20% Development Fund for a
specific year would be implemented within the year that it was given
appropriation so as to achieve the maximum benefits that the Fund could
provide; and
 Strictly comply with the mandates of the law that only allowable expenses
must be charged to the Fund, partaking the nature of investment or capital
expenditures.

Management’s Comments:
As explained by the Municipal Budget Officer, the non-implementation of
some of the projects incorporated in the 2017 Annual Investment Plan (AIP) has
caused by the realization of the requested budget from the National Government for
the same project(s) and due to some other reasons that can be considered more
advantageous to the LGU.

They have considered our Agricultural Program (Purchase of Fertilizers,
Planting Materials, Palay and Vegetables Seedlings) and Maintenance/Rehabilitation
of barangay road in 42 barangays (backfilling) as part of their Economic
Development Projects and the Installation of Concrete Artificial Reefs – MPAs for
their Environmental Management Programs.

When it comes to the completed project for cemetery improvement with an
appropriation of P500,000.00, the Management admitted the occurrence of observed
deficiencies and commented that they will be more extra prudent in the future so that
they can fully comply with the mandates of the law regarding the matter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC), the Municipal Accountant, and all other concerned
Municipal officials to submit justifications on why the procurement process for
the Construction of (Pulong Sagingan) CalitCalit-Mabalanoy bridge, from bid
preparation, to opening of bids, up to contract agreement have been completed
despite lacking bid documents, and awarding of the project to ineligible bidder,
contrary to the provisions set forth under the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.

Further, direct the BAC and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to
submit the lacking documents as discussed for further evaluation and audit, and
to ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the same RIRR of RA No. 9184
to guarantee that all prospective bidders in every government project are
compliant with all the existing tax laws, rules and regulations.

Management’s Comments:
In the exit conference, the concerned Municipal officials could not provide
sufficient explanations on the deficiencies noted, alleging that they are still in the
process of cross-checking the documents covered by said project. Nonetheless, they
promised to submit the required explanations, and the lacking documents to prove
their compliance with the existing procurement law, though if ever miss-
interpretation of the procurement process was committed by their group, then
appropriate actions would be made to attain full compliance of the aforecited
provisions of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Engineer and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the
lists of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those
that are to be implemented during the year, in compliance with the above cited
regulations and for transparency of its operation.

Management’s Comments:
The Municipality promised compliance with the general guidelines relevant to
PPA of government agencies, particularly Section 2.1 and Section 3.1 of COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Honorable
members of the Sangguniang include among its top priorities the construction of
a building for the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of
documents generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality
including its 42 barangays, particularly those for submission to the audit team,
with consideration on the preservation period of ten (10) years as mandated by
law.

Management’s Comments:
The Management explained that failure to comply with the requirement on the
provision of store room for the Commission on Audit for the safekeeping and
preservation of the agency’s disbursement vouchers and other related documents for a
period of ten years is due to insufficient space in the existing location of the
Municipal Hall Building.

There is an ongoing construction of the multi-purpose building wherein an
adequate stock room and office for COA will be provided. For the meantime, the
building that will be vacated this June 2018 by the Bureau of Jail Management and
Penology will be refurbished and be provided for such purpose.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer to explain why the Approved Budget for the Contracts (ABC) and
Program of Works (POW) were prepared not in accordance with the
Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) Department Order Nos.
163 and 197 series of 2015 and 2016.

Likewise, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor require the
Municipal Engineer and the BAC to explain the deficiency in the number of days
presented in the delivery schedule per PhilGEPS and contract duration per
contract.

Thus, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the concerned
officials and employees to keep abreast of the issuances of the DPWH in the
adoption and preparation of the ABC and POW standard formats for clarity on
the basis by which municipal projects are funded and implemented.

Management’s Comments:
The Municipal Engineer apologized for their non-compliance with DPWH
Department Order Nos. 163 and 167, series of 2015 and 2016, respectively, regarding
the preparation of ABC and POW. He claimed good faith, with the belief that the
said Department Orders are intended only to all DPWH Implementing Offices and the
adoption of POW/ABC/DUPA Standard Forms are applicable only to projects costing
P200 million and above. Nonetheless, the Municipality is willing to abide and follow
the Department Orders for all future projects of LGU.

With regard to the deficiency in the number of days presented in the delivery
schedule per PhilGEPS, and contract duration per contract, the concerned municipal
officers admitted that some of the data or information are lacking in the present
format that they are using. However, they promised to adopt the prescribed format
and address all the deficiencies noted by the Audit Team.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor  require the  Inventory  Committee  to
conduct  the  physical  count  on  properties,  after  which   prepare   the
corresponding inventory reports; and direct the Municipal Treasurer and  the
 Municipal Accountant to maintain property records   and  to  reconcile  the
same to assure the existence  and  reliability  of  the  Inventory  and  PPE
accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prioritize  the preparation  of
the  updated bank reconciliation  statements  and  submit  it  within   the
mandated timeline.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor make representation  with  the  source
agency  for the details and purpose of the downloaded funds.   Further,   we
recommend that the Municipal Engineer  monitor  the  implementation  of  the
projects to ensure that these will be completed  within  the  timeframe  set
so that expected benefits from the project will be attained.

We recommend that Management   allocate  1%  of  its   Internal   Revenue
Allotment for the strengthening and  implementation  of  programs,  projects
and activities for the protection and welfare of children as provided  under
RA 9344.

We recommend that the members of the  Local School  Board  strictly  observe
the provisions of DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1  series  of
2017 on the  prioritization  of  projects,  programs   and  activities  that
should be included in   the  Local  School  Board  Budget.  Henceforth,  the
Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer refrain  from  allowing  utilization
of SEF to expenditures not in conformity with the existing guidelines.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination  with  the  Municipal
Engineer, see to it that   identified  priority  programs  and  projects  be
implemented as planned for the benefit of its constituents.

We recommend that Management  strictly impose the provisions of the Revised
 Revenue Code and Rental Agreement between the  LGU  and  tenants  to  avoid
 accumulation of delinquent  rental fees. We further recommend that a Demand
 Letter be made to avoid accumulation of delinquent rentals.

We recommend that  the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned  officials  to
furnish the auditor with copies of  perfected  purchase  orders  within  the
prescribed period to facilitate the  auditorial  review  and  evaluation  of
terms and conditions thereof. In addition, we recommend  that  the  purchase
order  be  duly filled up  with  all  the  data  needed  as  guide  for  the
supplier in the delivery  of  goods  and  for  the  Municipality  to  impose
liquidated damages in case of delay in delivery of goods.

We recommend that the  Municipal  Mayor  issue   the  Certificate  of  Final
Acceptance after the one (1) year defects liability  period  and  after  all
defects and failures, if any,  shall  be  repaired  by  the  Contractor  and
inspected by  the  Municipal  Engineer  who  shall  prepare  the  Acceptance
Report.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct  the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and  Management  Officer  to  prepare   the  Local  Disaster  Risk
Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan  (LDRRMFIP)  as  prescribed  under
Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular  No.  2012-002  as  guide  in  monitoring  the
utilization of DRRM Fund.

We recommend that  the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Treasurer   to
effect  adjustment through issuance of an Official Receipt for over  deposit
of  collections.   Further,  we  recommend  that  the  accountable   officer
strictly monitor the total collections received before deposit to  the  bank
in order to avoid over or under deposit of collections.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor submit the approved  suspension  order
of work as cited in the Revised Implementing Rules  and  Regulations  of  RA
9184 to justify the  extension  of  contract  period  otherwise,  liquidated
damages will be imposed.

We recommend  that  the  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the
Municipal Treasurer to remit the taxes withheld on purchases  of  goods  and
services  to the BIR within ten (10) days after the end  of  each  month  as
prescribed under the BIR regulation to avoid possible imposition of  penalty
and surcharge.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer (GSO)-Designate to exert effort to reconcile the PPE account of the municipality;
2. Direct the GSO-Designate to provide with unit values of PPE items listed in the
Report of Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE).
3. Compel the Accountant and GSO-Designate to abide with the capitalization
threshold of P 15,000.00 as required;
4. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Mun. GSO-Designate to maintain PPELC
and PC respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to implement
and monitor implementation of programs and projects funded out of the 20%
Development Fund both of the Current and Prior Years continuing balances.
b. Compel the Municipal Engineer to fast track the preparation of engineering
plans, designs, programs of work, etc. and required documents;
c. Direct other support staff and personnel to facilitate completion of documents
relative to programs and projects funded by the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to:
1. Strictly adhere to Section 287 of RA 7160 & DBM-DILG joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
2. Ensure that only projects aligned with the purpose/objectives of the 20%
Development Fund be funded from such appropriation.

We recommended that the Local School Board implement and utilize the
proceeds of the Special Education Fund as contained in the SEF Annual Budget in
consonance with Sections 272 and 100 (c) of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Budget Officer
to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 known as
Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006 and appropriate the required allocation of
1% of its IRA for the LCPC activities. Furthermore, the Local Chief Executive
should cause the creation of the Local Council for the Protection of Children and
direct the said council to prepare its Annual Work and Financial Plan for the
attainment of the LCPC projects and activities for the welfare of the children.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Compel the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of servicing these
private lending institutions through salary deductions of the employees,
2. Refrain from signing or renewing Memorandum of Agreements (MOA)
with Private Lending Institutions.

We recommended that whenever medicines are procured, the Local Chief
Executive compel the Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Accountant to adhere
to the provisions cited in EO no. 49, DOH AO no. 163 series of 2002 and COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure that items procured from
legitimate sources and that they are effective as well as genuine. Likewise, the
Property Custodian should be compelled to submit to the Accounting office the
Monthly Report of Medicines Issued as a basis for dropping from the inventory
account and taking up Medicine Expenses.

We recommended that for RA 9184, only GPPB and those accredited
trainers listed in the GPPB website are authorized to conduct capability development
seminars.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant or employee in-charge of RATA to
strictly follow paragraph 2.3 of COA Circular No. 2000-05 dated October 4,
2000 to avoid disallowance in audit.
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to compute Transportation Allowance
less the number of days when the official used a government vehicle.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
1. Require the BAC Chairman to adhere strictly to Section 10 of RA
9184 of LGU’s procurement for Consultancy Services.
2. Submit the necessary documents required under Notice of Suspension
No 2018-06 dated January 26, 2018.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit legal basis to
support the payment of financial assistance to 80 job orders of LGU at P2,000.00
each.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Engineer, Municipal Agriculture, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer
and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to fast track the implementation of
various projects funded from fund transfers from source agencies to facilitate the
liquidation of the funds as well as the availment of the benefits of the projects by the
constituents.

We enjoined the Local Chief Executive to require the LDRRM council to
include the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF of the previous years
which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund in the LDRRMFIP so that it could
be utilized for disaster risk reduction projects and programs/activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD council
together with the LPDO to come-up with the database in attuning to the demands of
GAD issues and concerns.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to instruct the personnel in-charge in
the preparation of the remittance of employees and employer share due to
GSIS to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 8291 so that failures and
lapses will be avoided.
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be
remitted to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the
withholding was made or the withholding was accrued.
c. Require the concerned employee in-charge in the preparation of GSIS
remittances and municipal accountant to conduct thorough reconciliation
with its records with that of the GSIS and give priority to those affected
employees with negative balances for one (1) month to three (3) years, and
request from GSIS for refund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to record the reconciling
items to correct the amount for the Cash in Bank account and to inform the
depository Bank of the reconciling items requiring adjustments in the books of the
bank.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should consider creating and
organizing an Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No 4177 and AO 70
to give assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations
of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with
unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations.
The Municipal Accountant should also regularly reconcile the amounts withheld and
remitted to Pag-IBIG in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances
of Due to Pag-IBIG account in the financial statements.

In view of the above observations, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer be instructed to deposit all collections regularly pursuant to Joint DBM-DOF Cir. 1-81 and Section 69 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections weekly and all the Revenue Collection Clerk must remit daily. Accounting Entries No. 1 and No. 2 were recorded as proposed, but accounting entry No. 3 was recorded as debit to Due to Officers and Employees.  This entry is to correct the audit adjustments from 2013, refer to the Statement of Bank reconciliation dated December 31, 2013.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following:

that the Municipal accountant prepare monthly reconciliation statement between the accounting records and cashbooks on cash – local treasury account; and
that the Municipal Treasurer record the proposed adjustments to correct the balance cash – local treasury account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the concerned personnel to record the proposed adjustments and prepare the required reports.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor instructs:

the Municipal Treasurer to limit her cash advances to those authorized under COA Circular No. 97-002 and to observe the general principles of the imprest system that payments must be made by check for amounts exceeding ₱15,000.00; and 
henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should submit within the prescribed days the liquidation and supporting documents to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is directed for this 2017 to limit her cash advances to those authorized under COA Circular No. 97-002 and to observe the general principles of the imprest system, and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking liquidation documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Technical Working Group of the Bids and Awards Committee familiarize themselves and observe the updated guidelines in the preparation of the ABC to ensure that the correct upper limit or ceiling for acceptable bid prices are properly set for every project of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation were noted and prospectively complied with.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management: 

justify/explain the reasons for exceeding the procedural limits provided under R.A. No. 9184;
adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement and see to it that the procurement program allows sufficient lead time for such public bidding; and
resort to alternative methods of procurement only in highly exceptional cases provided for in Rule XVI to promote economy and efficiency.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee are hereby  directed  adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we reiterated our recommendation that:

Management determine the cost of the unperformed portion of the works for every day of delay and compute the amount of liquidated damages in accordance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. 9184 and the contract agreement; and
that the Municipal Mayor enforce immediate collection of liquidated damages and ensure the refund of the excess of the bill of quantities from the contract price from the contractors.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation were noted and prospectively complied with.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above audit observations, have recommended the following:

that a special book of account and depository account be maintained solely for the share from Tobacco Excise Tax (RA 7171); and
that management maintain a separate Registry of Allotment and Obligations and subsidiary ledger for the fund from RA 7171 for proper control.

We have recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator specify the KALAHI projects to be funded from the 20% Development Fund in the Annual Investment Plan which must be in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Circular 2011-1 and the Municipal Budget Officer to provide funds in the regular budget of the respective implementing offices for expenses that are administrative in nature.  Henceforth, refrain from charging expenses for wages, travelling expenses, supplies and the like under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Findings on the KALAHI accounts were already addressed, thus for the year 2017, the Budget for KALAHI were excluded from the 20% Development Fund and transferred under the Municipal Social Welfare Development Office. The 20% Development Fund Budget is now fully appropriated for the purpose as set by the DBM-DILG Joint Circular 2011-1.

The Local Chief Executive directed the MSWDO and GAD Focal Point to submit a copy of the LGU GAD Plan for 2016 and 2017. Please be informed that the recipients and beneficiaries of the Gender and Development Appropriations were primarily and substantially directed to women's gender program and activities.  The Annual GAD Plan and Budgeting Committee strongly endorsed for the utilization of the GAD on the promotion of economic empowerment.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above observations, we have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to prepare an investment plan for the LDRRMF of the Municipality for the current year and the unutilized balance from the previous years, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 (Annex C), to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the accomplishments and disbursement of the fund and guide in the accounting procedures to be taken up.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive directed the MDRRMO to submit a copy of the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan for 2016 and 2017. Furthermore, the MDRRMO is directed to submit the monthly Report on sources and utilization of DRRMF.  The Local Chief Executive directed the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to comply with COA Circular 2012-002 prescribing a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for QRF, MOOE and Capital Outlay. Furthermore, the Municipal Treasurer is directed to open a Trust Fund Account for the provision of unexpended / unobligated balance of the QRF, DRRMF-MOOE.  Finally, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant is directed to submit to COA the required documents for all various transactions totaling Php1,210,355.83.
