observations.managementAction:
Partially implemented

Reiterated under Finding No. C.1




Not implemented | Management has remitted the amount of P1,661,319.08 to the BIR thru Landbank of the Philippines in May 2017.  




No action taken. | Occurrence of over and/or under remittance of withheld taxes still exists due to lack of proper monitoring of withheld amounts from concerned suppliers and personnel.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive:

Compel the persons liable for the restitution or settlement of outstanding liabilities, and to see to it that current issuances are settled within the period prescribed by law; and


Consider the provision of RRSA on the solidary liability/obligation of the persons determined to be liable;

That the CAD to maintain SLs/records for the concerned officials/employees for the disallowed amount and regularly update the same when payments are made; and

That the Human Resource Management Office causes the immediate withholding of salaries or other compensation due those persons liable who are currently in the government service of San Juan City, through the City Treasurer, in satisfaction of their disallowance pursuant to COA regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Fully implemented




Fully implemented

Fully implemented




Partially implemented | Management sent demand letters dated June 2, 2017 to the persons liable in the NDs who are currently in the active service of the City Government informing them to submit proposals for the withholding of salaries in settlement of the disallowances.  

The persons liable sent letter-request to Management for the settlement of their liabilities 
by paying partially or in installment payments.

Management forwarded the request to COA, thru the Office of the Auditor, pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2017-021 dated November 3, 2017


Management is waiting for the decision of COA-Legal Services Sector, relative to the request of the concerned persons liable for the installment payment of their liabilities

In the meantime, Management has started withholding from the salaries the payment for the liabilities of some persons liable. | .

observations.recommendation:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current  year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.

Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.
observations.managementAction: The committee was able to conduct the physical inventory count partially. Inventory tags were put to some properties.
observations.reason: Inventory Report as of April and October 2017 were submitted. However, verification on the reports submitted the inventory takings were incomplete and the reports were not consolidated and tallied with the books of the municipality. We also verified that some properties had already property tags.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit copies thereof to the Auditor for review and verification of the accuracy of the Cash in Bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted but not monthly.
observations.reason: Validated and agreed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the OIC - Municipal Treasurer and the Revenue Officers follow the provisions of the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs in managing the collections to safeguard the same from possible loss/misuse/misappropriations of government funds.
observations.managementAction: There were still collections that were not remitted daily and sometimes collections were not deposited intact.
observations.reason: Validated and agreed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to COA the accounts of the municipality within the required time frame to facilitate the timely evaluation of the same.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit disbursement vouchers and duplicate copies of official receipt on time.
observations.reason: Validated and agreed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the officials and employees concerned in the processing of payment for terminal leave benefits, more specifically the approving authorities, revisit the guidelines before allowing payment for any claims. In light of the foregoing observation, it is worth emphasizing that the payment of terminal leave is based on the highest monthly salary received at any time during the employee’s period of employment in the government service and not on her latest salary, unless the latter is the highest received by the employee. Since payment has already made to the former Vice Mayor, we recommend that she refund the amount she received in compliance with the abovementioned provision.
We have recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor countersign all checks drawn for the payment of expenses of the Legislative Department pursuant to the provision of Section 40 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: The Vice Mayor countersigned checks for the expenditures appropriated for Sanggunian.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management require the drivers to accomplish the Trip ticket properly. Furthermore, that the Municipal Accountant see to it that Driver’s Trip Tickets attached to the disbursement vouchers are properly filled up.
observations.managementAction: Management properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets.
observations.reason: During post audit it was noted that Driver’s Trip Tickets were properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management assess and collect the correct amount of business tax as provided in the Revised Municipal Revenue Code to increase the income of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Proper assessment was already done however the submission of the Income Tax Returns were not yet imposed.
observations.reason: Validated and agreed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of an Accomplishment Report for the disbursement of gasoline expenses in the road maintenance and regravelling of various barangays to monitor whether the desired output was achieved and also serve as control whether government funds were properly disbursed.
observations.managementAction: Accomplishment Report was not prepared.
observations.reason: During the post audit it was found out that no accomplishment report was attached to disbursement for gasoline expenses in the road maintenance and regravelling.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management fully utilize the 20% Development Fund for priority programs/projects and activities for the benefit of the constituents.  Further, any unexpended balance should be re-appropriated as part of the current year’s development fund.
observations.managementAction: Some unimplemented projects from previous years were already realigned and implemented during the year.
observations.reason: Validated and agreed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the following proposed adjusting entries necessary to correct the amounts presented in Financial Statement
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were already prepared.
observations.reason: Validated and agreed

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management conduct the required physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pinpoint responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs. Moreover, to maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them. For control and monitoring, make use of ICS for semi-expendable items to avoid loss, as these are usually small items.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted the physical count on December 21-22, 2017 to be continued in the 1st Quarter of FY 2018 to comply in the submission of a Repor on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Nevertheless, the Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian started to maintain 2 ARE.
observations.reason: The LGU has partially conducted the physical count on December 21-22, 2017 to be continued in the 1st Quarter of FY 2018 to comply in the submission of a RPCPPE.  Nevertheless, the Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian started to maintain 2 ARE.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management enjoin the Inventory Committee to include in the conduct of inventory of the properties of the municipality the infrastructures and other community facilities and for the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the required Report to the DILG. We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant complete the Registry of Public Infrastructure and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee will conduct the inventory and prepare and submit the required Report to the DILG. The Municipal Accountant will complete the Registry of Public Infrastructure. Some were already disclosed in the Schedule of Public Infrastructure attached in the FS.
observations.reason: The LGU is yet to prepare and submit the required Report to the DILG. The Municipal Accountant will complete the Registry of Public Infrastructure. Some were already disclosed in the FS as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:
1. Submit justification why public bidding was not resorted to for the procurement totaling P2,226,162.00. 
2. Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR. Conduct public bidding for purchases of goods and the use of alternative modes of procurement be in accordance with the IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
3. Observe the thresholds in the procurement of goods. Resort to public bidding when the procurement exceed the threshold amount.
4. Submit to the Audit Team the deficiencies noted in the basic documentary requirements to all purchases under alternative modes of procurement to enable us to make decisions in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management and BAC committed to strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.reason: The BAC submitted justification on Nov. 2, 2016 in response to AOM No. 16-011. The Audit Team issued a rejoinder on Nov. 8, 2016 reiterating our recommendations, some of which are not yet complied with as to date.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) observe and strictly comply with the above-mentioned provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ascertain the legality of the bidding activities and validity of transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management and BAC committed to strictly comply with the provisions of the R.A. No. 9184.
observations.reason: The BAC submitted the justification on Nov. 2, 2016 in response to AOM No. 16-011. The Audit Team issued a rejoinder on Nov. 8, 2016 reiterating our recommendations, some of which are not yet complied with as to date.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the BAC Chairperson/Secretariat comply strictly with the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2007-3 on the payment of BAC Honoraria. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of the PPSAS on the proper recording of Honoraria and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries and charge the payment of the honoraria to the proper sources of funds as provided in the DBM Circular. Also, for the Accounting Office to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments of additional compensation in the form of honorarium in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulation or Revised Withholding Tax Table. Considering that honoraria were charged against the R.A. 7171 Funds which is not among those enumerated to be utilized under R.A. No. 7171, the grant of honoraria to the BAC has no legal basis, hence we further reiterate our recommendation on the immediate refund of the amount of P482,773.50.
observations.managementAction: The accountable members refunded the amount of P482,773.50 which was adjusted in the books accordingly.
observations.reason: Copies of Official Receipt and JEV representing refund of the amount of P482,773.50 were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: Considering that the monitoring and evaluation undertaken by the PMC are part of their regular functions and considering further that the honoraria were charged against R.A. No. 7171 Fund which is not among those enumerated to be utilized under R.A. No. 7171, the grant of honoraria to the PMC has no legal basis, hence, we have recommended, and the PMC agreed to comply, that management stop the practice of granting Honoraria to PMC and for the immediate refund of the amount of P358,400.00.
observations.managementAction: The accountable members refunded the amount of P358,400.00 which was adjusted in the books accordingly.
observations.reason: Copies of Official Receipt and JEV representing refund of the amount of P482,773.50 were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management strictly observe existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations as provided under Section 4 (6) PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006. It is further recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the related DVs and supporting documents pertaining to the above transactions to enable us to make further verification on the legality, propriety and legality of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already submitted some of the lacking vouchers leaving a balance of P4,563,038.08 which will be submitted within the target implementation of the observation.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office submitted some of the lacking vouchers leaving a balance of P4,563,038.08 which will be submitted within the target implementation of the observation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, as the Internal Auditor and one who certifies the validity of transaction and completeness of supporting documents, as well as the Municipal Treasurer, who certifies the funds availability, to comply with the Fundamental Principles for Disbursement of Public Funds as provided under Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445. It is further recommended that deficiencies in the transactions be addressed with and submitted to the Audit Team for further verification of the legality, propriety and validity of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the related disbursements with approval of proper officials and completing the required supporting documentation amounting to P2,345,043.79. Further, management committed to submit as soon as possible the supporting documents needed to ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office submitted disbursements with approval of proper officials and completing the required supporting documentation amounting to P2,345,043.79. Further, the management committed to submit as soon as possible the supporting documents needed to ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) and to expedite the reconciliation of records with the Accounting Office as well as the records of the Municipal and Provincial Assessor for an accurate basis for the setting up and year-end balances of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the List of Taxpayers to support the setting up of receivables.
observations.reason: Submitted the List of Taxpayers to support the setting up of receivables. The RPT and SET Receivables were adjusted accordingly.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:
1. Direct all AOs to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served, otherwise, failure of the AOs to liquidate or when no liquidation of previous cash advances is received on or before January 20, the Accountant shall cause the withholding of the AO’s salary; and,
2. Instruct the Accountant to strictly comply with the above regulations particularly, “No additional cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made.”
observations.managementAction: Response letter was made dated May 30, 2017, received by COA on May 31, 2017 re status of the unliquidated cash advances. As to date, the balance decreased to P1,400.00. The management issued memorandum dated July 10, 2017 to refund such cash advance.
observations.reason: As per Report on the Monitoring of Cash Advances as of December 31, 2017, Prior Year’s Cash advances were fully liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommend that Management return the P70,000,000.00 investment in the Trust Fund account and hasten the implementation of programs/projects for which the fund was created in order that the needed services to the constituents may not be delayed or sacrificed in favor of earning interest in time deposits.
observations.managementAction: Management returned the P70,000,000.00 investment in the Trust Fund account.
observations.reason: A Certification from the DBP on the termination of investment in the time deposit amounting to P70,732,439.71 has been received by the Audit Team, however, only P30 million has been transferred to the RA7171 account, the rest has been retained under the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer properly maintain the Cashbooks and to record cash transactions therein in accordance with Sections 26 to 28 of Vol. II MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has started to properly maintain the Cashbooks.
observations.reason: As to date, the cashbooks are updated although the totals are still written in pencil and daily balances are not closed and ruled.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management adopt measures that would facilitate the submission of their accounts on time as prescribed in the foregoing Sections of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and P.D. 1445. We further recommend that additional staff with accounting background be assigned at the Accounting Office in order to address the delay on the submission of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The required reports and supporting documents were being submitted to Accounting on a monthly basis.
observations.reason: The Office of the Accountant submits on a monthly basis, however, delayed in the submission has been noted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the BAC Chairperson agreed that Management cause strict compliance to COA Circular 2009-1 on the timely submission of contract with all its supporting documents by the BAC Chairperson/Secretariat to the Auditor within five (5) days upon execution/issuance of the contracts.
observations.managementAction: Some POs are being submitted within the prescribed period. Contracts of successfully bidded projects are not being submitted on time.
observations.reason: Although POs are being submitted within the prescribed period, contracts of successfully bidded projects are not being submitted on time. Reiterated in Observation No. 13, Page 84 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management stop the practice of charging prior year’s expenses against the current year’s appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation be observed pursuant to the aforementioned laws and regulations. If the incurrence of expenses during the year-end cannot be dispensed with in the exigency of service, unpaid obligations shall be recorded as payables to validate their payments in the succeeding years. A memorandum for the closing of the books of accounts be issued to all departments to obligate money claims for the current year before the cut-off date. Likewise, officials who continue to authorize the payment of such expenditures are jointly and severally liable for any disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The total amount was adjusted in the books accordingly.
observations.reason: Copy of JEV was submitted to the Audit Team to effect the adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (a) monitor the SWMP and oversee its implementation; (b) comply with the requirements prescribed under R.A. No. 9003 with regard to waste segregation collection and construction of MRFs in all barangays. Otherwise, any government officials concerned who fail to comply with and enforce rules and regulations promulgated shall be charged administratively in accordance with R.A. No. 7160 and other existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Units for MRF were distributed to 22 barangays, 3 schools and 1 public market. As for the SWM Plan, revision was needed. On waste segregation collection, there was no increase in the number of barangays due to limited capacity of the controlled dumpsite and waste segregation is still manageable.
observations.reason: SWM Plan has been submitted on June 28, 2017. For the controlled dumpsite, documentations needed to acquire the property is on process. On waste segregation collection, there was no increase in the number of barangays due to limited capacity of the controlled dumpsite and waste segregation is still manageable.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the LDRRMO to regularly prepare a Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF and submit the same to the COA Auditor. Likewise, we recommend that Municipal Accountant comply with the reportorial requirements on the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the LDRRMF of the LGU, NDRRMF given to LGUs and Receipts from Other Sources.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office have been submitting monthly reports on the Utilization of the LDRRMF to the Audit Team but not on a monthly basis.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office have been submitting reports on the Utilization of LDRRMF to the Audit Team but not on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Municipal School Board, and management agreed to comply to limit the utilization of the SEF to the priority programs and projects defined under R.A. No. 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.
observations.managementAction: Strict compliance with the provisions of Section 272 of Republic Act (RA) 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG.
observations.reason: Disbursements for CY 2017 amounting to P229,350.00 were still charged against the SEF which are not in accordance with Section 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 01 series of 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to comply that they plan and calendar the implementation of the programs and projects under the 20% Development Fund and prioritize projects that shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure in accordance with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 so that the intended benefits could be enjoyed by the recipients.
observations.managementAction: Management made necessary actions to fast track the implementation of the projects within the target within the 2018 budget.
observations.reason: Still to be implemented in 2018 Budget.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management comply with the provisions of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, PCW-DILG JMC No. 2014-01 and COA Circular No. 2004-00 and that concerned municipal officials and employees fully implement programs/projects/activities funded under the GAD Plan and Budget to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents. Further, for the GFPS to ensure and monitor the effective implementation of the annual GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Management is fast tracking the implementation of the PPAs included in the Annual GAD Plan.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 8, page 76 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management immediately settle the tax liabilities amounting to P4,614,124.96 to avoid the accumulation of interest and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: The management requested the BIR Regional Office to pay the tax liabilities in a monthly basis and for the condonation of the accumulated interest. The LGU has paid P706,887.60 for CY 2017.
observations.reason: The LGU has settled P706,887.60 for CY 2017. As of Dec. 31, 2017, the balance of the tax liability as appearing in the FS is P3,907,237.36.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the heads for the Nagyubuyuban and Taboc BHS make representations with the Municipal Health Officer (MHO). The MHO, on the other hand, make consultative meetings with the heads of the BHS particularly on their need requirements, evaluate the same and consider them in the preparation of the Project Procurement Management Plan for the Municipal Health Office. We have recommended further that the MHO make representations with barangay officials to consider the need requirements of the BHS in the preparation of their annual budget.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to comply.
observations.reason: Issues/ concerns to be incorporated in the 2018 budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Procurement Officer to purchase its common-use supplies with the PS-DBM and resort to the use of the alternative modes of procurement only when the needed supplies are not available with the PS-DBM as supported by a certification by the said Office.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO had partially procured supplies from the PS-DBM.
observations.reason: Implementation of Supplemental Feeding Program for CY 2017 were done thru Negotiated Procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management of San Juan to require the Accountant to set-up and maintain a Special Accounts in the General Fund for its economic enterprises through the use of complete subsidiary ledgers and record for all transactions pertaining to its operation.
observations.managementAction: No Sangguniang Bayan ordinance has been passed.
observations.reason: Sangguniang Bayan ordinance has been passed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to discontinue the reimbursement basis as mode for paying procured items or services and that they would strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO partially discontinued the reimbursement basis as mode for paying procured items in the implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program. Implementation of Supplemental Feeding Program for CY 2017 were done thru Negotiated Procurement.
observations.reason: Implementation of Supplemental Feeding Program for CY 2017 were done thru Negotiated Procurement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoined the GSO and Municipal Accountant to consider the implementation of the foregoing remedial measures, so as to support the assertion made and ensure reliability of the PPE accounts balances and to fairly present the financial statements.

a) strictly observe the rules and regulations in the acquisition of real properties and ensure that all land are titled and registered in the name of the Municipality;

b) conduct a complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s PPE; and

c) maintain updated Property Cards (PCs) and SLs as a control records and regularly undertake reconciliation of the GSO and Accounting Unit records.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality submitted copy of tax declaration to support that lands are acquired were not registered in the name of the Municipality. Likewise, incorporate in the books and inventory report those land evidently owned by the Municipality.

The Municipality conduct a physical inventory of its properties.

Ledger Card (LC) is maintained for each item except those pertaining to beginning balances, of GSO which they are continuously exerting effort to ascertain the description of these balances.
observations.reason: Some of the lands which were newly acquired were not reported in the Report of Physical Count of PPE

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation expenses monthly and every month thereafter until such time that the asset is fully depreciated or derecognized pursuant to Item Nos. 64 and 69 of the PPSAS.

Accordingly, conduct a regular review of the valuation accounts for PPE, taking into consideration the updated useful lives of the assets as a consequence of major repair or rehabilitation and effect the necessary adjustment as appropriate.

Further, incorporate disclosure in the notes to financial statements those properties with no description/available records despite diligent efforts to locate the same.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant computes depreciation monthly and every month thereafter until such time that the asset is fully depreciated or derecognized pursuant to Item Nos. 64 and 69 of the PPSAS.

Accordingly, conduct a regular review of the valuation accounts for PPE, taking into consideration the updated useful lives of the assets as a consequence of major repair or rehabilitation and effect the necessary adjustment as appropriate.

The corresponding depreciation and accumulated depreciation of the said properties were prepared and JEVs were drawn to effect the adjustments in the book of accounts of the Agency for the month of January 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) instruct the members of the BAC to submit justification on the recommendation made to award procurement contract for the Supplementary Feeding Program to the bidder in spite failure to comply with the requirements of the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and

b) observe diligence of a good father of a family in the conduct of eligibility checking and post qualification of the LCB to ensure that it transacts business with bonafide and legitimate suppliers only.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has submitted the lacking documents except for the TCC and assured the Audit Team that they will diligently observe the requirements of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

The Municipality observed diligence of a good father of a family in the conduct of eligibility checking and post qualification of the LCB.
observations.reason: Lapses were still observed in the conduct of eligibility checking and post qualification of the LCB.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned LDRRMO to comply with the reporting guidelines on the preparation and regular submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds.

Likewise, incorporate in the notes to financial statements appropriate disclosure of the availability and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund and its corresponding Utilization.
observations.managementAction:
In compliance with the AOM recommendations, monthly reports on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF for CY 2016 have been prepared by the Municipal Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation.

Likewise, incorporate in the notes to financial statements for CY 2016 appropriate disclosure of the availability and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund and its corresponding Utilization have likewise been incorporated in the notes to financial statements for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Engineer to regularly reconcile their records regarding ongoing projects.

Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary JEV to effect the adjustments in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Voucher has been drawn and taken up in the book of accounts in May 2017.
observations.reason: Reconciliation between the records of the Accounting and Engineering Office is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a listing of all borrowers prior to year 2013 so subsidiary ledgers may be created/updated.

We also recommended sending of demand letters to enforce settlement of matured loans.
observations.managementAction:
The Management is enforcing the collection of unsettled loan balance from all of the recipients.

Sending of demand letters to borrowers still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) fully implement the Municipal DRRM programs and see to it the funds were utilized in accordance with the approved budget and plan.
observations.managementAction: The Management justified that funds for the comprehensive flood control program were not utilized because such was not sufficient to cover the cost of the same. Only fuel used for the backhoe being utilized in the rehabilitation of the temporary flood control system was charged against the fund to prevent the impact of the typhoon.
observations.reason: Budget constraints.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit all collections timely. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant record collections only for the current year as income and prior year’s collections as prior ear’s adjustments so that the financial statements reflect the actual income for the year.
observations.managementAction: Collectors were advised to remit their collections before cut-off time so that these can be recorded on the date of collection. Collections after cut-off will be taken up in the next working day.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to increase her fidelity bond to an amount sufficient to cover the maximum accountability for a given period in accordance with the revised Schedule of Bonds provided by the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The fidelity bond of the Municipal Treasurer was increased in accordance with her maximum cash accountability.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and submit together with the DVs and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and Check Disbursement Journal.
observations.managementAction: Report of Checks Issued was prepared by the Municipal Treasurer and forwarded to the Accounting Section together with the paid vouchers.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Risk Reduction and Management Officer, to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly to COA for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF was prepared monthly.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from charging expenditures which do not support risk reduction activities and strictly follow the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-03 dated April 17, 2012 on utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
observations.managementAction: Regular operating expenses were charged to the Office of the LDRRMO.

observations.recommendation: Direct the concerned officials to comply the required documents for evaluation and prove validity of the transactions. Henceforth, strictly observe the rules and regulations governing the proper disbursements of the public funds.
observations.managementAction: The Management has submitted the Memorandum of Agreement and other documents to support the claim for honoraria.

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC secretariat for the prompt submission of copies of perfected contracts and supporting documents for timely auditorial review and technical evaluation.

observations.recommendation: Initiate concrete steps to comply with the minimum considerations for a dumpsite as provided under Section 39 of R.A. 9003. We further recommend that Management request the members of the Sangguniang Bayan to allocate funds for the establishment of a sanitary landfill in compliance with Section 40 and 41 of the same Republic Act.
observations.managementAction: The area which serves as the temporary dump site of the municipality was subject for closure as advised by DENR because it does not pass the minimum requirement set forth in RA 9003. The Municipality, as its initial step, allocated funds in the CY 2017 budget for the establishment of Material Recovery Facility (MRF) and Residual Containment Area.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still preparing the updated Bank Reconciliation Statements.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the provision of Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of the accounts. Otherwise, impose the administrative disciplinary action under Section 122 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant exerted effort to submit the accounts on time but due to limited manpower and voluminous workload submission was still delayed.

Support with verifiable evidence proving the efforts exerted in collecting the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to undertake the reconciliation of both books.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to impose upon the barangay thru a Memorandum of Agreement to submit an Inspection Report duly signed by the Municipal Engineer with the Statement of Work Accomplished by the contractor/implementing agency/barangay.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remind the local school board to strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of LGC, Article 363 of its Implementing Rules and Regulations and Item 4 of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 of the DECS, DBM and DILG, in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Mayor require the BAC to submit a copy of the duly perfected government contracts/purchase orders, irrespective of amount, and all supporting documents thereto, to the audit team within five (5) working days from issuance thereof. The Purchase Order must be legible in all respects and should contain all the necessary data and information pursuant to Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer with the help of the Municipal Assessor should create strategies to intensify collection of RPT taxes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all local officials concerned to comply with the requirements of RA 9184, its implementing rules and all laws, decrees, circulars, memoranda and issuances governing infrastructure projects of local governments. Specifically, we recommended that: a) All procurement should be meticulously and judiciously planned by the procuring entity. b) A certification that the detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted according to the prescribed agency standards and specifications in conformance with the provisions of this Annex should be attached in the contract and that the detailed engineering outputs are adequate for the procurement at hand. c) The infrastructure contracts be reviewed and revised to include the provision of process of arbitration in case of disagreements between parties so that subsequent contracts which will be entered into by the municipal government would comply with the relevant requirements of RA 9184 IRR and related laws.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly impose on the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer the preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Solid Waste Management Board to adhere strictly to RA 9003 or the Philippine Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and the environment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) Require the officials concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 111 of PD 1445, and Section 121 in order to adequately furnish the accurate information of the PPE of the LGU. a) Require the General Services Officer to conduct a thorough physical inventory taking on all properties and consolidate the same per type or category of properties in accordance with classification or category reflected in the financial statements; b) RPCPPE should include all properties of the LGU such as land, road networks, buildings, markets, structures, etc.; c) Require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to exert more effort to reconcile the difference between balances per books with the Physical count as of December 31, 2016 in order to reflect an accurate balance of the PPE account. d) A separate report on PPEs which are unserviceable and awaiting disposal should be made; Management should adopt existing rules and regulations in the valuation of PPEs in order to avoid disparity in assigning values to properties and arrive at the same amount.

We recommended that perfected contracts together with the documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation shall be furnished to the Auditor of the agency concerned within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the prompt implementation of BUB Programs/Projects/Activities within the timeframe set by the Source Agencies to address the poverty reduction objective of the program.

To ensure the submission of required documents, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Disbursing Officer to require the submission of DTR, Approved Application for Leave, Certificate of Appearance and Authority to Travel, before the payment of salaries. Likewise, for payment of Hazard Duty Pay, concerned personnel should submit the required documents mentioned in Section 5.8 of Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, viz:  List of positions in its plantilla with the corresponding job description that describes the basis for justifying such positions that are exposed to high risk or low risk hazard in accordance of IRR of RA No. 7305.  Certification by the Secretary of the Department of Science and Technology (DOST)/Department of Health (DOH)/Department of National Defense (DND)/Director of the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology that the place of assignment/travel is a strife-torn/embattled/disease-infested/distresses or isolated areas/stations, or areas declared under the state of calamity or emergency, or with volcanic activity and or/eruption  Duly accomplished time record of employees or travel report  Copy of special order from the agency/department head covering the assignment to hazardous/difficult areas  Approved DTR/Service Report

We recommended that the LGU officials ensure that the budgetary allocations and actual expenditures for Personal Services are within the 55% limitation. We further recommended that the local treasury and local government council should strive to find ways to improve collection efficiency of revenues from local sources so that the Personal Services ceiling will correspondingly increase.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) Provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to implement waste segregation at source; (b) Strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign on the no-littering policy among the residents; (c) Consider embarking on effective projects and programs to support the LGU ecological solid waste management program; and (d) Strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.

We recommended that planned programs, projects and activities should be implemented in order to attain the accomplishment of desirable socio-economic development and environment outcomes. Likewise, we recommended that the Annual Investment Plan should be carefully and judiciously planned with the view of attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We enjoined the Local officials to effect the implementation of the programs, projects and activities identified and programmed to be used for disaster risk reduction and management activities and programmed activities in the amount of P2,157,087.35 of the CY 2016 unexpended balance for risk reduction and management activities. Further, we recommended for the utilization of the unexpended balance for risk reduction and management activities.

We recommended that a meticulous, careful and judicious planning of procurement activities be done by the BAC before the Approved Budget of the Contract will be determined so as to arrive at a more reasonable price of goods. We further recommended that in the selection of suppliers/contractors, the BAC should be guided by a reasonable eligibility criteria to come up with eligible and qualified suppliers for an effective and efficient procurement system.
We recommended that the utilization of proceeds of the Special Education Fund be limited to the prioritized projects, programs and activities mandated in Sections 272 and 100 (c) of RA 7160.

We recommended that Local School Board officials strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and advise the school administrators in their respective locality to revise their School Improvement Plan to include the expenses enumerated under DepEd Order No. 12, s. 2012.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate and the Municipal Treasurer trace back the source of the discrepancy of the Cash in Bank account balances starting from 2010 records and prior, in addition to the nature of the bank debit memo in December 17, 2015 and record all book reconciling items to bring the balances into agreement so that the correct balance of cash is presented in the financial statements should likewise be traced. Henceforth, the Accounting Office should cause the reconciliation between the books of accounts and the records maintained by the Treasury as well as the bank records at the close of each month pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445 to ensure that all transactions affecting Cash and its related accounts are timely, accurately and completely recorded and reported in the financial statements to make it a reliable basis for decision making of the agency. The reconciling items should be taken up immediately upon identification. We also have recommended that the proposed adjustments be recorded to correct the above discussed erroneous entries.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following:
 the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer trace back the
source of the discrepancies of the Cash in Bank account balances starting
from 2010 records and prior and record all book reconciling items to
bring the balances into agreement so that the correct balance of cash is
presented in the financial statements;
 the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly reconciliation statements
between the bank, accounting records and cashbooks on all bank
accounts of the LGU to avoid occurrence of additional discrepancy.
Verified reconciling items should be taken up immediately; and
 the proposed adjustments be recorded to correct the cash book balance of
cash in bank accounts.

1.4 In view of the above observation, we have recommended that the Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer analyze the transactions in 2010 to determine the
cause/s of the difference and make the necessary adjustments. Henceforth,
the Municipal Accountant is reminded that reciprocal accounts should
always have corresponding entries and equal amounts.

4.4 In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following:
 that the Municipal accountant prepare monthly reconciliation statement
between the accounting records and cashbooks on cash – local treasury
account; and
 that the Municipal Treasurer record the proposed adjustments to correct
the balance cash – local treasury account.

5.11 In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that:
 The Municipal Government create an Inventory Committee to conduct
physical count of PPE in the Municipality. To facilitate the inventory
taking, all department heads are advised to instruct their members to
cooperate in the conduct of physical count;
 the Inventory Committee prepare the Physical Inventory Reports to be
submitted to the Property Officer for verification and reconciliation
with the records maintained by the Accounting Office and the property
cards of the Property Unit;
 the Municipal Government conduct a proper disposal of unserviceable
property; and
 henceforth, the Accounting Office prepare and maintain PPELC as
required under regulations to facilitate reconciliation with the Property
Office.

13.2 In view of the above observations, we have recommended that the members
of the BAC submit justification/explanation on the noted violations.
Henceforth, ensure that prospective bidders submit complete eligibility
requirements before they are marked as “passed” and declared as
LCB/HRB.

We recommended the following:
That the City Treasury Department:
Intensify efforts in order to increase the collection efficiency rate on revenues arising from business taxes by launching vigorous tax information campaign;
Determine/verify the amount of gross sales/receipts declared by the taxpayers by conducting regular examination of the books of accounts of all taxpayers; and
Recompute the annual tax levied on the various business establishments according to the schedule of rates provided in the Revenue Code of the City and accordingly make/cause tax credits on those over assessed or collection of under assessed taxes, as the case maybe;
b.	That the City Accounting Department:
Prepare and maintain detailed subsidiary ledgers for all accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units to facilitate proper recording and reporting of  financial transactions; and
Use the proper classification in the recording of collections based on the source documents and as reported by the City Treasury Department to avoid misclassification of accounts;
That the Business Permits and Licenses Office:
Require the taxpayers to submit/attach the pertinent documents to support their declaration in the application forms for business permits; and
Revisit the formula used in computing business taxes in the BPLTAS  in accordance with the provisions of the San Juan Revenue Code.

We recommended the following:
That the City Accounting Department:
Account for the rent income from various leased properties which were recorded in other income classification as well as those customers’ deposits and advance rentals misclassified as rent income and make the necessary adjusting entries;
Check or refer to the source documents before recording the transactions to prevent misclassification of accounts; and
Cause the immediate submission of all rental contracts and strictly comply with the provisions of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 to avoid suspension of payment of salaries of the officials concerned;
That the City Treasury Department:
Use the proper classification of the collections based on the source documents in reporting the same; and
Strictly comply with the conditions stipulated in the lease contracts to lessen over/under charging of advance payments.

We recommended that the Information Technology Office conduct the required seminars for the users and update the data in the GSMS to fully achieve the purpose for which the system was acquired and to determine at the earliest possible time the problems that may be encountered by the City in using the system or which part requires improvement, prior to the expiration of the contract, hence, the City may not incur additional costs for its improvement or enhancement.

We recommended that Management direct:
The Public Assistance Center to:
Strictly follow the set policies and guidelines in the granting of the medical/financial and burial assistance to prevent excessive grants and to facilitate proper/equal distribution of the funds to indigent constituents of the City;
Device a mechanism to monitor the distribution of the said benefit; and
Establish/maintain a database of all those who have already availed of the various financial assistance, containing all the existing information indicated in the submitted lists and to include the name of hospital where those patients were confined; and
That the City Budget Office cause the integration of the various sources of funds for the said financial aids in order to have a consolidated medical assistance program and to avoid duplication of benefits to the needy.

We recommended that Management cause/instruct:
The City Budget Office/City Treasury Department to see to it that estimated revenues are reasonably projected as collectible for the ensuing year so that projects are appropriately planned;
The City Engineering Department to ensure that the projects are implemented fully as planned and within the timeframe; and
The City Planning and Development Office to come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve the socio-economic condition of the locality.

We recommended that Management cause or direct:
The GAD Focal Point System to:
develop and implement more GAD related programs/activities that advocate women empowerment, promote gender equality and improve the welfare of women that will protect and fulfill their rights;
institute effective measures to monitor and report on the implementation of GAD programs/activities/projects to ensure that the different concerns of women and men are addressed equally and equitably in their PAPs in accordance with the above provisions of PCW/DILG/ DBM/NEDA; and
adhere to the PCW, NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 in the Preparation of Annual GAD Plan and Budget, and Accomplishment Report; and to COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA Audit Team;
The City Planning and Development Office, in coordination with the GAD Focal Point System to establish a GAD database with complete information  to ensure the generation, processing, review, and updating of sex-disaggregated data that will serve as basis in performance-based and gender-responsive planning and budgeting; and
The City Treasury Department to ensure that estimated revenues are reasonably projected as collectible for the ensuing year so that PAPs are appropriately planned.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Thoroughly review the contract prior to its approval, and consider amending the same to include relevant matters for the protection  of the City; and
Submit immediately, the documents supporting the contracts to facilitate review of the same by the COA Technical Audit Specialists.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that Management comply with COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the publicity and other forms of announcement to the public pertaining to the projects/programs/activities of the City for transparency, accountability and public monitoring.

We recommended that the City Accounting Department:
Immediately remit the full amount of taxes withheld and make the persons responsible for withholding to personally answer for penalty and surcharges that may be imposed by the BIR for late remittance of the amount withheld; and
Recheck the accuracy of the total amount of withheld taxes for each month before making the payments thru EFPS to avoid the under or over remittances of the same.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendations:
That the Local Chief Executive:
compel the persons liable for the restitution or settlement of outstanding liabilities, and to see to it that current issuances are settled within the period prescribed by law; and
consider the provision of RRSA on the solidary liability/obligation of the persons determined to be liable;
That  the City Accounting Department to maintain subsidiary ledgers/records for the concerned officials/employees for the disallowed amount and regularly update the same when payments are made; and
That the Human Resource Management Office cause the immediate withholding of salaries or other compensation due those persons liable who are currently in the government service of San Juan City, through the City Treasurer, in satisfaction of their disallowance pursuant to COA regulations.

For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we have recommended that the Management:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current  year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.
Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards,  AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit copies thereof to the Auditor for review and verification of the accuracy of the Cash in Bank accounts.

We have recommended that the OIC - Municipal Treasurer and the Revenue Officers follow the provisions of the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs in managing the collections to safeguard the same from possible loss/misuse/misappropriations of government funds.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to COA the accounts of the municipality within the required time frame to facilitate the timely evaluation of the same.

We have recommended that the officials and employees  concerned  in the processing of payment for terminal leave benefits, more specifically the approving authorities, revisit the guidelines before allowing payment for any claims. In light of the foregoing observation, it is worth emphasizing that the payment of terminal leave is based on the highest monthly salary received at any time during the employee’s period of employment in the government service and not on her latest salary, unless the latter is the highest received by the employee. Since payment has already made to the former Vice Mayor, we recommend that she refund the amount she received in compliance with the abovementioned provision.
We have recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor countersign all checks drawn for the payment of expenses of the Legislative Department pursuant to the provision of Section 40 of the NGAS Manual.

We have recommended that the Management require the drivers to accomplish the Trip ticket properly. Furthermore, that the Municipal Accountant see to it that Driver’s Trip Tickets attached to the disbursement vouchers are properly filled up.
We have recommended that the Management assess and collect the correct amount of business tax as provided in the Revised Municipal Revenue Code to increase the income of the municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of an Accomplishment Report for the disbursement of gasoline expenses in the road maintenance and regravelling of various barangays to monitor whether the desired output was achieved and also serve as control whether government funds were properly disbursed.

We have recommended that the management fully utilize the 20% Development Fund for priority programs/projects and activities for the  benefit of the constituents.  Further, any unexpended balance should be re-appropriated as part of the current year’s development fund.
