We have recommended that the management settle/appeal disallowance within six (6) months from receipt of the notice. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/disallowed in post-audit.

For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we have recommended that management conduct the required physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pinpoint responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs. Moreover, to maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them. For control and monitoring, make use of ICS for semi-expendable items to avoid loss, as these are usually small items.

We have recommended that management enjoin the Inventory Committee to include in the conduct of inventory of the properties of the municipality the infrastructures and other community facilities and for the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the required Report to the DILG. We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant complete the Registry of Public Infrastructure and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We have recommended that Management:
Submit justification why public bidding was not resorted to for the procurement totaling P2,226,162.00.
Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR. Conduct public bidding for purchases of goods and the use of alternative modes of procurement be in accordance with the IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
Observe the thresholds in the procurement of goods. Resort to public bidding when the procurement exceed the threshold amount.
4.  Submit to the Audit Team the deficiencies noted in the basic documentary requirements to all purchases under alternative modes of procurement to enable us to make decisions in audit.

We have recommended and Management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) observe and strictly comply with the above-mentioned provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ascertain the legality of the bidding activities and validity of transactions.

We have reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that they comply strictly with the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2007-3 on the payment of BAC Honoraria. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of the PPSAS on the proper recording of Honoraria and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries and charge the payment of the honoraria to the proper sources of funds as provided in the DBM Circular. Also,  for the Accounting Office to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments of additional compensation in the form of honorarium in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulation or Revised Withholding Tax Table. Considering that honoraria were charged against the R.A. 7171 Funds which is not among those enumerated to be utilized under R.A. No. 7171, the grant of honoraria to the BAC has no legal basis, hence we further reiterate our recommendation on the immediate refund of the amount of P482,773.50

Considering that the monitoring and evaluation undertaken by the PMC are part of their regular functions and considering further that the honoraria were charged against R.A. No. 7171 Fund which is not among those enumerated to be utilized under R.A. No. 7171, the grant of honoraria to the PMC has no legal basis, hence, we have recommended, and the PMC agreed to comply, that management stop the practice of granting Honoraria to PMC and for the immediate refund of the amount of P358,400.00.

We have recommended that management strictly observe existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations as provided under Section 4 (6) PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006. It is further recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the related DVs and supporting documents pertaining to the above transactions to enable us to make further verification on the legality, propriety and legality of the transactions.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, as the Internal Auditor and one who certifies the validity of transaction and completeness of supporting documents, as well as the Municipal Treasurer, who certifies the funds availability,  to comply with the Fundamental Principles for Disbursement of Public Funds as provided under Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445. It is further recommended that deficiencies in the transactions be addressed with and submitted to the Audit Team for further verification of the legality, propriety and validity of the transactions.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) and to expedite the reconciliation of records with the Accounting Office as well as the records of the Municipal and Provincial Assessor for an accurate basis for the setting up and year-end balances of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts.

We have recommended that management:
Direct all AOs to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served, otherwise, failure of the AOs to liquidate or when no liquidation of previous cash advances is received on or before January 20, the Accountant shall cause the withholding of the AO’s salary; and,
2. Instruct the Accountant to strictly comply with the above regulations particularly, “No additional cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made.”

We have recommend that Management return the P70,000,000.00 investment in the Trust Fund account and hasten the implementation of programs/projects for which the fund was created in order that the needed services to the constituents may not be delayed or sacrificed in favor of earning interest in time deposits.

We have recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer properly maintain the Cashbooks and to record cash transactions therein in accordance with Sections 26 to 28 of Vol. II MNGAS for LGUs .

We have recommended that management adopt measures that would facilitate the submission of their accounts on time as prescribed in the foregoing Sections of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and P.D. 1445. We further recommend that additional staff with accounting background be assigned at the Accounting Office in order to address the delay on the submission of accounts.

We have recommended and the BAC Chairperson agreed that Management cause strict compliance to COA Circular 2009-1 on the timely submission of contract with all its supporting documents by the BAC Chairperson/Secretariat to the Auditor within five (5) days upon execution/issuance of the contracts.

We have recommended that Management stop the practice of charging prior year’s expenses against the current year’s appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation be observed pursuant to the aforementioned laws and regulations. If the incurrence of expenses during the year-end cannot be dispensed with in the exigency of service, unpaid obligations shall be recorded as payables to validate their payments in the succeeding years. A memorandum for the closing of the books of accounts be issued to all departments to obligate money claims for the current year before the cut-off date. Likewise, officials who continue to authorize the payment of such expenditures are jointly and severally liable for any disallowances.

We have recommended that the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (a) monitor the SWMP and oversee its implementation; (b)comply with the requirements prescribed under R.A. No. 9003 with regard to waste segregation collection and construction of MRFs in all barangays. Otherwise, any government officials concerned who fail to comply with and enforce rules and regulations promulgated shall be charged administratively in accordance with R.A. No. 7160 and other existing laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended that Management require the LDRRMO to regularly prepare a Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF and submit the same to the COA Auditor. Likewise, we recommend that Municipal Accountant comply with the reportorial requirements on the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the LDRRMF of the LGU, NDRRMF given to LGUs and Receipts from Other Sources.

We have recommended to the Municipal School Board, and management agreed to comply to limit the utilization of the SEF to the priority programs and projects defined under R.A. No. 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.

We have recommended and Management agreed to comply that they plan and calendar the implementation of the programs and projects under the 20% Development Fund and prioritize projects that shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure in accordance with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 so that the intended benefits could be enjoyed by the recipients.

We  have recommended that Management comply with the provisions of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, PCW-DILG JMC No. 2014-01 and COA Circular No. 2004-00 and that concerned municipal officials and employees fully implement programs/projects/activities funded under the GAD Plan and Budget to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents. Further, for the GFPS to ensure and monitor the effective implementation of the annual GAD PPAs.
We have recommended that Management immediately settle the aforesaid tax liabilities to avoid the accumulation of interest and surcharges.
We have recommended Management to enforce the immediate settlement of the balance of the unsettled audit disallowances in order to protect the interest of the government.

We have recommended that the heads for the Nagyubuyuban and Taboc BHS make representations with the Municipal Health Officer (MHO). The MHO, on the other hand, make consultative meetings with the heads of the BHS particularly on their need requirements, evaluate the same and consider them in the preparation of the Project Procurement Management Plan for the Municipal Health Office. We have recommended further that the MHO make representations with barangay officials to consider the need requirements of the BHS in the preparation of their annual budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  enjoined  the  GSO  and
     Municipal Accountant to consider the implementation of  the  foregoing
     remedial measures, so as to support  the  assertion  made  and  ensure
     reliability of the PPE accounts balances and  to  fairly  present  the
     financial statements.

       a) strictly observe the rules and regulations in the acquisition of
          real  properties  and  ensure  that  all  land  are  titled  and
          registered  in  the  name   of   the   Municipality.   Likewise,
          incorporate  in  the  books  and  inventory  report  those  land
          evidently owned by the Municipality;


       b) conduct a complete physical inventory of all the  Municipality’s
          properties; and


       c) maintain updated Property Cards  (PCs)  and  SLs  as  a  control
          records and regularly undertake reconciliation of  the  GSO  and
          Accounting Unit records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct  the  Municipal
     Accountant to compute the  depreciation  expenses  monthly  and  every
     month thereafter until such time that the asset is  fully  depreciated
     or derecognized pursuant  to  Item  Nos.  64  and  69  of  the  PPSAS.
     Accordingly, conduct a regular review of the  valuation  accounts  for
     PPE, taking into consideration the updated useful lives of the  assets
     as a consequence of major repair  or  rehabilitation  and  effect  the
     necessary adjustment as appropriate. . Further, incorporate disclosure
     in  the  notes  to  financial  statements  those  properties  with  no
     description/available records despite diligent efforts to  locate  the
     same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

       a. instruct the members of the BAC to submit justification  on  the
          recommendation  made  to  award  procurement  contract  for  the
          Supplementary Feeding Program  to the bidder in spite failure to
          comply with the requirements of the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and


       b. observe diligence of a good father of a family in the conduct of
          eligibility checking and post qualification of the LCB to ensure
          that  it  transacts  business  with  bonafide   and   legitimate
          suppliers only.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  concerned
     LDRRMO to comply with the reporting guidelines on the preparation  and
     regular submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and
     Utilization of Funds prepared by the Municipal Accountant, for  proper
     and  timely  evaluation.   Likewise,  incorporate  in  the  notes   to
     financial statements appropriate disclosure of such for additional and
     relevant information of financial statement users.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  Municipal
     Accountant and Engineer to regularly reconcile their records regarding
     ongoing projects. Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant  to  make
     the necessary JEV to effect the adjustments in order to fairly present
     the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor revise immediately the Inventory
Committee and require them to conduct the physical count, after which prepare the
Report on Physical Count of PPE as basis in the reconciliation with the accounting
records.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant update and submit on time the
monthly bank reconciliation statements as provided under Section 3.4 of COA
Circular No. 96-011.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to see to it
that projects are implemented within the target date of completion to ensure that
constituents are provided with the infrastructure facilities at the earliest time.
Henceforth, we recommend that liquidated damages be imposed on the payment of
delayed completion of the project.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
remit all collections timely. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant
record collections only for the current year as income and prior year’s c collections
as prior year’s adjustments so that the financial statements reflect the actual income
for the year.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer increase her fidelity bond to an
amount sufficient to cover the maximum accountability for a given period in
accordance with the Revised Schedule of Bonds provided by the Bureau of
Treasury.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Report of Checks Issued
(RCI) and submit it together with the DVs and supporting documents to the
Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and
Check Disbursement Journal.

We recommend that Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal
Risk Reduction and Management Officer, submit the Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF monthly to COA for monitoring purposes.

We recommend that Management refrain from charging expenditures which do not
support risk reduction activities and strictly follow the provisions of DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-03 dated April 17, 2012 on utilization of Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.

We recommend that the concerned officials comply the required documents for
evaluation and prove validity of the transactions. Henceforth, strictly observe the
rules and regulations governing the proper disbursements of the public funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

e) Require the officials concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section
111 of PD 1445, and Section 121 in order to adequately furnish the accurate
information of the PPE of the LGU;

f) Require the General Services Officer to conduct a thorough physical inventory
taking on all properties and consolidate the same per type or category of
properties in accordance with classification or category reflected in the
financial statements and that the RPCPPE should include all properties of the
LGU such as land, road networks, buildings, markets, structures, etc.;

g) Require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to exert
more effort to reconcile the difference between balances per books with the
Physical count as of December 31, 2016 in order to reflect an accurate balance
of the PPE account.

h) Require the GSO to prepare a separate report on PPEs which are
unserviceable and awaiting disposal.

Management should adopt existing rules and regulations in the valuation of
PPEs in order to avoid disparity in assigning values to properties and arrive at the
same amount.

We recommended that perfected contracts together with the documents
forming part thereof by reference or incorporation shall be furnished to the Auditor of
the agency concerned within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the prompt
implementation of BUB Programs/Projects/Activities within the timeframe set by the
Source Agencies to address the poverty reduction objective of the program.

To ensure the submission of required documents, we recommended that the
Local Chief Executive direct the Disbursing Officer to require the submission of
DTR, Approved Application for Leave, Certificate of Appearance and Authority to
Travel, before the payment of salaries.

Likewise, for payment of Hazard Duty Pay, concerned personnel should
submit the required documents mentioned in Section 5.8 of Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012, viz:
 list of positions in its plantilla with the corresponding job description that
describes the basis for justifying such positions that are exposed to high
risk or low risk hazard in accordance of IRR of RA No. 7305.
 Certification by the Secretary of the Department of Science and
Technology (DOST)/Department of Health (DOH)/Department of
National Defense (DND)/Director of the Philippine Institute of
Volcanology and Seismology that the place of assignment/travel is a
strife-torn/embattled/disease-infested/distresses or isolated areas/stations,
or areas declared under the state of calamity or emergency, or with
volcanic activity and or/eruption
 Duly accomplished time record of employees or travel report
 Copy of special order from the agency/department head covering the
assignment to hazardous/difficult areas
 Approved DTR/Service Report

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

(e) Provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish
their own MRFs and to implement waste segregation at source;
(f) Strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign on the no-
littering policy among the residents;
(g) Consider embarking on effective projects and programs to support the
LGU ecological solid waste management program; and
(h) Strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines
under RA No. 9003.

We recommended that planned programs, projects and activities should be
implemented in order to attain the accomplishment of desirable socio-economic
development and environment outcomes.

Likewise, we recommended that Annual Investment Plan be carefully and
judiciously planned with the view of attaining desirable socio-economic development
and environmental outcomes.

We enjoined the local officials to effect the implementation of the programs,
projects and activities identified and programmed during the year out of LDRRM
Fund to ensure readiness of the LGU incase of calamity.

Further, we recommended for the utilization of the unexpended balance for
risk reduction and management activities.

We recommended that a meticulous, careful and judicious planning of
procurement activities be done by the BAC before the Approved Budget of the
Contract will be determined so as to arrive at a more reasonable price of goods.
We further recommended that in the selection of suppliers/contractors, the
BAC should be guided by a reasonable eligibility criteria to come up with eligible
and qualified suppliers for an effective and efficient procurement system.

We recommended that the utilization of the proceeds of the Special Education
Fund be limited to the prioritized projects, programs and activities mandated in
Sections 272 and 100 (c) of RA 7160.

We recommended that Local School Board officials strictly observe the
provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and advise the school administrators in their
respective locality to revise their School Improvement Plan to include the expenses
enumerated under DepEd Order No. 12, s. 2012.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the financial statements with due diligence and in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards on the conversion of the financial statements in accordance to the PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. The Municipal Accountant was directed to undertake reconciliation of the Final Budget the Actual. For CY 2016, Supplemental Budgets for the year 2015 and make necessary explanatory notes on the difference between Final Budget and Actual Amounts which is required in the conversion of the financial statements in accordance to the PPSAS.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
 The Municipal Accountant should record the proposed adjusting entries
 Upon completion of the projects in the following year, the Municipal Accountant should close the CIP and Due to Other NGAs accounts and transfer the CIP accounts to the General Fund and recognize the Income from Grants and Donations or Prior Year’s Adjustments. The CIP Account will be closed to the appropriate asset account or Public Infrastructure after which the Public Infrastructure will be closed to Government Equity.
 The Municipal Accountant should follow the proper accounting of fund transfers from National Government Agencies and Other LGUs and be extra careful in the recording of transactions to avoid misstatements in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. Proposed adjusting entries were recorded and adjustments were taken up in the books of the LGU in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
 that the Municipal accountant prepare monthly reconciliation statement between the accounting records and cashbooks on cash – local treasury account; and
 that the Municipal Treasurer record the proposed adjustments to correct the balance cash – local treasury account.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The Municipal Treasurer was directed to maintain ledgers. The action plan are to issue demand letter and Tax campaign.

Management admitted that no physical count was conducted as of December 31, 2016. Nonetheless, they agreed to conduct annual physical inventory at year-end and prepare the required reports on properties.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct:
 the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
 the Municipal Accountant to trace the recorded road, highways and bridges which were closed to government equity prior to 2016 and subsequently record in its books of accounts the amount of road works equivalent to the required transition provision, keep and maintain Registry of Public Infrastructures for Road and subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
 the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed on the recommendations of the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to formulate strategies to intensify and facilitate tax collections. If need be, avail of remedies embodied in Section 174 of RA 7160 to ensure collection of taxes due the municipal government in order to increase the delivery of basic services. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Certified List of All Property Tax Delinquencies to include all tax delinquents and reconcile the same with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that the receivables set up at the beginning of the year was directed by the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) and not the LGU’s assessment. The Municipal Treasurer was directed to maintain Taxpayers’ Index Card to keep track of the yearly collection and support balances of the account in the Financial Statements. The LGU plans to issue demand letters and tax campaign.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor instructs:
a. the Municipal Treasurer to limit her cash advances to those authorized under COA Circular No. 97-002 and to observe the general principles of the imprest system that payments must be made by check for amounts exceeding ₱15,000.00; and
b. henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should submit within the prescribed days the liquidation and supporting documents to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the observations and ensured prospective compliance.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the following observations, we have recommended the following:
1) That the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to see to it that Disbursement Vouchers for government contracts are duly and properly supported before payments are made;
2) Guidelines to process financial assistance should be set and observed to regulate the program. Absence of required documentations should be an automatic basis for disqualification. For this purpose, the following minimum requirements should be institutionalized, viz;
a) Medical Certificate (for medical assistance)
b) Death Certificate (for burial assistance)
c) School ID, enrollment form, certificate of registration (for educational assistance)
d) MSWDO case study with complete details.
e) Receipt of financial assistance duly acknowledged and dated.
3) The amount set for each type of financial assistance should also be complied with to ensure a non-discretionary determination of amounts given to each client;
4) Payments for financial assistance should be made by check in the names of the recipients; and
5) The Municipal Accountant should ensure that all disbursements are with complete supporting documents and submitted within the reglementary period.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, management justified that the lapses in the documents for the distribution of financial assistance were simply errors committed and will no longer be repeated for the succeeding transactions.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Technical Working Group of the Bids and Awards Committee familiarize themselves and observe the updated guidelines in the preparation of the ABC to ensure that the correct upper limit or ceiling for acceptable bid prices are properly set for every project of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the observations for prospective compliance.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that all perfected contracts and purchase orders be submitted to COA within five days after perfection thereof for the immediate review of the reasonableness of the cost, conduct of inspection and other necessary audit procedures.
observations.managementAction: Management assured future compliance.
The Bids and Awards Committee was instructed and directed to comply with the implementation of RA 9184 and attend trainings/updates on the Government Procurement Law.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above observations, we have recommended that the members of the BAC submit justification/explanation on the noted violations. Henceforth, ensure that prospective bidders submit complete eligibility requirements before they are marked as “passed” and declared as LCB/HRB.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee was instructed and directed to comply with the implementation of RA 9184 and attend trainings/updates on the Government Procurement Law.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:
 justify/explain the reasons for exceeding the procedural limits provided under R.A. No. 9184;
 adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement and see to it that the procurement program allows sufficient lead time for such public bidding; and
 resort to alternative methods of procurement only in highly exceptional cases provided for in Rule XVI to promote economy and efficiency.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee was instructed and directed to comply with the implementation of RA 9184 and attend trainings/updates on the Government Procurement Law.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we reiterated our recommendation that:
 Management determine the cost of the unperformed portion of the works for every day of delay and compute the amount of liquidated damages in accordance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. 9184 and the contract agreement; and
 that the Municipal Mayor enforce immediate collection of liquidated damages and ensure the refund of the excess of the bill of quantities from the contract price from the contractors.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the observations and the Municipal Engineer was instructed to compute the liquidated damages to collect from the contractors.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above audit observations, have recommended the following:
 that a special book of account and depository account be maintained solely for the share from Tobacco Excise Tax (RA 7171); and
 that management maintain a separate Registry of Allotment and Obligations and subsidiary ledger for the fund from RA 7171 for proper control.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to the audit recommendations.

The LGU thru the Budget Officer have been directed to undertake necessary revisions and corrections to comply with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 as to the correct utilization and disbursement of the 20% Development Fund. The KALAHI counterpart provide funds in the regular budget for the administrative expenses.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator specify the KALAHI projects to be funded from the 20% Development Fund in the Annual Investment Plan which must be in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Circular 2011-1 and the Municipal Budget Officer to provide funds in the regular budget of the respective implementing offices for expenses that are administrative in nature. Henceforth, refrain from charging expenses for wages, travelling expenses, supplies and the like under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management informed the audit team during the exit conference that for CY 2017, the budget for KALAHI was excluded from the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality maintain a special account and complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the 20% development fund in compliance with the aforementioned law to facilitate monitoring of development projects. Operations of the fund should be accounted for through the maintenance of books of accounts/complete subsidiary ledgers to enable the generation of financial statements at any given time. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer should regularly transfer the 20% development fund to facilitate monitoring of its utilization and ensure that the fund is not used for unauthorized programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the audit recommendations.
Management maintained that the PPAs were GAD activities.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to prepare an investment plan for the LDRRMF of the Municipality for the current year and the unutilized balance from the previous years, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002, to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the accomplishments and disbursement of the fund; the Municipal Accountant to maintain the depository account for LDRRMF in the Trust Fund books; the Municipal Treasurer to limit fund transfers up to the budgeted amount.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO has been preparing Investment Plan of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant already maintained the depository account for LDRRMF in the Trust Fund Books. The Municipal Treasurer was directed to limit fund transfer up to the budgeted amount.

Reiterated under Finding No. A.2 | The City Inventory Committee composed of GSD, Accounting and other department personnel resumed inventory taking from November 2016 to February 2017.  Re-organization was made last Nov. 4, 2016 to comply with the present need with regards to PPE. The City Accounting Department is continuously coordinating with General Services Department for the reconciliation of PPE.
observations.reason:
The variances between the CAD books and GSD RPCPPE decreased remarkably; however, the GSD merely copied the properties and balances in the CAD Lapsing Schedule, instead of indicating in the RPCPPE the actual properties seen and noted during the inventory taking; and
PPE were recorded in the books on a per transaction basis or per Disbursement Voucher, instead of by classification, specification and nature of PPE.

observations.managementAction: Reiterated under Finding No.A.1 | Management regularly sends out delinquency notices through mails and personal delivery;and taps support of all Barangays in information dissemination.
observations.reason: The deficiencies noted can be corrected only upon the effectivity of the General Revision of Fair Market Values.

observations.managementAction: Hon. Mayor Guia Gomez, et.al refunded the amount of P337,025 as evidenced by OR No. 1029124 dated May 10, 2017 representing overpayment of expenses incurred in the travel to the United States of America conducted by various City officials.
observations.reason: All CAs still outstanding as of December 2015 have been liquidated the following year except for previous years included in the beginning balance.

observations.recommendation: Implement the projects as planned and re-assess whether the remaining projects not yet implemented are really needed by the constituents of the City of San Juan.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated under Finding No. B.17 | Management has implemented one project in CY 2016
observations.reason:
Proper planning was not observed by Management.

The other planned projects were not implemented due to insufficiency of funds, unavailability of location and the projects were already implemented by national agencies.

observations.managementAction:
Reiterated under Finding No. A.7 | MMDA stated in its letter dated November 23, 2016 that for the purpose of uniformity in computing the LGU’s 5 percent statutory contribution per LGU, they used the figures submitted in the SRE for CY 2015 to the BLGF as basis in computing IRA share to MMDA.

The City will remit the share of DPWH-DILG upon obtaining their account.

The City has transferred the share of the Office of the Building Official to its Trust Fund account from CY 2015 to September 2016.
observations.reason: The balance of the account Due to NGAs still includes the amount of P856,132 which remained inactive for a period ranging from two to seven years

observations.recommendation:
stop charging the payment of special allowances granted to public school teachers and principals from the Special Education Fund; and
reimburse the amount of P5,205,397 from General Fund to Special Education Fund.

(Note: There was an Errata made for the updated amount to be reimbursed)
observations.managementAction:
The City had stopped charging the payment of special allowances to public school teachers and principals from SEF. 

The amount of P4,335,956 was reimbursed from GF to SEF under check #215233 dated 9/13/2016, leaving a difference of 
P869,441 which will be included in the supplemental budget for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
Comply with the rules and regulations pertaining to GAD and to:

complete the setting-up of the GAD database consisting of sex-disaggregated data and other information related to GAD to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; 
meticulously identify the PPAs that would effectively address gender issues before inclusion in the GPB; and
strictly monitor the implementation of GAD PAPs to be able to provide the basic services necessary for the constituents especially women and children.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated under Finding No. B.18 | Setting-up of GAD database is still on-going
observations.reason:
The GAD database has yet to be completed since results of survey for two Barangays are not encoded yet.

GAD PAPs amounting to P36,200,218 were not implemented during CY 2016 due to unavailability of cash for the hard projects and unavailability of venues for the trainings/ seminars.

observations.recommendation:
revisit the LDRRMP and ensure that the Plan includes the goals, objectives, expected outputs and performance indicators and measures consistent with the NDRRMP as well as the specific timelines for each activity and Plan’s period of coverage; 
ensure that performance indicators and measures are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant and time-bound to facilitate assessment of the progress toward the achievement of the outcomes, objectives and goals; and
ensure that the approved LDRRMP as well as the LDRRMFIP are submitted to the OCD for review and evaluation.

strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 on the preparation of LDRRMFIP for efficient implementation of programs, projects and activities funded from the LDRRMF. The LDRRMFIP should be prepared in the prescribed form and should be duly signed by the Chief, CDRRMO, approved by the City Mayor and the Sangguniang Panlungsod, and submitted to the OCD for review and evaluation.  Likewise, the list of programs, projects and activities for the unexpended LDRRMF shall be included in the LDRRMFIP solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities.

prepare the prescribed LDRRMFIP;
evaluate carefully the goods for procurement for DRRM purposes with consideration to their necessity, shelf life, storage requirements and the reasonableness of the quantities to be procured for stockpiling to ensure that such goods are utilized for the purpose for which the same were procured; and 
identify goods for procurement and for the responsible officer/personnel to sign the relevant documents to establish accountability and responsibility for the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated under Finding No. B.15 | Some of the planned projects were either implemented or partially implemented
observations.reason:
Management prepared Annual Investment Plan, instead of using the prescribed format of the annual LDRRMFIP.

PPAs were not implemented due to lack of better planning scheme.

observations.managementAction: Starting 2016 all contracts are being forwarded to the Office of the City Accountant to be signed as witness to the contract.
observations.reason: Management submitted copies of suspension and resumption orders on July 13, 2016. Evaluation made by COA TAS showed that there are no liquidated damages incurred.

observations.managementAction:
The City and PUP-main campus have made amendments to Memorandum of Agreement to include all courses in the scholarship and to include miscellaneous accounts in the tuition.

Starting January 2016, collected rent income are recorded at gross amount.
Reiterated under Finding No. B.19 | Management had submitted the requirements in recommend-ation letter a.
observations.reason: There are still several transactions which are not supported with supporting documents like plans, drawings, and pre-repair and post-repair inspection reports.

observations.recommendation: Cause the immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers together with the necessary support documents and for the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the required supporting documents of the contracts to facilitate the conduct of auditorial and technical review as required by COA regulations.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers are supported by BAC documents and Management is complying with the proper submission of Purchase Orders
observations.reason: Some contracts for bidded projects are still not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: Exert all measures to compel the persons liable for the restitution or settlement of outstanding liabilities, and to see to it that current issuances are settled within the period prescribed by law.
observations.managementAction:
Reiterated under Finding No. D.22 | Management had submitted the required documents in compliance with the Notices of Suspension amounting to P39,232,217, hence the same were accordingly lifted.

Demand letters were sent by Management to those who are still in active service to submit their proposals for the withholding of their salaries in settlement of the disallowances to prevent outright deduction of the full amount of disallowance from their compensation.
observations.reason: No action was made on the other persons liable despite issuance of COA Order of Execution.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned adhere to the provisions of Section 124, Volume I of the Manual     on the New Government Accounting System for local government units and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 to determine the validity, accuracy, and reliability of the property, plant and equipment owned by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The committee was not able to conduct the physical inventory count due to lack of time.
observations.reason: There was no annual inventory report for the calendar year 2016 submitted as to date. Reiterated in Observation No.1, page 32 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer follow the provisions of Section 32 of the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs in managing her collections to safeguard the same from possible loss/misuse/misappropriations of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Current year has been strictly done. But for prior years, Management are verifying if there were unrecorded deposits. Unrecorded due to non-submission of deposit slip in previous years.
observations.reason: Validated and agreed. Reiterated in Observation No. 3 page 34 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management continue their diligence in causing the settlement of long outstanding unliquidated cash advances from persons responsible and liable in conformity with the pertinent provisions set forth under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Current cash advances were already been settled.
observations.reason: Previous cash advances were not yet reconciled and therefore the balance still exists.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer see to it that RHU personnel should only receive the  benefits they are entitled and refund the excess subsistence allowance received. Likewise, he should also check that duly accomplished DTRs are  present and attached to the payroll not only for RHU personnel but all the municipal employees.
observations.managementAction: Correct amount of subsistence and laundry allowance were paid.
observations.reason: Validated and agreed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that prior year’s expenditures should not be charged to current year’s appropriations.
observations.managementAction: No prior expenditure was charged to current year’s appropriations.
observations.reason: Validated and agreed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Officials and employees accomplish properly all the official documents attached to the disbursement voucher and transactions should not be paid if the supporting documents are not complete and properly accomplished.
observations.managementAction: Management try to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: During post audit of the transactions there were still supporting documents that were not properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality submit their revenue and disbursement documents on time.
observations.managementAction: Submitted revenue and disbursement documents late.
observations.reason: The municipality was still late in the submission of revenue and disbursement documents. Reiterated in Observation No. 4 page 34 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects and activities for the benefit of the constituents.  Any unexpended balance should be re-appropriated as part of the current year’s development fund as provided in Item 11.3 of the DILG Memorandum Circular No.96-263 dated December 9, 1996.
observations.managementAction: Management did not fully implement the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 10 page 40 of the report

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the GAD Focal Person should see to it that the fund should be fully utilized during the year to attain the objectives embodied in the plan for that particular year.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan was fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that disallowances be settled/appealed within six (6) months from receipt of the notice. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/disallowed in post-audit.
observations.managementAction: Notices of disallowance were under appeal.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 12 page 42 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed that the Inventory Team submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE and for the GSO/Property Custodian to maintain complete records by updating their property ledger cards and stock card/property cards, respectively and reconcile with the Physical Inventory Report not later than January 31 of each year to avoid accumulation of unreconciled balance of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Team is still in process of accomplishing the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: The Inventory Team is yet to perform an inventory of all properties of the municipalities and to submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 59 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reflect in the RCD the gross amount and the discounts of RPT Collections and not net of discounts to enable the Municipal Accountant to account for discounts granted on prompt payment of real property taxes using the above accounts as provided for by the PPSAS so that the correct balances of income, assets and liabilities will be reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer reflected in the RCD the gross amount and the discount of the RPT Collections and the Municipal Accountant already made the necessary adjusting entries in the respective General Journals.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, as the Internal Auditor and one who certifies the validity of transaction and completeness of supporting documents, as well as the Municipal Treasurer, who certifies the funds availability,  to comply with the Fundamental Principles for Disbursement of Public Funds as provided under Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445. It is further recommended that deficiencies in the transactions be addressed with and submitted to the Audit Team for further verification of the legality, propriety and validity of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Disbursements were released only after completion of signature by proper officials.
observations.reason:
Some disbursements in 2016 still do no bear approval of proper officials. Deficiencies noted in the transactions for 2015 were not addressed with and submitted to the Audit Team. Reiterated in Observation No. 8, page 75 of the report.
AOM No. 2016-009 was also issued for noted missing vouchers. (Observation No. 7, page 73 of the report).

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to Management  and they agreed to require the BAC to comply strictly with the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2007-3 on the payment of BAC Honoraria. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of the PPSAS on the proper recording of Honoraria and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries and charge the payment of the honoraria to the proper sources of funds as provided in the DBM Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have made the necessary adjusting entries
observations.reason: The BAC still charged its honorarium against RA 7171 Funds. Reiterated in Observation No. 5, page 68 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Inventory Committee to include in the conduct of inventory of the properties of the municipality the infrastructures and other community facilities.  The Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the required Report to the DILG. The Municipal Accountant to complete the Registry of Public Infrastructure and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The Inventory Team is still in the process of taking inventory of all the properties of the municipalities. Reiterated in Observation No. 2, page 61 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Management to cause strict compliance to COA Circular 2009-1 on the timely submission of contract with all its supporting documents by the BAC Chairperson/Secretariat to the Auditor within five (5) days upon execution/issuance of the contracts.
observations.reason: POs are being submitted on a monthly basis. Reiterated in Observation No. 14, page 82 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to comply with the provisions  of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, PCW-DILG JMC No. 2014-01 and COA Circular No. 2004-001 and institutionalize the creation of a GFPS who will take a lead in mainstreaming gender in agency PAPs, coordinate the preparation of the agency GPB and the GAD AR, monitor its implementation and report on its results.
observations.managementAction: The Management has complied with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order No. 49-2016 dated June 3, 2016 creating the GFPS.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to cause the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to create a Special Trust Fund account solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities in the Trust Fund books in compliance with COA Circular 2012-02. We recommend further that the Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfers of unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan issued Resolution No. 24-2016 dated May 16, 2016, a resolution interposing no objection to the request to open 2 new accounts with the Land Bank of the Philippines for the 20% Development Fund and Calamity Fund.
observations.reason: The LDRRMF account with CA No. 0202-0258-54 was opened and the amount of deposits included CY appropriations. The unexpended balance was transferred to the Special Trust Fund Account in the Trust Fund books.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management to enforce the immediate settlement of the balance of the unsettled audit disallowances in order to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: As of June 30, 2016, a letter containing the notice of disallowamce was sent to Hon. Aquino.
observations.reason: No further action was taken since it was reported that Hon. Alfonso Roberto A. Aquino died on August 17, 2016. Reiterated in Observation No. 22, page 97 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Inventory Team conduct physical inventory of its PPE and for the GSO/Property Custodian to maintain complete records by updating their property ledger cards and stock card/property cards respectively and reconcile with the Physical Inventory Report not later than January 31 of each year to avoid accumulation of unreconciled balance of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Physical Inventory was conducted in May to June 2015
observations.reason: There was no reconciliation of records since the physical inventory report was not yet prepared. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 59 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended management to:
Cause the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an assessment roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties;
Cause the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list of taxpayers and the corresponding amount of tax due and collectible as basis for setting up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and
Cause the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the RPT/SET Receivables account with the Municipal Treasurer’s list of RPT delinquent accounts. Make necessary adjustments, if any, to present fairly the financial statements.
observations.reason: No records submitted by the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer. Reiterated in Observation No. 9, page 76 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:
Direct all AOs to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose for which these were given had been served, otherwise, failure of the AOs to liquidate or when no liquidation of previous cash advances was received on or before January 20, the Accountant shall cause the withholding of the AO’s salary; and,
Instruct the Accountant to strictly comply with the above regulations particularly, “No additional cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made.”
Instruct the Accountant to record the grant of cash advances pertaining to travel, special purpose activities, time-bounded under advances to Officers and Employees account and prepare the necessary adjusting entry for the reclassification of the outstanding balance of account 123 to account 148.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P411,435 was liquidated in the CY 2016.
observations.reason: A balance of P25,570 remains unliquidated as per Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances as of December 31, 2016. Reiterated in Observation No. 10, page 77.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended management to direct the GAD Focal Person and the Budget Officer to comply with the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 regarding allocation of fund for GAD Plan and Budget and ensure that all activities undertaken are gender responsive. We recommend further that the GAD Focal Person in coordination with the MPD Officer to mainstream gender perspectives in the major programs and projects of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The municipality appropriated amount based on the JC 2012-01 through attribution in every department for their specific GAD program, projects and activities (PPAs). Further, the Municipal Mayor issued EO No. 49-2016 creating the GFPS.
observations.reason: Out of the total CY 2016 budget of the Municipality of San Juan amounting to P110,753,738.26, 7.55% or P8,361,306.50 were appropriated for GAD policies, programs and activities.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the immediate submission of the said mandatory reports and/or accounts and that additional staff with accounting background be assigned in the Accounting Office. Likewise, management adopt measures that would facilitate the submission of their accounts on time as prescribed in the foregoing Section of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The mandatory reports were submitted immediately and additional staff with accounting background has been assigned in the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Although the accounts are being submitted monthly and within the prescribed period, verification through post-audit showed some disbursements are not included in the file and being submitted in succeeding months. Reiterated in Observation No. 13, page 81 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Procurement Officer to purchase its common-use supplies with the PS-DBM and resort to the use of the alternative modes of procurement only when the needed supplies are not available with the PS-DBM as supported by a certification by the said Office.
observations.managementAction: Supplies were partially procured from the PS-DBM
observations.reason: Office supplies amounting to P15,054.35 only were purchased from the PS-DBM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to comply strictly with the provisions of Section 62.2.3.3 of R.A. No. 9184 on the posting of warranty security in all its procurements for its protection in case of defects that would occur within the warranty period and to save in the costs of repairs.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2015 and succeeding years, the Management withheld the ten percent (10%) retention fee on infrastructures only.
observations.reason: The management has continued to withheld 10% retention fee on infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management to comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by requiring the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer to submit regularly the Report on the Utilization of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Reports are submitted upon request.
observations.reason: No records were turned over to the appointed LDRRM Officer. Further, the Accounting Department is the one currently submitting the said report. Reiterated in Observation No. 17, page 86 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended strict compliance of the provisions of DILG MC No. 2004-78 and discontinue the grant of honoraria to the members of the Project Monitoring Committee, unless there is a specific authority for its payment.
observations.reason: The PMC has continued granting honoraria charged to R.A. No. 7171 fund. Reiterated in Observation No. 6, page 71 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management of San Juan to require the Accountant to set-up and maintain a Special Accounts in the General Fund for its economic enterprises through the use of complete subsidiary ledgers and record for all transactions pertaining to its operation.
observations.reason: No Sangguniang Bayan ordinance has been passed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to discontinue the reimbursement basis as mode for paying procured items or services and that they would strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO had partially discontinued the reimbursement basis as mode for paying procured items in the implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program.
observations.reason: Some disbursements made during the first quarter of CY 2015 were still on reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation:

Municipal Accountant and
the Property Custodian to:

prioritize the
reconciliation of the

count with the property
and accounting records,
and adjust accordingly any
difference found to arrive
at the reliable balance of
PPE accounts; and

ensure that it is properly

existing rules and
regulation; and PPE

existing serviceable and
unserviceable properties.

mentioned during the

discrepancies and the

committed to
immediately reconcile
the affected PPE

observations.reason:
Reconciliation initially started upon receipt of the
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM).

the Municipal Accountant
to:

coordinate with the

reconcile the
discrepancies of the Due

accounts balances; and

prepare the necessary

The Municipal
Accountant after
continuously

between reciprocal

and Due to LGUs. These
were attributed to the

Per Provincial Books
Due from LGU, San Juan
General Fund:

Adjusting entries have been drawn and
