observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require
the concerned officials
to:

come up with a detailed
Annual Invested Plan (AIP)
indicating the priority
programs, projects and
activities (PPAs) in its
Barangays through
consultation and
deliberation with the
concerned Barngays
officials;

incorporate in the AIP the
complete description of
the project, exact
location, intended
beneficiaries and
estimated cost of each
project; and

monitor the implementation
and ensure the optimal
utilization of the funds
for the intended
development programs and
projects.
observations.managementAction:
The unexpended balances
of projects under
Maintenance and Other
Operating Expenses
(MOOE) were transferred
to other priority
projects which are
included in the approved
Annual Investment Plan
(AIP). On the other
hand, Capital Outlay
projects which are bid out
in 2015 were implemented
and paid during the
year.
observations.reason: Implementation of programs and optimal utilization of funds pertaining to the 20% Development Program has not yet been realized as at year-end due to some budget constraints and other factors. This is reiterated under Part II of this report under Observation No. 6.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the
Municipal Mayor direct the
GAD Focal Point System
(GFPS), through the TWG
to:

properly identify gender
issues, require only the
inclusion of attainable
gender sensitive and
responsive programs,
projects and activities
and indicate well defined
key performance indicator
in the agency’s GAD Plan
and Budget (GBP) to
facilitate sound
evaluation and
assessment.

We also recommended that
the LGU GFPS assess the
status of the LGU’s
institutional mechanism on
gender mainstreaming
annually; prepare the GAD
Accomplishment Report
(GAD AR) rigidly, incorporate
therein concrete and
comprehensive
output/outcome in the
actual result column which
is comparable to a
specified performance
indicator and target; and
make sure to present the
approved budget for each
GAD activity by specific
object of expenditure to
come up with a reasonable
budget estimate and for
maximum utilization of
fund; and regularly
monitor the implementation
of GAD PPAs and its
related budget to ensure
that they are expended for
the purpose it has been
intended, assess and
account the result in the
GAD AR for proper
monitoring, evaluation
and action.
observations.managementAction:
GAD Plan and Budget was
prepared as prescribed
in the Joint Circular
with all the required
information and
submitted to DILG for
review and approval,
however, the Management
admitted failure to
utilize the budget fully
due to some hindrances
during the
implementation stage.

GAD Accomplishment
Report was prepared
well, however, some
difficulties has not been
avoided.
observations.reason: Some gender issues identified has not been fully implemented as the concerned offices failed to meet the required documents for its implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Welfare and
Development Officer to:

review the Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA)/Trust
Agreement prior to the
implementation of the SFP
and comply with the
guiding principles
provided for its
implementation;

immediately implement the
CY 2015 SFP for the
remaining balance to
address and attain the set
objectives of the program
without unreasonable
delay; and

indicate the items’ complete
specifications and
appropriate delivery date in
the Purchase Order (PO)
and Inspection and
Acceptance Report (IAR).
Make sure that items
listed in the PO and IAR
match with its
corresponding allocation
and distribution list; and
submit authority or basis
for the discrepancy
between the total number
of beneficiaries fed as
presented in the physical
and financial reports
against the proposed
target and non-compliance
with the age requirement
bracket provided under AO
No. 08, series of 2012.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Social
Welfare and Development
Office (MSWDO) after
learning the results of
Audit Observation
Memorandum (AOM)
re-evaluated the MOA
pertaining to the SFP
and ensured continuous
compliance and
adherence thereto.

The MSWDO and the Day
Care Workers immediately
implement the remaining
balance of the CY 2015
feeding program. Though,
the SFP started late (due
to “bidding” procurement
procedures), they were
able to finish the 120 days
supplementary feeding.

The items’ complete
specifications and
appropriate delivery date in
the PO and IAR were
ensured to match with its
corresponding allocation
and distribution list.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Management exercise
prudence in the management
of funds for the advantage
of the government. If
warranted, evaluate the
agency’s financial
accounts and liquidity
status in order to preclude
the incurrence of
unnecessary expenses such
as interest payments,
bearing in mind good
stewardship of public
funds.
observations.managementAction:
Management promised to
fully comply with the
requirements mandated
for all their present and
future transactions. It was
found out that the funds
placed under time
deposit accounts were
not idle, however, SB
Resolution No. 148-A-2016
was issued immediately in
order to close such
accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor direct the
responsible officer to
ensure the timely and
regular submission of
perfected Purchase
Orders/Job Order/
Contracts and all
supporting attachments
within the required
period.
observations.managementAction:
Management ensures
consistent and timely
submission of their POs/
Contracts despite
distance to fully comply
with COA Circular Nos.
96-010 and 2009-001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
General Services Officer
meticulously and judiciously
plan procurement for each
quarter and incorporate
them in the Annual
Procurement Plan (APP)
in order to adopt
competitive/public bidding
to provide assurance that
the lowest possible price
is obtained from such
procurement.

Ensure that only those
that are emergency in
nature and those ordinary
or regular supplies and
materials not available in
the Procurement Services
and within the threshold
be purchased through
shopping.
observations.managementAction:
The Management agreed
with the recommendation
and prepared the APP for
CYs 2016 and 2015 in
accordance with the
provisions of RA No.
9184. However, they
opted to use the
Alternative Mode of
Procurement guided by
Rule XVI Section 48.1 of
the RIRR of RA No.
9184 which reads “In all
instances, the procuring
entity shall ensure that
the most advantageous
price for the Government
is obtained” and that they
have no intention
whatsoever to evade or
circumvent the
requirements of law.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Management submit to the
Auditor justification for
the purchase of such
vehicles without authority
from DILG.

We also recommend that the
Management comply with
the provisions of RA 9184
and other applicable
guidelines in government
procurement. Avoid
specification for brand
names to allow
competitiveness in the
procurement process.
observations.managementAction:
Management admitted that
they are not aware of
Administrative Order
(AO) No. 233, Series of
2008 as amended by AO
No.15 dated May 25,
2011 requiring the LGU to
obtain and submit
Authority to Purchase
from the Secretary of the
DILG prior to purchase
vehicles. Had they been
informed, they assured
compliance thereto.
observations.reason: Management justified that they were unaware of the said Administrative Order. Nonetheless, such was disallowed in audit. Notices of Disallowance were issued relative thereto, in which, COA Region IV A decision has been issued upholding the disallowance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Social Welfare
and Development Office
(MSWDO) conduct a
comprehensive evaluation
of the program by
analyzing the trend of
borrowings to determine
the level of its
performance in terms of
uplifting the borrowers’
economical condition and
entrepreneurial
development. Verify
whether the proposed
business were actually
commenced and still
operational;

Municipal Treasurer
furnish the Municipal
Accountant a listing of
all borrowers prior to
year 2013 so subsidiary
ledgers may be
created/updated. We also
recommend sending of
demand letters to enforce
settlement of matured
loans; and

Management, based on the
results of analysis and
evaluation of the MSWDO,
review the design,
objective and purpose of
the SEA program as well
as its policies and
procedures of
implementation. Define the
true rationale of the
program, whether it be
entrepreneurial or plainly
aid in nature. Make
revision of procedures or
instigate controls, as
accordingly, recommended
by MSWDO, particularly in
the screening  of the
borrowers and monitoring
of the progress of their
small scale ventures to
ensure not only the
recovery of the loans but
also the commercial
advancement of the
borrowers. Otherwise,
scrap the SEA program
and formulate anew that
would be better fit the
needs of the constituents.
observations.managementAction:
Since the issuance of
AOM pertinent hereof,
the Municipality/MSWDO
has decided to stop the
release of cash assistance
to all SEA applicant.
Likewise, they are
enforcing the collection
of unsettled loan balance
from all of our recipients.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management
Council (MDRRMC) fully
implement the Municipal
DRRM programs and see to
it that the funds were
utilized in accordance
with the approved budget
and plan.
observations.managementAction:
Management justified that
funds for the
comprehensive flood
control program were
not utilized because such
was not sufficient to
cover the cost of the
same. Only fuel used for
the backhoe being
utilized in the
rehabilitation of the
temporary flood control
system was charged
against the fund to
prevent the impact of the
typhoon.
observations.reason: Due to budget constraints, the Municipality was able to request assistance from Local and National Government Agencies, prompting them to defer the implementation of the comprehensive flood control program and utilization of the allotted LDRRMF for the said project.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that GAD
Focal Point System (GFPS),
through the TWG:

prepare the GPB as
prescribed in the Joint
Circular with all the
required information and
submit to DILG for review
and approval;

require all departments/
offices to include in the
GAD Plan and Budget all
priority programs, projects
and activities (PPAs) that
would address identified
gender issues, evaluate the
objectives performance
indicators and targets and
ensure that these are well-
formulated to facilitate
monitoring and evaluation;

prepare the consolidated
GAD Accomplishment Report
(AR) based from the DILG
approved GPB following the
prescribed format for more
accurate assessment; and

ensure that all PPAs
specified in the GPB are
adequately funded and
fully implemented by
planning and strategizing
each GAD activity to take
full advantage of the funds
appropriated. We also
recommended that GFPS-
TWG regularly monitor the
GAD Budget to ensure that
they are spent for what is
intended and report them
in the GAD
Accomplishment Report to
facilitate proper
evaluation.
observations.managementAction:
GAD Plan and Budget
(GPB) for CY 2015 was
prepared as prescribed
with all the required
information and was
submitted to the DILG for
review and approval.

Other Departments
submitted their GAD
activities and were
included in CY 2015 GAD
Plan and Budget.

Consolidated GAD
Accomplishment Report
(AR) for CY 2015 was
prepared following the
prescribed format.

Management ensures that
those programs, projects
and activities incorporated
in their CY 2015 GPB were
adequately funded so as
to facilitate implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the
following:

the Property Custodian to
submit the Inventory and
Inspection Report for
Unserviceable Properties
(IIRUP) to the Accounting
Office to facilitate
reclassification of these
properties to Other Assets
account; and

the Municipal Accounting
Office to comply with
Section 4(p) of the New
Government Accounting
System (NGAS) Manual by
preparing the necessary
Journal Entry Voucher to
reclassify those
unserviceable properties
to Other Assets account
based on the IIRUPs
submitted by Property
Custodian so that only
those that are serviceable
and in use by the
Municipal Government
would be depreciated and
properly included in the
PPE account balance; and

The Property Custodian to
immediately dispose the
unserviceable properties
pursuant to Section 79 of
PD 1445 and other existing
rules and regulations
under Sections 170-180 of
RA 7160 in order not to
expose those properties to
elements that cause
further deterioration or
loss of salvage value.
observations.managementAction:
Report of unserviceable
was submitted to the
Accounting Office but was
not classified to Other
Assets awaiting proper
disposal.

The Accountant prepared
the necessary Journal
Entry Voucher to
reclassify those
unserviceable properties
to Other Assets.
observations.reason: The General Service Office is in the final stage of consolidating the reports on unserviceable properties currently deposited at Motorpool. A letter request for inspection of said properties will soon be furnished to COA to request for appraisal of the properties and eventually dispose the same.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the
Municipal Mayor and other
local officials consider its
personal services
expenditures within the
limitation set forth in the
Local Government Code.

Moreover, it is
recommended that the
municipal officials and
employees refund the PEI
paid to them in excess of
the PS limitation.
observations.managementAction:
Management complied
with the limitation set
forth in the Local
Government Code in the
disbursement for
personal services.
Further, the Municipal
officials and employees
have fully settled the ND
on PEI for CYs 2012 and
2013 amounting to P
5,062,200.00.
observations.reason: Notice of Disallowance pertaining to the grant of PEI was fully settled in December, 2016, reference NSSDC No. 17-001dated March 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: Consider transferring its combo deposit with the First Consolidated Bank (FCB) to a compliant GFI after the lapse of one year transition period in conformity with existing laws and regulations in accordance with DOF Department Circular No. 001-005.
observations.managementAction: The account was already closed and transferred to DBP

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant observe strictly COA Circular No. 2012-009 in the proper accounting for LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were already made by the Municipal Accountant .
observations.reason: JEV was drawn to take up the adjustment

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the provision of Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of the accounts. Otherwise, impose the administrative disciplinary action under Section 122 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant exerted effort to submit the municipal accounts on time
observations.reason: Submission of accounts were still delayed.

observations.recommendation:
Direct all officials and employees with outstanding cash to immediately liquidate the same.
Stop granting additional cash advance if the previous ones have not been settled/liquidated pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. 1445.
Observe strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and prescribed Travel Law to preclude disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: Reminder were made to concerned officials and employees
observations.reason: There still cash advances ranging from 1 to 10 years

observations.recommendation: Assign personnel whose function is solely to reconcile the records of the Accounting and Treasury to present the correct PPE account balances. Require the GSO to list unserviceable items in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Report and undertake its final disposal to prevent further deterioration
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation in the books will be made when the reports of the inventory committee will be finalized.
observations.reason: There was no physical inventory count conducted during the year.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to update and submit on time the monthly bank reconciliation statements as provided under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still updating the BRS preparation
observations.reason: Preparation of BRS was still delayed.

observations.recommendation:
Initiate concrete steps to comply with the minimum considerations for a dumpsite as provided under Section 39 of R.A. 9003.
Further, request the members of the Sangguniang Bayan to allocate funds for the establishment of a sanitary landfill in compliance with Section 40 and 41 of the same Republic Act.
observations.managementAction: The established dumpsite was closed due to no compliance of ECC of DENR. Recovery Facility was constructed for waste processing.
observations.reason: The Sangguniang Bayan allocated funds for Residual Containment Material Area (Temporary Disposal Facility) for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC secretariat for the prompt submission of copies of perfected contracts and supporting documents for timely auditorial review and technical evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer requested the BAC Secretariat to submit contracts with their supporting documents within the prescribed timeframe
observations.reason: Submission of contracts with supporting documents are still delayed.

observations.recommendation: Direct all those who received the PEI to refund the amount of ₱10,000.00 each, the payment of which is not legally due them, the PS limitation provided under Section 325 (a) of RA 7160, having been not observed by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Monthly salary deduction in the payroll were made until fully refunded.
observations.reason: Partial refunds were made through salary deduction in the payroll.

observations.recommendation: Direct all liable officials and employees to immediately settle in full the amount disallowed to enable the Municipality to utilize the amount for its priority development projects.
observations.managementAction: Repayments were made through payroll deduction at minimal amount because some of the employees cannot afford to refund the disallowed in full.
observations.reason: Monthly repayments were made through payroll deduction.

observations.recommendation:
Require the concerned official to adhere strictly to the provision of Section 325 (a) of the Local Government Code of 1991.
Immediate refund of the above-mentioned expenditure is hereby enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Refunds were made monthly through payroll deduction with outstanding balance of P22,900.00 at the end
observations.reason: Repayments were made by installment through monthly payroll deduction

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the transfer of the properties in the name of the municipality by coordinating with the Municipal Assessor, DENR and other related agencies for the needed documents.
observations.managementAction: The concerned municipal officials are still complying the required documents for the titling of lots owned by the municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still complying the required documents for the titling of lots owned by the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
Exhaust all remedies including legal means in collecting of the accounts. Should this procedure turn futile, request for authority from the Commission on Audit for the possible write off of those accounts where the Municipality has no valid claim or collection thereof is totally impossible.
Support with verifiable evidence proving the efforts exerted in collecting the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent collection/Letters to persons responsible.
observations.reason: Partial settlement of ₱5,000.00 was collected during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accounts of implementing the provisions of Section 105 of MNGAS to maintain separate books of accounts of the three economic activities of the LGU. The net income of these activities should accrue back to the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 5 CY 2014

observations.recommendation: Instruct the accountant that she should be guided with the procedures contained in Section 105-111 of Chapter 6, Manual of the New Government Accounting system for LGUs regarding maintenance of special accounts in the General Fund. Require the accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for special accounts in the GF for the LGUs, thereby have readily adequate information as to the utilization of the 20% EDF as well as the corresponding object of expenditures and if these in line with the guidelines set forth for the 20% EDF.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 7 CY 2014 Newly hired accounting staff will still be trained to handle special accounts on its full implementation.

observations.recommendation: To avoid misuse of government funds and prevent losses, it is recommended the necessary safeguards should be observed by management, One way as required is to settle immediately by means of refund of the unused cash advance.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 1 CY 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer to effect the necessary adjustments to conform with the DBM and Philhealth issuances.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 3 CY 2015 .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the officials authorized to receive monthly RATA to comply with the provisions of the Local Budget Circular No. 103 which took effect on January 1, 2013 and cause the Municipal Accountant and the official who is in-charge in the preparation of the payroll for RATA to attach in the payroll a certified list of officers who are collecting RATA indicating the number of days they have rendered actual service, and information as to whether there is leave of absence incurred and what kind of leave, if there is any. For those officials who adopt a work schedule other than the usual eight hours per day, forty hours per week, the Municipal Accountant shall likewise attach a certified list of officers indicating the percentage of number of sessions attended in relation to the total number of sessions conducted in a month.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 4 CY 2015 .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Human Resource Officer to include in contracts for job order workers statements of duties and responsibilities or its equivalent term to serve as standard with which to base the accomplishment.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 7 CY 2015 .

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a. Seminars/trainings where participants come from the province should be conducted within the province in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other relative projects. b. Send participants only to seminars with their area of concern. c. Make representation with the Officers of the PCL to conform with the regulations on seminars/trainings as mandated by COA and DILG. Documentary requirements such as Activity Design, Post Activity Report and other necessary documents (e.g. approval/authority from concerned government office for the conduct of seminar (DILG) should be attached once the participant will attend the said seminar/training.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 8 CY 2015 .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Health Officer to certify on the purchase request whenever medicines are procured that said procurement is compliant with the PNDF as provided in EO 49. Adherence to the related provisions cited in DOH AO No. 163 series of 2002 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 is also recommended to ensure that items procured originated from legitimate sources and that they are effective as well as genuine.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 10 CY 2015 .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to Section 313 of the Local Government Code, and Section 111 of PD 1445. She should likewise maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for special accounts in the GF for the LGUs for the 20% EDF in order to have a readily adequate and reliable information as to the expenditures of these projects as well as the financial status, and if these were in line with the guidelines set forth under the 20% EDF implementation.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 11 CY 2015 .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer with the help of the Municipal Assessor should create strategies to intensify collection of RPT taxes.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 12 CY 2015 .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Solid Waste Management Board to adhere strictly to RA 9003 or the Philippine Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and the environment.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 15 CY 2015 .
